collection: C.V. Starr East Asian Library — Chinese rubbings
note: Metadata via the public OAI-PMH endpoint for staging/selection. Images referenced (image / record_url), not copied. Check each record's rights before import.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou. This album also includes two handwritten colophons dated 1659 and 1780, the latter added by Weng Fanggang 翁方綱 (1733–1818), suggesting a late Ming date for this rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen yin xun
entries.description: Pan Di's glosses on the Eastern Zhou Stone Drum inscriptions; a late Ming rubbing of a Yuan dynasty engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou. The relative legibility of certain characters suggests a mid-Qing date for this rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiyang lie jie (1)
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou. This rubbing includes the first of the ten inscriptions only; taken from a replica inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taishan ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription from Taishan, Shandong, one of six or seven inscriptions dating from Qinshihuang's tours of the east; with supplementary text and the names of attendant ministers added by Qin'ershi. In the calligraphy of Li Si. Twenty-nine characters only remaining. This rubbing taken from a Jiaqing era (1796-1820) engraving (after an old rubbing) commissioned by Ruan Yuan (1764-1849); includes colophones added by Ruan Yuan and Weng Fanggang (1733-1818).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qun chen shang shou ke shi
titles_all: Qun chen shang shou ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription recording congratulations offered by court officials on the occasion of the birthday of the prince of Zhao 趙王.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ganquan shan can zi
entries.description: Inscriptions from a fragmented stone found by Ruan Yuan 阮元 (1764–1849) at Ganquanshan, Jiangsu, in 1806 and believed by Weng Fanggang 翁方綱 (1733–1818) to date to the construction of the palace of Liu Xu 劉胥, Prince Li 厲王, i.e., sometime before 54 B.C.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ganquan shan can zi bing ti ji
titles_all: Ganquan shan can zi bing ti ji
entries.description: Inscriptions from a fragmented stone found by Ruan Yuan 阮元 (1764–1849) at Ganquanshan, Jiangsu, in 1806 and believed by Weng Fanggang 翁方綱 (1733–1818) to date to the construction of the palace of Liu Xu 劉胥, Prince Li 厲王, i.e., sometime before 54 B.C. This rubbing includes postscripts by Ruan Yuan and Weng Fanggang concerning the discovery and identification of the stones.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu xiao wang ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscribed with an Wufeng era (57-54 B.C.) date: uncovered at the former site of the Lingguangdian of the princedom of Lu during the Mingchang era (1190-96). Includes postscript (dated 1191) by Gao Deyi recording discovery of the stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu xiao wang ke shi bing ti ij
titles_all: Lu xiao wang ke shi bing ti ij
entries.description: Stone inscribed with an Wufeng era (57–54 B.C.) date: uncovered at the former site of the Lingguangdian 靈光殿 of the princedom of Lu during the Mingchang era (1190–96). Includes postscript (dated 1191) by Gao Deyi 高德裔 recording discovery of the stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Laizihou ke shi
entries.description: The Laizihou inscription, unearthed by Yan Fengjia 顏逢甲 and Sun Shengrong 孫生容 in Zouxian, Shandong, in 1817, and dating to the Xin dynasty of Wang Mang 王莽 (r. 9–23). This album also includes a rubbing of Yan and Sun's colophon of 1817.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shen jun shen dao you que
titles_all: Shen jun shen dao you que
entries.description: Inscription from the right memorial pillar (que) from the passage to the tomb of Shen □, director of Xinfeng 新豐 and defender of Jiaozhi 交趾. Includes rubbing of Vermilion Bird surmounting textual inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shen jun shen dao zuo que
titles_all: Shen jun shen dao zuo que
entries.description: Inscription from the left memorial pillar (que) from the passage to the tomb of Shen □, director of Xinfeng 新豐 and defender of Jiaozhi 交趾.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chu jun kai tong Baoye dao ke shi
titles_all: Chu jun kai tong Baoye dao ke shi
entries.description: Stele inscription of the text of the imperial edict received by the governor of Hanzhong 漢中 commandery ordering construction of the Baoye road.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yupen er zi ke shi
entries.description: Inscription of the words “yu pen” from Shimen 石 門 , Shaanxi, from a large rock at the edge of the Bao River 褒 水. This rubbing also includes inscriptions of personal names dating to the Southern Song.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shimen mo ya
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen, Shaanxi: “Shimen.”

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi hu mo ya
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen, Shaanxi, traditionally attributed to the scholar-recluse Zheng Zizhen and said to have been written by him while living in retirement in the Bao Valley 褒谷: “Shi hu.”

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jianchu yuan nian mai di quan
titles_all: Jianchu yuan nian mai di quan
entries.description: Deed of purchase of hill land by six brothers for 30,000 cash during the Jianchu era (76–84). This rubbing of the inscription lacks the two characters“Daji” 大吉.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.description: Deed of purchase of hill land by six brothers for 30,000 cash during the Jianchu era (76–84). This rubbing of the inscription includes the two characters “Daji” 大吉.

entries.title: Nanwuyang que nan que
entries.description: Inscription from the south memorial pillar (que) of Nanwuyang, from the tomb of an unnamed instructor and husbander of Gongcao 功曹 township.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Nanwuyang que xi que
entries.description: Inscription from the west memorial pillar (que) of Nanwuyang, from the tomb of Huang Shengqing 皇聖卿 of Pingyi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Taishi shi que ming (que qian ming)
titles_all: Songshan Taishi shi que ming (que qian ming)
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of the Taishi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing includes the inscription preceding the title inscription (“Taishi que qianming” 太室闕前銘) only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Taishi que hou ming
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of the Taishi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing includes the inscription following the title incsription ("Taishi que hou ming") only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Taishi shi que e
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of the Taishi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Shaoshi xi que ming
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of the Shaoshi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing contains ten characters of the original inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Shaoshi shi que ming
entries.description: Inscriptions from the memorial pillar (que) of the Shaoshi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing contains ten characters of the original inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan Shaoshi shi que e
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of the Shaoshi peak of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kaimu miao shi que ming (shang ceng)
titles_all: Kaimu miao shi que ming (shang ceng)
entries.description: Inscription from the memorial pillar (que) of Kaimu miao on Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing includes the upper portion of the inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kaimu miao shi que ming (xia ceng)
titles_all: Kaimu miao shi que ming (xia ceng)
entries.description: Inscriptions from the memorial pillar (que) of Kaimu miao on Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This rubbing includes the lower portion of the inscription only. Attached to one end of this rubbing is a rubbing of the pictorial relief depicting the hare in the moon pounding the elixir of immortality.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Pingguo kai dao ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating construction of a mountain road under the supervision of Liu Pingguo, general of the left in the state of Kucha. This album also includes a Qing dynasty postscript by Xi Lun 錫綸 concerning the discovery of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Pingguo kai dao ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating construction of a mountain road under the supervision of Liu Pingguo, general of the left in the state of Kucha. This rubbing taken from a later recarving of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xixia song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription commemorating repair of the Xixia trestle road carried out under the supervision of Li Xi, governor of Wudu 武都, to facilitate travel for the local population.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fu ge song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription commemorating construction of a bridge at Xili by Li Xi, governor of Wudu 武都, to facilitate travel for the local population.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Huai biao
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen 石門, Shaanxi, commemorating the merit and achievements of Yang Huai and his younger brother Yang Bi 楊弼: composed by Bian Yu, who was put in mind of Yang Huai’s accomplishments while traveling through Shimen.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lou Shou bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Lou Shou (97–174), scholar-recluse. This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript dated 918 and a handwritten postscript by Yang Shoujing 楊守敬 (1839–1915) concerning his acquisition of the rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jian'an shi er nian ke shi
titles_all: Jian'an shi er nian ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription of a Jian’an era (196–220) date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yi dong que ti ming
titles_all: Gao Yi dong que ti ming
entries.description: Inscription from the east memorial pillar (que) from the passage to the tomb of Gao Yi (d. 209), governor of Yizhou. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yi xi que ti ming
titles_all: Gao Yi xi que ti ming
entries.description: Inscription from the west memorial pillar (que) from the passage to the tomb of Gao Yi (d. 209), governor of Yizhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gun xue mo ya
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen, Shaanxi, traditionally attributed to Cao Cao and said to have been written by him on arriving at Hanzhong 漢中 sometime during the Jian’an era (196–220): “Gun xue.”

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Bao tong ge dao ti ming
titles_all: Li Bao tong ge dao ti ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen, Shaanxi, commemorating completed construction of a trestle road by Li Bao in 263: this rubbing taken from a later recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tianjian jing lan deng
entries.description: Various inscriptions from the Tianjian reign (502–19), including inscriptions from a well railing and from the memorial pillars (que 闕) marking the spirit ways to the tombs of Xiao Hong 蕭宏, Xiao Ji 蕭績, Xiao Zhengli 蕭正立, and Xiao Ying 蕭瑛.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Xi shang bei
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Zheng Daozhao from Tianzhu shan, Shandong: the (earlier) Zheng Xi stele, extolling the virtue of Zheng Daozhao’s father, Zheng Xi (426–93), director of the Secretariat.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Xi xia bei
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Zheng Daozhao from Yunfeng shan, Shandong: the (later) Zheng Xi stele, extolling the virtue of Zheng Daozhao’s father, Zheng Xi (426–93), director of the Secretariat. Includes title inscription.

This album also includes a handwritten colophon by Wu Yun 吳雲 (1811–83) concerning identification and location of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Langyatai ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription from Langyatai, Shandong, one of six or seven inscriptions dating from Qin Shihuangdi’s 秦始皇帝 (r. 221–210 B.C.) tours to the east: with supplementary text and the names of attendant ministers added by Qin'ershi 二世皇帝. In the calligraphy of Li Si. The relative legibility of certain characters suggests an early Qing date for this rubbing. Previously in the collection of Shen Shuyong 沈樹鏞 (1832–73).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taishan ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription from Taishan, Shandong, one of six or seven inscriptions dating from Qinshihuang's tours to the east: with supplementary text and the names of attendant ministers added by Qin'ershi. In the calligraphy of Li Si. This rubbing taken from the stone fragments unearthed by Jiang Yinpei 蔣因培 in 1815. This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript added by Xu Zonggan 徐宗幹 (1796–1866) in 1832.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yishan ke shi
entries.description: Stone inscription from Yishan, Shandong, one of six or seven inscriptions dating from Qinshihuang’s tours to the east: with supplementary text and the names of attendant ministers. In the calligraphy of Li Si. This rubbing is taken from a later engraving based on the original, probably the Song dynasty Chang'an engraving, now housed in the Beilin 碑林, in Xi’an 西安, Shanxi: the relative legibility of certain characters suggests a Ming dynasty date for the rubbing. This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript added by Zheng Wenbao 鄭文寶 (953–1013) in 993.

This rubbing is taken from a later engraving based on the original, probably the Song dynasty Chang’an engraving, now housed in the Beilin 碑林, in Xi’an 西安, Shanxi: the relative legibility of certain characters suggests a mid-Qing date for the rubbing. entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou. The relative legibility of certain characters in the text suggests an early date for this rubbing, probably late Yuan or early Ming. Previous owners include Dai Xi 戴熙, Bao Xi 寶熙, Wang Xiaoyu 王孝禹, and Zhu Yi'an 朱翼盦, among others.

entries.title: Lou shi ban jing ji ji fo jing liang zhong
titles_all: Lou shi ban jing ji ji fo jing liang zhong
entries.description: Mountainside inscription recording initial construction of a grotto temple by the dharma master Fang and its completion by the dharma master Chou 稠, accompanied by a portion of juan 12 (“Shengxing pin” 聖行品) of the Mahaparinirvana-sutra 大般涅盤經 and a gatha from the Avatamsaka-sutra 華嚴經; the votive inscription and the sutras were both dedicated by disciples of Chou’s during the Qianming era (560).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wen shu bo re jing
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of the Wenshu banruo jing. Inlcudes title inscription and rubbing of an image of the Buddha.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jianshan mo ya
entries.description: Ten mountainside inscriptions, including two sutras, personal names, and a votive inscription concerning dedication of the engraving by Wei Zishen 韋子深, descendant of Wei Xian 韋賢 of Chang’an, and the monk Seng’an 僧安 at Jianshan, Shandong, in 575.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jin gang jing can jing ji ju
titles_all: Jin gang jing can jing ji ju
entries.description: Mountainside inscription (fragmentary) of the Diamond Sutra, from Shijing yu, Shandong, dating to the Wuping era: eighteen characters only. The characters have been individually extracted from the original inscription of the sutra and rearranged to form a gatha. In vermilion and black.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jin gang jing can zi sa zi
titles_all: Jin gang jing can zi sa zi
entries.description: Mountainside inscription (fragmentary) of the Diamond Sutra: thirty characters only, inscribed at Shijing yu, Taishan, during the Wuping era. The characters have been individually extracted from the original inscription of the sutra and do not represent any particular passage.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shuiniu shan mo ya jing ke
titles_all: Shuiniu shan mo ya jing ke
entries.description: Mountainside inscription (fragmentary) of a Buddhist sutra: fifty-two characters only, inscribed at Shuiniu shan, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo jing da zi
entries.description: Six big characters from a mountainside inscription of a sutra: “ming” 名, “ling” 令, “shou” 壽, “duo” 多, “ze” 則, “gang” 剛.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kuang Zhe ke jing song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of a sutra and a votive inscription concerning its dedication by Kuang Zhe, son of Kuang Heng 匡衡, at Xiaotie shan, Shandong, during the Daxiang era (579-80).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiaotie shan mo ya
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of a sutra from Gangshan, Shandong, and inscription of personal names, both dating from the Northern Zhou: Sun Qia 孫洽, the priest Daoyi 僧道壹, and Lü Changsong 閭長嵩.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan wu liang shou fo jing
titles_all: Guan wu liang shou fo jing
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of the Aparimitayus-sutra, from Gangshan, Shandong, and dating from the Daxiang era (579–80).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huihui deng ti ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of the names of three mendicants, from Gangshan, Shandong, dating from the Daxiang era (579-80): Hui-hui, Fa-hui, and Daocheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo jing mo ya ke zi
titles_all: Fo jing mo ya ke zi
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of a Buddhist sutra, possibly from Shandong, possibly dating to the Northern Dynasties era: six characters only, "you" 有, "tian" 天, "ren" 人, "shi" 時, "xia" 下, "ze" 則.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Miao fa lian hua jing
entries.description: Inscription of the Lotus Sutra by the dharma master Jingwan 靜琬, at Fangshan, Beijing, dating from the Sui dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daming zhou ke shi
entries.description: Inscription of the Avalokitesvara-mantra (Daming zhou) from Qianfo shan, Shandong: “Om ma ni padme hum” 唵嘛呢巴彌哞.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuhan shan fo jing
entries.description: Inscription of a Buddhist sutra; possibly from Yuhan shan, Shandong, possibly of Sui date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the first of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the second of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the fourth of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the right portion of the fifth of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the left portion of the fifth of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Taishan ming
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Tang Xuanzong commemorating successful completion of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Taishan, Shandong. This rubbing comprises the second of five parts.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taishan yu yin ming bing xu
titles_all: Taishan yu yin ming bing xu
entries.description: An impression of the jade seal traditionally said to have been used by Song Zhenzong (r. 998-1022) when he performed the feng and shan sacrifices at Taishan; with accompanying inscriptions and colophons. The original seal was destroyed by fire; the impression reproduced in this engraving represents a Daoguang era (1821-50) stone engraving based on the original. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Duanzhou shi shi ji
entries.description: Inscription by Li Yong recording visit by a prefect of Duanzhou to Songtai 嵩臺, a large chamber-like cave in the mountain Qixing yan, in Guangdong; in Li’s calligraphy. This album also includes a rubbing of an inscription of a personal name, dated 1169.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longrui gong ji
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by He Zhizhang concerning the renaming of Longrui gong, Kuaiji 會稽; in He’s calligraphy. This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript by Tao Junxuan 陶濬宣 (d.1915) dated 1893.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Da Tang zhong xing song
entries.description: The “Da Tang zhongxing song,” composed by Yuan Jie during the Shangyuan era (760–62) and copied out and inscribed on the cliff overlooking Wuxi in Hunan by Yan Zhenqing during the Dali era (766–79). These albums also include two handwritten colophons dated 1894.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bore tai ti ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a “wisdom terrace” (bore tai) by Li Gong 李貢, editorial director, during the Dali era (766-79); the inscription, in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing, is located on a wall of Shenguang si 神光寺, Fuzhou 福州.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xie tian shu bing shu sheng gong de ming (1)
titles_all: Xie tian shu bing shu sheng gong de ming (1)
entries.description: Inscription by Song Zhenzong commemorating performance of the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices at Taishan during the Dazhong xiangfu era (1008–16), and recounting the merit and achievements of Song Taizu 太祖 (r. 960–76) and Song Taizong 太宗 (r. 976–97) in expanding the borders of the state and settling the empire. This rubbing represents the first of the inscription’s five stones.

This rubbing represents the second of the inscription’s five stones.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baoyuan er nian ti ke
entries.description: Rubbing of a stone inscribed with the words “Baoyuan ernian liuyue” 寶元二年六月; possibly a portion of Ye Qingchen’s 葉清臣 (1000–1049) 1039 autograph at Shimen dong 石門洞, Zhejiang.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang Tingjian ti bi
entries.description: Two inscriptions by Huang Tingjian, from the cliffs at Liubei chi, Sichuan: “Wuliangshou fo” 無量壽佛; and “Nanji laoren” 南極老人. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang Tingjian ti bi
entries.description: Two inscriptions by Huang Tingjian, from the cliffs at Liubei chi, Sichuan: “Wuliangshou fo”; and “Nanji laoren.”

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Biao jun shen dao shi ren deng qi zhong
titles_all: Biao jun shen dao shi ren deng qi zhong
entries.description: Rubbings of seven items dating from the Wufeng era (57 B.C.–54 B.C.) through the end of the Later Han dynasty: inscriptions on stone guardians from the spirit way to the tomb of the lord of Biao, with a postscript added by Ruan Yuan 阮元 (1764–1849) in 1894.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shanhe yan luo cheng ji
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Shimen, Shaanxi, commemorating restoration of the Shanhe dike.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chu jun ge dao mo ya shi wen
titles_all: Chu jun ge dao mo ya shi wen
entries.description: Transcription of the text of the Han dynasty Chujun gedao stele 鄐君閣道碑, with appended inscription of names; in the calligraphy of Yan Mao, magistrate of Nanzheng 南鄭.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yupen mo ya ti ji
entries.description: Inscription of personal names from Shimen, Shaanxi, from a large rock at the water’s edge inscribed with the words “yupen” 玉盆; inscriptions left by Zhao Yan 趙彥 and others while traveling through the area during the Baoqing era (1225–27).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dade qi nian ta ben
entries.description: Fragment of a rubbing bearing the inscription "Dade qinian."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: You Yuhan shan xi yu ji
titles_all: You Yuhan shan xi yu ji
entries.description: Account of a tour of Yuhan shan by Li Jian of Licheng, in the party of Zhang Mengchen 張夢臣, grand master for court service, during the Yuanzhi era (1335–40).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: You Qianfo shan ti bi shi
titles_all: You Qianfo shan ti bi shi
entries.description: Mountainside inscription of two poems composed by Wang Dayu and Wang Hong while touring Qianfo shan, Shandong, during the Daoguang era (1821–50); in Wang Hong’s calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Xi bei shi wen
entries.description: The (earlier and later) Zheng Xi steles, extolling the virtue of Zheng Xi (426–93), director of the Secretariat; in Wu Yun’s transcription, with accompanying colophons. The stele inscriptions were originally composed by and written in the calligraphy of Zheng Xi’s son, Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (d. 515).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu liang yi jing
entries.description: A portion of the Wu liang yi jing inscribed on the western wall of cave no. 2 (southern sector) at Xiangtang shan (North), Hebei, dating to the Northern Qi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiamen gu ke
entries.description: Inscription of the "Xiamen guke": a portent text. Possibly from Lingshi, Shanxi; date uncertain. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiamen gu ke yin
entries.description: Rubbing of the verso of the stone inscribed with the "Xiamen guke": a whorl and a flower pattern. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sui ci bing yin
entries.description: Inscription beginning with the words "Sui ci bingyin"; exact date, provenance, and content of inscription uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: □ bi ti ji
entries.description: Rubbing (fragmentary) of an inscription of uncertain date and provenance. Title inscription reads "bi"

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Can shi
entries.description: Rubbing of a stone fragment inscribed with a single graph; period, provenance uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bu zhi ming ta ben yi
titles_all: Bu zhi ming ta ben yi
entries.description: Rubbing of an indecipherable graph, in mirror script; date and provenance unknown.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yun yuan er zi
entries.description: Mountainside inscription from Korea: “Yun yuan.”

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gui Fu ti ji
entries.description: Rubbing (fragmentary) of an inscription written by the scholar Gui Fu after viewing the engraving from which the rubbing Tsa 125 (6)-28.2 was taken.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chu jun kai tong Baoye dao ke shi
titles_all: Chu jun kai tong Baoye dao ke shi
entries.description: Stele inscription of the text of the imperial edict received by the governor of Hanzhong 漢中 commandery ordering construction of the Baoye road.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan san que ming
entries.description: Inscriptions and pictorial reliefs from the three memorial pillars (que) of Songshan, Henan—the memorial pillars of the peaks Taishi and Shaoshi and of Kaimu miao—commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This album also includes handwritten colophons by Wu Yun 吳雲 (1811–83) and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan san que ming
entries.description: Inscriptions from the three memorial pillars (que) of Songshan, Henan—the memorial pillars of the peaks Taishi and Shaoshi and of Kaimu miao—commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This album includes rubbings of textual inscriptions only, no pictorial relief.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songshan san que ming
entries.description: Inscriptions and pictorial relief from the three memorial pillars (que) of Songshan, Henan—the memorial pillars of the peaks Taishi and Shaoshi and of Kaimu miao—commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain. This album includes one rubbing only of pictorial relief and lacks two of the textual inscriptions (“Taishi que qianming” 太室闕前銘, “Jidu ming” 季度銘 ) included in the preceding two collections.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gu wen
entries.description: The Stone Drum inscriptions: verse celebrating the hunting exploits of a duke of Qin, engraved on ten drum-shaped stones. The inscriptions date from the Eastern Zhou. The relative legibility of certain characters suggests a late Ming date for this rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shimen song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription commemorating repair of the Baoye 褒斜 road completed under the supervision of Yang Huan (zi Mengwen 孟文).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shimen song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription commemorating repair of the Baoye 褒斜 road completed under the supervision of Yang Huan (zi Mengwen 孟文).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shimen song
entries.description: Mountainside inscription commemorating repair of the Baoye 褒斜 road completed under the supervision of Yang Huan (zi Mengwen 孟文).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Song gao miao ming ji
entries.description: Inscription from the three memorial pillars (que) of Songshan, Henan, commemorating sacrifices made to the spirit of the mountain, commemorating sacrifices made by Tangxi Dian in supplication for rain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yi que heng e ti ming
titles_all: Gao Yi que heng e ti ming
entries.description: Horizontal inscription from the passage to the tomb of Gao Yi (d. 209), governor of Yizhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yi shuang que ti ming
titles_all: Gao Yi shuang que ti ming
entries.description: Inscriptions from the memorial pillars (que), east and west, from the passage to the tomb of Gao Yi (d. 209), governor of Yizhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yangjia can bei
entries.description: Stele inscription (fragmentary) commemorating the merit and achievements of a former official, recently deceased; erected by subordinate officials and local citizens during the Yangjia era (132-35). This rubbing taken from a later recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pei Cen li gong bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the meritorious service of Pei Cen, governor of Dunhuang, during his posting to the Western Regions. This rubbing taken from a later recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wu Ban (d. 145), member of the Wu clan; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei yin
entries.description: Verso of the Wu Ban (d. 145) stele, bearing the inscription "Wu shi bei"; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wu Ban (d. 145), member of the Wu clan; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei yin
entries.description: Verso of the Wu Ban (d. 145) stele, bearing the inscription "Wu shi bei"; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong. This rubbing includes a two-line inscription recording a visit to the shrine site by three men at a later, unspecified date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wu Ban (d. 145), member of the Wu clan; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Ban bei yin
entries.description: Verso of the Wu Ban (d. 145) stele, bearing the inscription "Wu shi bei"; from the Wu Family Shrines at Jiaxiang, Shandong. This rubbing includes a two-line inscription recording a visit to the shrine site by three men at a later, unspecified date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi Ying bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording the request of Yi Ying, administrator of Lu 魯, to post subofficial functionaries and guards to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi Ying bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording the request of Yi Ying, administrator of Lu 魯, to post subofficial functionaries and guards to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi Ying bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording the request of Yi Ying, administrator of Lu 魯, to post subofficial functionaries and guards to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Mengchu shen ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the merit and achievements of Li Mengchu, regional inspector of Yizhou; erected by subordinate officials and citizens of the region during the Yongxing era (153-54).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Qian jie
entries.description: Tablet inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Qian (121-54), son of Kong Zhou 孔宙 (104-64), commandant of Taishan 泰山 and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.description: Tablet inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Qian (121-54), son of Kong Zhou 孔宙 (104-64), commandant of Taishan 泰山 and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.title: Kong Lin jie
entries.description: Tablet inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Lin, nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius; tablet unearthed in 1793.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Lin jie
entries.description: Tablet inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Lin, nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius; tablet unearthed in 1793.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Lin jie
entries.description: Tablet inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Lin, nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius; tablet unearthed in 1793.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li qi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording sacrifices offered to Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回, and the manufacture of ritual vessels for the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Han Chi 韓敕 (fl. 155-58), administrator of Lu 魯, during the Yongshou era (155-58). Includes verso and sides of stele with inscription of contributors' personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li qi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording sacrifices offered to Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回, and the manufacture of ritual vessels for the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Han Chi 韓敕 (fl. 155-58), administrator of Lu 魯, during the Yongshou era (155-58). Includes verso and sides of stele with inscription of contributors' personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li qi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording sacrifices offered to Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回, and the manufacture of ritual vessels for the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Han Chi 韓敕 (fl. 155-58), administrator of Lu 魯, during the Yongshou era (155-58). Includes verso and sides of stele with inscription of contributors' personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Gu bei e
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zheng Gu (d. 158), gentleman of the interior. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Gu bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zheng Gu (d. 158), gentleman of the interior. This album contains two sets of rubbings of the epitaph, each including title inscription, and a rubbing of the fragment unearthed by Li Kun in 1728, the latter with a handwritten colophon by Zheng Shuqian 鄭淑虔 dated 1847. Previous owners include Song Luo 宋犖 (1634-1713), Tu Zhou 屠倬 (1781-1828), and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Gu can bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zheng Gu (d. 158), gentleman of the interior. This rubbing includes the fragment unearthed by Li Kun 李鵾 in 1728 only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Gu can bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zheng Gu (d. 158), gentleman of the interior. This rubbing includes the fragment unearthed by Li Kun 李鵾 in 1728 only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cang Jie ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription from a shrine dedicated to Cang Jie. This album also includes rubbings of colophons, among them one added by Lü Dazhong (fl. 1053-96) in 1060.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Zhou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Kong Zhou (104-64), commandant of Taishan, father of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Zhou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Kong Zhou (104-64), commandant of Taishan, father of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Shou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhang Shou (89-168), marquis of Zhu Yi 竹邑.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Zhou bei yin
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Kong Zhou (104-64), commandant of Taishan, father of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius. Verso of stele only: inscription of personal names of pupils and former subordinates of Kong Zhou.

entries.title: Kong Zhou bei yin
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Kong Zhou (104-64), commandant of Taishan 泰山, father of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius. Verso of stele only: inscription of personal names of pupils and former subordinates of Kong Zhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Zhou bei yin
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Kong Zhou (104-64), commandant of Taishan 泰山, father of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius. Verso of stele only: inscription of personal names of pupils and former subordinates of Kong Zhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Zhou bei yin

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fenglong shan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices made at Fenglong shan. Inscription unearthed by Liu Baonan 劉寳楠 (1791-1855) in 1847.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fenglong shan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices made at Fenglong shan. Inscription unearthed by Liu Baonan 劉寳楠 (1791-1855) in 1847.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huashan miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription from the Huashan miao, Huayin, Shaanxi, commemorating sacrifices made to the mountain by successive emperors of the Han dynasty; recarving commissioned by Yuan Feng 遠逄, governor of Hongnong 弘農, in 165. This rubbing taken from a replica engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Heng Fang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Heng Fang (106-68), chamberlain of the court for the palace garrison. This album also includes a handwritten colophon added by Shen Shuyong (1832-73) in 1870. Collectors' seals and a handwritten annotation on the album's label indicate that this rubbing was once in the possession of Sun Xingyan 孫星衍 (1753-1818).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Heng Fang bei yin
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Heng Fang (106-68), chamberlain of the court for the palace garrison. Verso of stele only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Chen si Kongzi zou ming
titles_all: Shi Chen si Kongzi zou ming
entries.description: Inscription of the text of the memorial sent up to the imperial secretary by Shi Chen, administrator of Lu 魯, requesting permission to perform sacrifices at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Jianning era (168-72).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Chen si Kongzi zou ming

entries.description: Inscription of the text of the memorial sent up to the imperial secretary by Shi Chen, administrator of Lu 魯, requesting permission to perform sacrifices at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Jianning era (168-72).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Chen xiang Kongzi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices offered to Confucius by Shi Chen, administrator of Lu 魯, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, in 169.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Chen xiang Kongzi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices offered to Confucius by Shi Chen, administrator of Lu 魯, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, in 169.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Youdao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Guo Youdao (zi Linzong 林宗, 128-69) of Taiyuan 太原; composed by Cai Yong 蔡邕. This rubbing taken from the stone fragment unearthed in Jining, Shandong, and accepted by some as the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xia Cheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Xia Cheng (115-70), magistrate of Chunyu in Beihai commandery. This rubbing taken from a later engraving, probably the recarving commissioned by Tang Yao during the Jiajing era (1522-66).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Biao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Biao (123-71), governor of Boling commandery and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius; erected by the subordinate officials of the commandery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Biao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Biao (123-71), governor of Boling commandery and nineteenth lineal descendant of Confucius; erected by the subordinate officials of the commandery. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

Verso of stele only: inscription of names of the former subordinate officials who erected the stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiping can bei
entries.description: Epitaph (fragmentary, and consequently lacking the name of the epitaph's subject) dated 174. This rubbing includes inscriptions added to the stone by Weng Fanggang (1733-1818) and others after its discovery in 1793.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiping can bei
entries.description: Epitaph (fragmentary, and consequently lacking the name of the epitaph's subject) dated 174. This rubbing includes inscriptions added to the stone by Weng Fanggang 翁方綱 (1733-1818) and others after its discovery in 1793.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Geng Xun bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Geng Xun, governor of Wudu. This rubbing postdates the reworking of the inscription noted in Jinshi cuibian 金石萃編.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han Ren ming
entries.description: Stone inscription commending the virtuous administration of Han Ren (d. 175), magistrate of Wenxi. Includes title inscription. This album also includes rubbings of postscripts added by Zhao Bingwen (1159-1232), Li Xianneng (1192-1232), and Li Tianyi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yin Zhou bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Yin Zhou (116-77), retainer of Yuzhou 豫州. Includes two characters only of title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sangong shan bei
entries.description: Stone inscription commemorating sacrifices made to Sangong shan, Fenglong shan, and Lingshan, Hebei. Includes title inscription of four large characters in seal script, with six smaller characters (three on either side of the main title inscription) in clerical script.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sangong shan bei
entries.description: Stone inscription commemorating sacrifices made to Sangong shan, Fenglong shan 封龍山, and Lingshan 靈山, Hebei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pan Qian de zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Pan Qian, magistrate of Liyang 溧陽; erected by the subordinate officials and citizens of Liyang. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pan Qian de zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Pan Qian, magistrate of Liyang 溧陽; erected by the subordinate officials and citizens of Liyang. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pan Qian de zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Pan Qian, magistrate of Liyang 溧陽; erected by the subordinate officials and citizens of Liyang. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiping shi jing can shi
entries.description: Rubbings of fragments of the Xiping stone classics, including passages from the Shangshu, Shijing, Yili, Gongyang zhuan, and Lunyu. This rubbing probably taken from a Song or Ming engraving based on the original. This rubbing also includes two handwritten colophons dated 1891 and 1903.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Jixuan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zheng Jixuan (129-85), magistrate of Weishi 尉氏.

entries.title: Zhang Qian bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Zhang Qian, former magistrate of Gucheng; erected by former subordinate officials of Gucheng. Includes title inscription; and verso of stele with inscription of personal names of donors.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiao Min bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with epitaph of Qiao Min, palace attendant. This rubbing taken from a later recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Bao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Bao, retainer of Yuzhou, elder brother of Kong Rong (153-208), and twentieth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Bao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Kong Bao, retainer of Yuzhou, elder brother of Kong Rong 孔融 (153-208), and twentieth lineal descendant of Confucius.

entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chao ☐, magistrate of Yu. Includes title inscription. This rubbing taken from the original inscription, no longer extant.

entries.title: Yu ling Zhao jun bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chao ☐, magistrate of Yu. Includes title inscription. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xian ren Tang Gongfang bei
entries.description: Stele inscription written at the direction of Guo Zhi 郭芝, governor of Hanzhong 漢中, including a brief account of the recluse-immortal Tang Gongfang's transcendence in 7 B.C. and the construction of a shrine dedicated to him. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu xiang ye Kong miao can bei
titles_all: Lu xiang ye Kong miao can bei
entries.description: Stele (fragmentary) commemorating a visit by an unidentified administrator of the state of Lu to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Han dynasty; the stone was uncovered at the temple during the Qianlong era (1736-95).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han can bei
entries.description: Stele (fragmentary) from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: the uppermost row of characters only from a section of textual inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Rong bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wu Rong (d. 168), aide to the chamberlain for the imperial insignia during the Later Han. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han can zi bei
entries.description: Rubbing of a fragment of a stone stele: twelve characters only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang jun gong de bei
entries.description: Stele inscription honoring the merit and achievement of a certain Wang, who used his own body to block floodwaters when the river's dikes could no longer contain them; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wei si xin zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the promulgation of a new policy for the circulation of currency; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kai tu ti zi
entries.description: Inscriptions from a stele in the Jieting of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: the title inscription, "Jie tu"; and "Zigong shouzhi jie" 子貢手植楷.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Kongzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription attributed to Cao Zhi commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong; enfeoffment of Kong Xian 孔羨 as Zongsheng hou 宗聖侯, in which capacity he was to preside over all sacrifices offered at the temple; and the posting of subofficial functionaries and guards to the temple compound. Calligraphy attributed to Liang Hu. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Kongzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription attributed to Cao Zhi commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong; enfeoffment of Kong Xian 孔羨 as Zongsheng hou 宗聖侯, in which capacity he was to preside over all sacrifices offered at the temple; and the posting of subofficial functionaries and guards to the temple compound. Calligraphy attributed to Liang Hu. Includes (two rubbings of) title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Kongzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription attributed to Cao Zhi commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong; enfeoffment of Kong Xian 孔羨 as Zongsheng hou 宗聖侯, in which capacity he was to preside over all sacrifices offered at the temple; and the posting of subofficial functionaries and guards to the temple compound. Calligraphy attributed to Liang Hu. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shou shan biao
entries.description: Inscription of the memorial whereby Cao Pi 曹丕 (187-226) accepted the abdication of Han Xiandi 獻帝 (r. 189-220). Calligraphy attributed to Liang Hu 梁鵠; engraving attributed to Zhong You 鍾繇. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shang zun hao zou bei
entries.description: Inscription of the memorial whereby Cao Pi's 曹丕 (187-226) military and political advisors urged him to accept the abdication of Han Xiandi 獻帝 (r. 189-220). Calligraphy attributed by some to Liang Hu 梁鵠, by others to Zhong You 鍾繇 (151-230). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cao Zhen bei
entries.description: Stele (fragmentary) inscribed with the epitaph of Cao Zhen (d. 231), eulogizing his exploits in battle with the state of Shu 蜀.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fan Shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Fan Shi, governor of Lujiang; erected by former subordinate officials. Includes title inscription, and verso of stele with inscription of personal names of former subordinate officials. This rubbing also includes postscripts added to the verso of the stele by Huang Yi (1744-1802) and Li Kezheng 李克正.

entries.title: Tian fa shen chen bei
entries.description: Stele inscription (fragmentary) commemorating discovery of a stone inscribed with prognostications. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.description: Stele inscription (fragmentary) commemorating discovery of a stone inscribed with prognostications. This rubbing also includes a postscript by Hu Zongshi (dated 1091) concerning discovery of the fragmented stele. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shan Guoshan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the occurrence of auspicious events following the accession of Sun Hao 孫皓 (Guiming hou 歸命侯, r. 264-80); erected on the occasion of the shan 禪 sacrifice offered at Guoshan in 275.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Deng Ai ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of a shrine dedicated to Deng Ai (197-264), undertaken by Zheng Nengmiao 鄭能邈, general establishing majesty (jianwei jiangjun 建威軍將), in 367.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.title: Guangwu jiang jun □ chan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the acquisition of new territory by Chan ☐, general extending martial virtue (guangwu jiangjun 廣武軍將). This sheet also includes a rubbing of one of the two sides of the stele, with inscription of personal names of subordinate officers.

entries.title: Yang Yang shen dao que
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph from the spirit way to the tomb of Yang Yang, "filial and incorrupt" commandant of cavalry for Ba commandery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cuan Baozi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cuan Baozi (381-405), governor of Jianning. This rubbing also includes a postscript by Deng Erheng dated 1852.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cuan Baozi bei

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cuan Baozi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cuan Baozi (381-405), governor of Jianning; this rubbing taken from a Qing recarving. This album also includes a handwritten colophon (dated 1876) by Zhao Song.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Hao Daiwang ji gong bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the merit and achievements of Hot'ae Wang, more commonly known as Kwanggaet'o Wang 廣開土王, of Koryo, in expanding the borders of the kingdom and settling the land. This is not a rubbing, but a traced double-outline (shuannggou 雙鉤) of the characters of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Song gao ling miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of Lingmiao on Songshan, Henan, during the Taiyan era (435-40) and establishment of a sanctuary (daochang 道場) there by Kou Qianzhi 寇謙之. Includes title inscription. This album also includes a handwritten colophon by Cai Ding dated 1877.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cuan Longyan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cuan Longyan (386-446), regional inspector of Ningzhou. This rubbing also includes postscripts added by Ruan Yuan (1764-1849), Zhang Hao (1811-70), and Qiu Jun'en.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cuan Longyan bei bing yin
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cuan Longyan (386-446), regional inspector of Ningzhou. This rubbing also includes postscripts added by Ruan Yuan (1764-1849), Zhang Hao (1811-70), and Qiu Jun'en.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huifu si bei
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa by the eunuch Wang Qingshi 王慶時, on behalf of the emperor and empress, during the Taihe era (477-99). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Diao Bi Gan wen
entries.description: Eulogy for Bi Gan (12th cent. B.C.), composed by Xiaowendi of the Northern Wei, Yuan Hong 元宏; this rubbing taken from a Yuanyou (1086-94) engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Faya deng yi qian ren zao ta bei
titles_all: Faya deng yi qian ren zao ta bei
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a nine-storied stupa by the mendicant Faya and the members of a Buddhist sodality--one thousand donors in all--on belhalf of Xiaowendi 孝文帝 (r. 471-99) of the Northern Wei, during the Zhengshi era (504-8). This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript (dated 585) added by Yang Fagui at the time of the stone's removal from its original site.

entries.title: Jing Xianjun xiu Chanjing si bei bing yin
titles_all: Jing Xianjun xiu Chanjing si bei bing yin
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of Chanjing si at Changge, Henan, by Jing Xianjun, cavalry general and regional inspector of Yingzhou 潁州, during the Xinghe era (539-42). This album also includes a rubbing of the verso of the stele, with inscription of personal names.
