entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Mengchu shen ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the merit and achievements of Li Mengchu, regional inspector of Yizhou; erected by subordinate officials and citizens of the region during the Yongxing era (153-54).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan Sheng de zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Guan Sheng (450-547), regional inspector of Jizhou; erected by former subordinate officials of Jizhou during the Wuding era (543-550). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tai gong Lü Wang biao
entries.description: Stele inscription by Mu Zirong, governor of Ji 汲 commandery, commemorating restoration of Taigong miao, Jixian, by descendants of the Shang 尚 clan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ximen Bao ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating Ximen Bao's successful termination of the sacrifices at Ye 鄴, and recounting the establishment and gradual decline of the shrine dedicated to him at Yexian; stele erected by Gao Yue 高岳, prince of Qinghe 清河, during the Tianbao era (550-59). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ximen Bao ci bei yin
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating Ximen Bao's successful termination of the sacrifices at Ye 鄴, and recounting the establishment and gradual decline of the shrine dedicated to him at Yexian; stele erected by Gao Yue 高岳, prince of Qinghe 清河, during the Tianbao era (550-59). Verso of stele only: inscription of personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ximen Bao ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating Ximen Bao's successful termination of the sacrifices at Ye 鄴, and recounting the establishment and gradual decline of the shrine dedicated to him at Yexian; stele erected by Gao Yue 高岳, prince of Qinghe 清河, during the Tianbao era (550-59). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tongque tai shi nian men ji
titles_all: Tongque tai shi nian men ji
entries.description: Inscription recording construction of a stone gate at the Tongque tai in Anyang, Henan, by Chen Ji 陳冀 and others, during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiang Dule zao xiang bei
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of the Buddha of Medicine 藥王, Puxian 普賢 (Samantabhadra), and Manjusri, by Qiang Dule, area commander-in-chief of the Min-Shu 岷蜀 region, and others, on behalf of the memory of Zhou Wenwang 周文王, Yuwen Tai 宇文泰 (506-557), in 557. Includes title inscription and rubbings of two images.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dingguo si Ling ta bei ming
titles_all: Dingguo si Ling ta bei ming
entries.description: Votive stele with an inscription concerning construction of a stupa by Gao Rui (535-71), prince of Zhao commandery, at Dingguo si, Lingshou, during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dingguo si song
entries.description: Inscription praising the restoration of Dingguo si, Lingshou, by Gao Rui 高叡 (535-71), prince of Zhao commandery, during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Shuzu Fuzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Zheng Shuzu, regional inspector of Guangzhou 光州 and son of the calligrapher Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (d. 515), commemorating a visit his father had earlier paid to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Shuzu Fuzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Zheng Shuzu, regional inspector of Guangzhou 光州 and son of the calligrapher Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (d. 515), commemorating a visit his father had earlier paid to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Shuzu Fuzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Zheng Shuzu, regional inspector of Guangzhou 光州 and son of the calligrapher Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (d. 515), commemorating a visit his father had earlier paid to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jun Xiuluo bei bing yin
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtue of Jun Xiuluo; the stone was cut (and a pavilion to house the stele built) by the local elders of Sishui in recognition of his high moral character. This album also includes a rubbing of the verso of the stele inscribed with the text of the Weimo jing 維摩經.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xinluo Zhenxing wang xun shou bei
titles_all: Xinluo Zhenxing wang xun shou bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating a tour of inspection of the border area by Chinghŭngwang of Silla in 568.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuwen Chang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Yuwen Chang (d. 569), left vice director of the Department of State Affairs. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei
titles_all: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei
entries.description: Inscription extolling the virtue of Guo Ju, one of the twenty-four exemplars of filial piety; written by Shen Siyong, an attendant in the entourage of Hu Changren 胡長仁, prince of Longdong, who passed by the funerary shrine of Guo Ju while traveling through Pingyin 平陰, Shandong, and was so moved by the memory of Guo's virtue that he ordered the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei
titles_all: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei
entries.description: Inscription extolling the virtue of Guo Ju 郭巨, one of the twenty-four exemplars of filial piety; written by Shen Siyong, an attendant in the entourage of Hu Changren, prince of Longdong, who passed by the funerary shrine of Guo Ju while traveling through Pingyin 平陰, Shandong, and was so moved by the memory of Guo's virtue that he ordered the inscription. This album also includes a handwritten colophon added by Wu Yun (1811-83).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei e
titles_all: Longdong wang gan xiao song bei e
entries.description: Inscription extolling the virtue of Guo Ju, one of the twenty-four exemplars of filial piety; written by Shen Siyong, an attendant in the entourage of Hu Changren 胡長仁, prince of Longdong, who passed by the funerary shrine of Guo Ju while traveling through Pingyin 平陰, Shandong, and was so moved by the memory of Guo's virtue that he ordered the inscription. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cao Ke bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cao Ke (448-544), governor of Qiao commandery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pingdeng si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of Pingdeng si by Gao Run 高潤, son of Gao Huan 高歡 (d. 547), during the Wuping era (570-76).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lou Dingyuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of Buddha of Infinite Life, Guanyin, and the bodhisattva Mahasthamaprapta 勢至 by Lou Dingyuan, second son of Lou Zhao 婁昭 and prince of Linhuai, at Longxing si, Qingzhou, during the Wuping era (570-76).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Di Zhen bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Di Zhen (d. 534) of Zhongshan 中山, father of a concubine to Xiaojingdi 孝靜帝 (r. 534-50) of the Eastern Wei and regional inspector of Dingzhou. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Di Zhen bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Di Zhen (d. 534) of Zhongshan 中山, father of a concubine to Xiaojingdi 孝靜帝 (r. 534-50) of the Eastern Wei and regional inspector of Dingzhou. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Su bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Gao Su, posthumously title Zhongwu, prince of Lanling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bei qi jing chuang
entries.description: A Buddhist sutra; inscribed on an ornamental stone pillar.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuwen □ de zheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Yuwen ☐, regional inspector of Guangzhou 光州; erected by local gentry.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Bochen chong xiu fo ta ji
titles_all: Guo Bochen chong xiu fo ta ji
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of a stupa by Gao Bochen and Li Yanshou 李延壽 of Yiling 刈陵, Luzhou 潞州, during the Kaihuang era (581-600).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhong Sina deng zao qiao bei
titles_all: Zhong Sina deng zao qiao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating construction of a stone bridge by Zhong Sina and thirty-nine others in Zouxian, Shandong, in 586. Includes personal names of the forty who sponsored the construction.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longzang si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription from Longzang si; erected by Wang Xiaoxian 王孝僊 of Jincheng 金城, prefect of Hengzhou, and the local gentry, in response to imperial command, in 586. Includes title inscription. This album also includes rubbings of inscriptions of personal names from the verso, sides, and title inscription portion of the stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Fen can bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhao Fen (zi Shimao 士茂, d. 590) of Tianshui 天水, general-in-chief, posthumously titled Ding, duke of Huai'an 淮安定公.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: San lao ji ri ji bei
titles_all: San lao ji ri ji bei
entries.description: Stele inscription, presumably from a clan temple, recording the names and death dates of deceased ancestors. Unearthed in Yuyao, Zhejiang, in 1852.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Fen can bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhao Fen (zi Shimao 士茂, d. 590) of Tianshui 天水, general-in-chief, posthumously titled Ding, duke of Huai'an 淮安定公.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cao Zhi bei
entries.description: Stele erected in 593 at a temple dedicated to Cao Zhi (192-232) in Donge, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhuge Ziheng ping Chen zao xiang song
titles_all: Zhuge Ziheng ping Chen zao xiang song
entries.description: Votive inscription dedicated by Zhuge Ziheng, commander-in-chief in charge of Jianyi 建義; Zhuge Shashan 諸葛刹删, adjunct commandant; Xiao Sengqian 蕭僧遷; and others--a hundred in all--in return for the divine protection that favored them during their conquest of the state of Chen. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhengjie si can bei
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Doulu Tong 豆盧通, commander unequaled in honor, at Zhengjie si during the Kaihuang era (581-600).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lishui qiao qian bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating construction of a stone bridge across the Li River in Nanhe, Hebei, by the elder Song Wenbiao 宋文彪 and others, during the Kaihuang era (581-600); this is the earlier of the two Li River bridge inscriptions. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li shi xiang bei zhi song
titles_all: Li shi xiang bei zhi song
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning construction of a shrine and display of ancestral portraits by the dharma master Zhiding 智定 (secular surname, Liu 劉) at the request of the Li clan of Zhao 趙 commandery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Heruo Yi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Heruo Yi (520-96), area commander-in-chief commissioned with extraordinary powers for Lingzhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Anxi gong Li jun bei
entries.description: Tomb inscription of Li ☐, duke of Anxi commissioned with extraordinary powers to act as administrator of Qiongzhou 邛州 and other military prefectures (zhujunshi 諸軍事), son of Li Yanzhi 李炎之 and grandson of Li Jingchao 李景超. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription from a Confucian temple; erected by Kou Wenyue 寇文約 in Wanxian, Hebei, during the Renshou era (601-4). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ning Xuan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ning Xuan, grand master for proper consultation for Qinjiang; erected by his son Ning Shuyu 甯叔于 and Shuyu's younger brothers. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ning Xuan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ning Xuan, grand master for proper consultation for Qinjiang; erected by his son Ning Shuyu 甯叔于 and Shuyu's younger brothers. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
titles_all: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Chen Shuyi, magistrate of Qufu, in 611. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
titles_all: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Chen Shuyi, magistrate of Qufu, in 611. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
titles_all: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Chen Shuyi, magistrate of Qufu, in 611. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
titles_all: Chen Shuyi xiu Kong miao bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating restoration of the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Chen Shuyi, magistrate of Qufu, in 611. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longhua bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating construction of the Longhua ta 龍華塔 by Cui Boyou 崔伯友, Dou Tuo 竇陁, Zhao Tiezheng 趙鐵正, Helian Shiliang 赫連師亮, and Zhen Xiangyuan 甄相願, on behalf of Sui Wendi (r. 581-604), during the Daye era. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liang gong can bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Liang ☐ of Wushi 烏氏, Anding 安定, duke of Deyang and area commander-in-chief commissioned with special powers to act as administrator of the military prefectures (zhujunshi 諸軍事) Liangzhou 涼州, Ganzhou 甘州, and Guazhou 瓜州. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baoliang jing
entries.description: The Baoliang jing; this rubbing comprises the first two sections only, the "Shamen pin" 沙門 and the "Biqiu pin" 比丘品. This rubbing taken from a Sui dynasty engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qin wang gao Shaolin si zhu jiao bei
titles_all: Qin wang gao Shaolin si zhu jiao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription composed by Li Shimin, prince of Qin and the future Tang Taizong, citing the priests of Shaolin si for their aid in defeating the rebel Wang Shichong 王世充 (d. 621) and calming the area of Henan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zang ma ming
entries.description: Tomb inscription for a horse belonging to Geshu Jitong during the Wude era (618-26), purportedly by Wang Zhijing; a forgery

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dengci si bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating the establishment of Dengci si, Fanshui 氾水, by Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49), on behalf of the souls of those slain in battle, after the defeat of Wang Shichong 王世充 (d. 621) and Dou Jiande 竇建德 (573-621) at the Fan River 氾水 in Zhengzhou 鄭州; in the calligraphy of Yan Shigu. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dengci si bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating the establishment of Dengci si, Fanshui 氾水, by Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49), on behalf of the souls of those slain in battle, after the defeat of Wang Shichong 王世充 (d. 621) and Dou Jiande 竇建德 (573-621) at the Fan River 氾水 in Zhengzhou 鄭州; in the calligraphy of Yan Shigu. Includes title inscription. This rubbing alo includes an inscription by Shao Tang 邵堂 dated 1822.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dengci si bei
entries.description: Stele commemorating the establishment of Dengci si, Fanshui 氾水, by Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49), on behalf of the souls of those slain in battle, after the defeat of Wang Shichong 王世充 (d. 621) and Dou Jiande 竇建德 (573-621) at the Fan River 氾水 in Zhengzhou 鄭州; in the calligraphy of Yan Shigu. This rubbing also includes an inscription by Shao Tang dated 1822.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhaoren si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the establishment of Zhaoren si by Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49), on behalf of the souls of those slain in battle, after the defeat of Xue Ju 薛舉 (d. 618) at Binzhou; in the calligraphy of Yu Shinan. This rubbing includes inscriptions of personal names dated 1525 and 1529.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fang Yanqian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Fang Yanqian (547-615); erected by his son Fang Xuanling 房玄齡 (578-648) at Tingshan 亭山, Qizhou 齊州, in 631. Composed by Li Baiyao and written in the calligraphy of Ouyang Xun. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fang Yanqian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Fang Yanqian (547-615); erected by his son Fang Xuanling 房玄齡 (574-648) at Tingshan 亭山, Qizhou 齊州, in 631. Composed by Li Baiyao and written in the calligraphy of Ouyang Xun. This album also includes rubbings of the verso and sides of the stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pei Jingmin bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Pei Jingmin (d. 596), assistant area commander-in-chief of Yizhou under the Sui; erected by his son, Pei Ji 裴勣 of Wenxi, Hedong 河東, in 637. Composed by Li Baiyao and written in the calligraphy of Yin Lingming.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pei Jingmin bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Pei Jingmin (d. 596), assistant area commander-in-chief of Yizhou under the Sui; erected by his son, Pei Ji 裴勣 of Wenxi, Hedong 河東, in 637. Composed by Li Baiyao and written in the calligraphy of Yin Lingming.

entries.title: Pei Jingmin bei

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huangfu Dan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Huangfu Dan (554-604), pillar of state; erected by his son, Huangfu Wuyi 皇甫無逸 of Anding 安定. This album also includes two handwritten notes concerning the edition of the rubbing and the identity of the inscription's engravers--possibly a summary of a discussion between Yang Shoujing (1839-1915) and a member of the Mitsui clan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jin ci ming
entries.description: Inscription from the Jinci, the shrine outside Taiyuan dedicated to Shu Yu 叔虞 (fl. 1024-1005 B.C.); the inscription was composed and copied out by Tang Taizong while visiting the shrine to offer sacrifice in 646. Includes title inscription, in "flying white" script (feibaishu 飛白書).

entries.title: Li qi bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording sacrifices offered to Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回, and the manufacture of ritual vessels for the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, by Han Chi 韓敕 (fl. 155-58), administrator of Lu 魯, during the Yongshou era (155-58). Includes verso and sides of stele with inscription of contributors' personal names.

entries.title: Xin jing
entries.description: The Heart Sutra, in the calligraphy of Zheng Wanjun, imperial son-in-law at the court of Tang Ruizong (r. 710-12). Includes a colophon of appreciation by Sun Ren (fl. 1451) dated 1471.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi qin ming
entries.description: Inscription purportedly by a certain Gu Nanya, mourning the death of his late wife, Zhuang Ning 莊甯 (zi Qingqing 清卿), and recounting how his grief led him to bury the qin she had played; a Qing forgery by a scholar of Jiangsu, probably executed for the purpose of indirectly expressing his own grief.

entries.title: Da tang ji gong song bei
titles_all: Da tang ji gong song bei
entries.description: Inscription by Tang Gaozong extolling the merit and achievement of Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49) in his capture of Dou Jiande 竇建德 (573-621); written at Dengci si, Fanshui 氾水, while the emperor was touring Wuzhou 武州 and Zhengzhou 鄭州, the site of Dou's defeat. In the emperor's script. Includes title inscription, in "flying white" script (feibaishu 飛白書).

This rubbing taken from a slightly later engraving (based on a traced copy of the original) executed in Tongzhou during the Longshuo era (661-63).

Erected by the devotees of Huiri si 慧日寺, Chang'an 長安. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qinghe zhang gong zhu Li Jing bei
titles_all: Qinghe zhang gong zhu Li Jing bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Jing, the Qinghe princess, wife of Cheng Chuliang 程處亮 and daughter of Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiguo xian fei Lu shi bei
titles_all: Jiguo xian fei Lu shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of the woman née Lu, of Luoyang 洛陽, consort of Li Shen 李慎, prince of Jiguo, tenth son of Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49); interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sangong shan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices at Sangong shan, Hebei, by a master Feng, chancellor of Changshan, in entreaty for good fortune.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiguo xian fei Lu shi bei
titles_all: Jiguo xian fei Lu shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of the woman née Lu, of Luoyang 洛陽, consort of Li Shen 李慎, prince of Jiguo, tenth son of Tang Taizong 太宗 (r. 627-49); interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chong jian Yanqing si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of Yanqing si by the priest Canhui during the Qianfeng era (666-68); the temple was originally established during the Tianbao era (550-559).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Xiaotong bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Xiaotong (606-67), general of the Left Guard and nephew of Tang Gaozu 高祖 (r. 618-26). This album also includes a handwritten colophon by Jiu Dan concerning the date of the rubbing itself.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Xiaotong bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Xiaotong (606-67), general of the Left Guard and nephew of Tang Gaozu 高祖 (r. 618-26).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Biluo bei
entries.description: The Biluo stele, a votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Tianzun 天尊 on behalf of the woman née Fang 房, great consort of Li Yuanjia 李元嘉, prince of Han 韓, by her four sons during the Xianheng era (670-74); composed by her son Li Zhuan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang A'nan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhang A'nan, palace attendant and general of the Left Gate Guard; in the calligraphy of the Buddhist priest Puchang. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ma Zhou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ma Zhou (601-48) of Gaotang, director of the Secretariat, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ma Zhou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ma Zhou (601-48) of Gaotang, director of the Secretariat, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ashina Zhong bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ashina Zhong (d. 673), a Dai 代, general-in-chief of the Right Courageous Guard, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ashina Zhong bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Ashina Zhong (d. 673), a Dai 代, general-in-chief of the Right Courageous Guard, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Ji bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Li Ji (594-669) of Huazhou 滑州, Weinan 衛南, grand preceptor of the heir apparent, posthumously titled Zhenwu, duke of Yingguo; composed by and written in the calligraphy of Tang Gaozong. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Ji bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Li Ji (594-669) of Huazhou 滑州, Weinan 衛南, grand preceptor of the heir apparent, posthumously titled Zhenwu, duke of Yingguo; composed by and written in the calligraphy of Tang Gaozong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jingshan si shi xiang ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image in stone by the great consort née Wei 韋, of Jiguo 紀國, at Jingshan si, Luoyang, sometime during the reigns of Tang Gaozong (r. 650-83) or Wu Zetian (r. 684-705).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jingshan si shi xiang ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image in stone by the great consort née Wei 韋, of Jiguo 紀國, at Jingshan si, Luoyang, sometime during the reigns of Tang Gaozong (r. 650-83) or Wu Zetian 武則天 (r. 684-705).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Zhao Shaolin si shi
entries.description: Poem composed by Wu Zetian while visiting Shaolin si with Tang Gaozong 高宗 (r. 650-83) and there viewing temple buildings that her mother, née Yang, had built.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chu Liang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chu Liang (560-647); erected by his son, Chu Suiliang 褚遂良 (596-658), aide for Puzhou 普州, during the reign of Tang Gaozong 高宗 (r. 650-83). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chu Liang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chu Liang (560-647); erected by his son, Chu Suiliang 褚遂良 (596-658), aide for Puzhou 普州, during the reign of Tang Gaozong 高宗 (r. 650-83). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ba du tan shen jun bei
titles_all: Ba du tan shen jun bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating reerection of an altar to the spirits of the eight directions by Xiao Tan, magistrate of Yuanshi, and Feng Yi 馮義, prefect of Zhaozhou 趙州, and others, and recounting instances of divine influence and intervention in the area. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Xuanzong kou shou ming
entries.description: Tomb inscription of Wang Xuanzong of Langya, as related by him, shortly before dying, to his seventh younger brother, Wang Shaozong; with a preface by Shaozong. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yisugu Shenqing bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Yisugu Shenqing (599-660), acting general of the Left Metropolitan Guard. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Xia bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Jiang Xia, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling by grace of his father's interment there; composed by Jiang's nephew Jiang Xi and written in his calligraphy. This album also includes two handwritten colophons, one of them added by Gu Zengshou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng si tan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating Wu Zetian's 武則天 (r. 684-705) visit to the sacrificial altar on Songshan, Henan, in 696 in order to perform the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices; inscription composed by Wu Sansi and written in the calligraphy of Xue Yao, by the empress's command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng si tan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating Wu Zetian's 武則天 (r. 684-705) visit to the sacrificial altar on Songshan, Henan, in 696 in order to perform the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices; inscription composed by Wu Sansi and written in the calligraphy of Xue Yao, by the empress's command. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheng xian Taizi miao bei bing yin
titles_all: Sheng xian Taizi miao bei bing yin
entries.description: After performing the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Songshan in 696, Wu Zetian conferred on Wangzi Jin 王子晉 (6th cent. B.C.) the title Shengxian taizi, "Immortal Prince," and ordered construction of a temple on the site of sacrifice; in 700 this stele was erected to commemorate the temple's completion and the prince's preferment. Composed by the empress and written in her calligraphy. Includes title inscription, in "flying white" script (feibaishu 飛白書). These albums also include rubbings of the verso of the stele, with inscriptions of personal names, including that Zhong Shaojing 鍾紹京 (fl. 707).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheng xian Taizi miao bei bing yin
titles_all: Sheng xian Taizi miao bei bing yin
entries.description: After performing the feng 封 and shan 禪 sacrifices on Songshan in 696, Wu Zetian conferred on Wangzi Jin 王子晉 (6th cent. B.C.) the title Shengxian taizi, "Immortal Prince," and ordered construction of a temple on the site of sacrifice; in 700 this stele was erected to commemorate the temple's completion and the prince's preferment. Composed by the empress and written in her calligraphy. Includes title inscription, in "flying white" script (feibaishu 飛白書). These albums also include rubbings of the verso of the stele, with inscriptions of personal names, including that Zhong Shaojing 鍾紹京 (fl. 707), and verses added during the Kangxi era.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shunling can shi
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of the woman née Yang 楊, mother of Wu Zetian 武則天 (r. 684-705), interred at Shunling; stele and gravesite prepared by the empress's command. Epitaph composed by Wu Sansi, nephew of Wu Zetian, and written in the calligraphy of Li Dan (Tang Ruizong). This album includes rubbings of the fragments found by Ma Fengzhi and a rubbing of a postscript by Ma Yuhua concerning his discovery of one of the fragments.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ci Lu Zhengdao chi
entries.description: Imperial edict by Tang Zhongzong citing the official Lu Zhengdao for his meritorious service; in the emperor's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ci Lu Zhengdao chi
entries.description: Imperial edict by Tang Zhongzong citing the official Lu Zhengdao for his meritorious service; in the emperor's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dugu Renzheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Dugu Renzheng (633-709) of Luoyang 洛陽, Henan, magistrate of Fangyu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dugu Renzheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Dugu Renzheng (633-709) of Luoyang 洛陽, Henan, magistrate of Fangyu. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Duohu jun bei
entries.description: Text of the Tago stele (one of the Kōzuke sampi 上野三碑), recording establishment of Tago district in the ancient province of Kōzuke during the Wadō era (708-15).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhougong ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription written to commemorate rainfall following prayers for rain offered by Li Jie 李傑 (d. 718), administrator of Henan, and others, at Zhougong ci, Yanshi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jingyu si Fazang chan shi ta ming
titles_all: Jingyu si Fazang chan shi ta ming
entries.description: Inscription from the stupa of Fazang of Jingyu si, master of the Chan sect.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu Zhiwei bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Yu Zhiwei (635-713) of Chang'an 長安, acting chief minister of the Court of State Ceremonial and grandson of Yu Zhining 于志寧 (588-665). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Sixun bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Li Sixun (651-716), kinsman of the Tang imperial house and general-in-chief of the Right Militant Guard; composed by Li Yong, a younger kinsman, and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Sixun bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Li Sixun (651-716), kinsman of the Tang imperial house and general-in-chief of the Right Militant Guard; composed by Li Yong, a younger kinsman, and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xingfu si can bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Wen ☐ 文☐, general-in-chief of the garrison army; composed of characters originally written by Wang Xizhi, subsequently collected and rearranged by the priest Daya 大雅 of Hongfu si 弘福寺.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi Yanzi zan
entries.description: Inscription (fragmentary) extolling Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回 (521-481 B.C.); composed by Tang Xuanzong 玄宗 and written in the emperor's calligraphy. Xuanzong ordered copies of the inscription engraved in stone in every province of the empire.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi Yanzi zan
entries.description: Inscription (fragmentary) extolling Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回 (521-481 B.C.); composed by Tang Xuanzong and written in the emperor's calligraphy. Xuanzong ordered copies of the inscription engraved in stone in every province of the empire.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi Yanzi zan
entries.description: Inscription (fragmentary) extolling Confucius and his disciple Yan Hui 顏回 (521-481 B.C.); composed by Tang Xuanzong and written in the emperor's calligraphy. Xuanzong ordered copies of the inscription engraved in stone in every province of the empire.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu shi tai jing she bei
titles_all: Yu shi tai jing she bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the establishment of a "pure abode" at the Censorate; composed by Cui Shi and copied in 723, after Shi's death, by Liang Shengqing, attendant censor. (Obverse of Hsin 724.)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yisugu Xingyan bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Yisugu Xingyan (636-707), general of the Right Militant Guard, interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu sheng song
entries.description: Inscription lauding erection of a stele on Huashan 華山, Shaanxi, by Tang Xuanzong 玄宗 (r. 712-56). Includes title inscription. This album also includes rubbings of the verso and sides of the stele, with inscriptions of personal names dating from the mid-eighth through the eleventh centuries.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dao'an chan shi bei
entries.description: Epitaph of Dao'an (d. 708) of the Chan sect, dharma master of Huishan si on Songshan, Henan. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shaolin si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the background and history of Shaolin si on Songshan, Henan, by Pei Cui; composed by Pei Cui and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lushan si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the background and history of Lushan si, Changsha. Composed by Li Yong, written in his calligraphy, and inscribed by him (adopting the name Huang Hexian).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dazhi chan shi bei
entries.description: Epitaph of the seventh patriarch of the Dongshan 東山 school of the Chan sect posthumously named Dazhi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yizhou tie xiang bei song
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha in iron by Lu Hui 盧暉; the inscription was dedicated after Lu's death by the citizens of Yi commandery in commemoration of his virtue and achievements.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tian Wan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Tian Wan, prefect of Yizhou; erected by the subordinate officials and citizens of Yizhou during the Kaiyuan era (713-41).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Jian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Tang Jian (579-656), interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling; erected by his great-grandson Tang Jin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Jian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Tang Jian (579-656), interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling; erected by his great-grandson Tang Jin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Jian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Tang Jian (579-656), interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling; erected by his great-grandson Tang Jin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Jian bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Tang Jian (579-656), interred at the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling; erected by his great-grandson Tang Jin. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan Hui song
entries.description: Inscription extolling Yan Hui (521-481 B.C.), disciple of Confucius; composed by Zhang Zhihong, magistrate of Qufu, at the order of Li Tinghui 李庭誨, supervisor-in-chief for Yanzhou 兗州, in 742. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan Hui song
entries.description: Inscription extolling Yan Hui (521-481 B.C.), disciple of Confucius; composed by Zhang Zhihong, magistrate of Qufu, at the order of Li Tinghui 李庭誨, supervisor-in-chief for Yanzhou 兗州, in 742. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan Hui song
entries.description: Inscription extolling Yan Hui (521-481 B.C.), disciple of Confucius; composed by Zhang Zhihong, magistrate of Qufu, at the order of Li Tinghui 李庭誨, supervisor-in-chief for Yanzhou 兗州, in 742. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lingyan si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the background and history of Lingyan si, Changqing, Shandong; composed by Li Yong and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lingyan si bei
entries.description: Stele inscription concerning the background and history of Lingyan si, Changqing, Shandong; composed by Li Yong and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han Shang gao hua yue wen
titles_all: Han Shang gao hua yue wen
entries.description: Inscription commemorating a vow made by Han Shang, right rectifier of omissions, to the spirit of Huashan 華山, Shaanxi, to remain upright and incorruptible and to serve emperor and state selflessly.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi tai xiao jing
entries.description: The Xiaojing, with a preface and annotations by Tang Xuanzong; in his calligraphy. The stone on which the classic is engraved was originally housed in the National University, located in the Wuben 務本 ward of the Tang capital at Chang'an 長安. First, second, and fourth (of four) portions of the inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ren Lingze bei
entries.description: Epitaph from the spirit way to the tomb of Ren Lingze (664-728) of Le'an 樂安, administrative aide to the superior area commander-in-chief; composed by Li Yong and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Duobao ta bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Cen Xun commemorating a vow made by Chujin 楚金, master of the Chan sect, to erect a stupa dedicated to the Prabhutaratna Buddha at Qianfu si, Chang'an 長安; Chujin's purpose ultimately moved Tang Xuanzong 玄宗 (r. 712-56) to help him to complete the stupa. In the calligraphy of Yan Zhenqing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dongfang Shuo hua zan bei
entries.description: Inscription by Xiahou Zhan concerning his visit to a shrine dedicated to Dongfang Shuo (154-93 B.C.) in Shentoudian, Shandong, and there viewing a portrait of the Han scholar-official; in the calligraphy of Yan Zhenqing. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dongfang Shuo hua zan bei yin
titles_all: Dongfang Shuo hua zan bei yin
entries.description: Inscription by Yan Zhenqing concerning the 754 engraving of the "Dongfang Shuo huazan" composed by Xiahou Zhan 夏侯湛 (243-91) on his visit to the shrine dedicated to Dangfang Shuo (154-93 B.C.) in Shentoudian, Shandong; in the calligraphy of Yan Zhenqing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu shi tai jing she bei yin
titles_all: Yu shi tai jing she bei yin
entries.description: Verso of the Censorate's "pure dwelling" stele (dated 724): inscription of personal names of censors, 724-56. (Verso of Hsin 403.)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huashan qi yu wen
entries.description: Inscription written to commemorate rainfall following prayers for rain offered to Huashan, Shaanxi, by Zhang Weiyi, prefect of Huazhou 華州, and Liu Jing 劉景, magistrate of Huayin, during the Qianyuan era (758-60).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guohe cheng bei
entries.description: Inscription from the Tagajō in the former province of Mutsu in northern Honshū, naming the boundaries of the castle lands; commissioned by Fujiwara no Nakamaro 藤原仲麻呂 (Emi no Oshikatsu 恵美押勝, 706-64) during the Tempyō-hōji era (757-64). This rubbing taken from a later engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guojia miao bei bing yin
entries.description: Stele inscription from the clan temple of Guo Jingzhi (667-744) of Taiyuan 太原, father of Guo Ziyi 郭子儀 (697-781); composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yiting ming bing xu
entries.description: Inscription from Yiting, built by Pei Yan 裴鶠 on an islet in the Yangzi River at Wuchang in 765. Composed by Pei Qiu and written in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing and Li Ju; the inscription has been styled "Sanjue" 三絕, for embodying the "three perfections." Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Baochen bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending Li Baochen (d. 781), military commissioner of Chengde, for his compliance and loyalty to the central government.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: San fen ji
entries.description: Tomb inscriptions of three male members of the Li clan, composed by their younger brother Li Jiqing and written in the calligraphy of their kinsman Li Yangbing during the Dali era (766-79); this rubbing taken from a Song dynasty recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: San fen ji
entries.description: Tomb inscriptions of three male members of the Li clan, composed by their younger brother Li Jiqing and written in the calligraphy of their kinsman Li Yangbing during the Dali era (766-79); this rubbing taken from a Song dynasty recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qi xian ying ji
entries.description: Inscription by Li Jiqing concerning the relocation of ancestral graves of the Li clan; in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing. This rubbing taken from a Dazhong xiangfu era (1008-16) engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qi xian ying ji
entries.description: Inscription by Li Jiqing concerning the relocation of ancestral graves of the Li clan; in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing. This rubbing taken from a Dazhong xiangfu era (1008-16) engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dazheng chan shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Dazheng of Jing'ai si, master of the Southern School of the Chan sect; composed by Wang Jin and written in the calligraphy of Xu Hao.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zang Huaike bei
entries.description: Epitaph from the spirit way to the tomb of Zang Huaike (669-724), posthumously titled chief minister of the Court of Imperial Sacrifices in recognition of his son Zang Xirang's 臧希讓 military merit; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. The stone was reerected by members of the clan in 770.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zang Huaike bei
entries.description: Epitaph from the spirit way to the tomb of Zang Huaike (669-724), posthumously titled chief minister of the Court of Imperial Sacrifices in recognition of his son Zang Xirang's 臧希讓 military merit; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. The stone was reerected by members of the clan in 770.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Magu xian tan ji
entries.description: Inscription composed by Yan Zhenqing for an altar dedicated to the immortal Magu in Nancheng, Jiangxi; in his calligraphy. This rubbing taken from a Qianlong era engraving by Guo Qiyi; includes colophons by Huang Zhaolong 黃肇隆 and Guo Qiyi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ba guan zhai hui bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing recording sponsorship of a monastic meal for Buddhist monks by Xu Xiang 徐向, prefect of Songzhou, and the civilian and military inhabitants of the prefecture, who had assembled the monks to chant for the recovery of Tian Shengong 田神功 (d. 776), then ill; in Yan's calligraphy. Includes title inscription. Originally erected in 772, the stone was reerected by Cui Zhuo 崔倬, prefect of Songzhou, in 851.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ba guan zhai hui bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing recording sponsorship of a monastic meal for Buddhist monks by Xu Xiang 徐向, prefect of Songzhou, and the civilian and military inhabitants of the prefecture, who had assembled the monks to chant for the recovery of Tian Shengong 田神功 (d. 776), then ill; in Yan's calligraphy. Includes title inscription. Originally erected in 772, the stone was reerected by Cui Zhuo 崔倬, prefect of Songzhou, in 851.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuan Jie bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Yuan Jie (zi Cishan 次山, 719-72), supervisor-in-chief for Rongzhou; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Song Jing bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Song Jing (663-737) of Guangping, right counselor-in-chief of the Department of State Affairs; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. Includes rubbings of the sides of the stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Song Jing bei ce
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Song Jing (663-737) of Guangping, right counselor-in-chief of the Department of State Affairs; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. Left and right sides of the stele only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong miao xin men ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating construction of a new gate at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Dali era (766-79). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong miao xin men ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating construction of a new gate at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Dali era (766-79). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ye Yue miao wen
entries.description: Inscription by Lu Zhaoche, newly installed magistrate of Huayin, vowing to exert himself to the utmost in the execution of his office and entreating the spirits of Huashan 華山, Shaanxi, to lend him their aid.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhongsi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wang Zhongsi (731-75) of Taiyuan 太原, former military commissioner of Hexi, Longyou, etc., and duke of Qingyuan; composed by Yuan Zai and written in the calligraphy of Wang Jin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Lüqing bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wang Lüqing (d. 776) of Chang'an 長安, military vice commissioner of Shuofang. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Lüqing bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Wang Lüqing (d. 776) of Chang'an 長安, military vice commissioner of Shuofang.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Hanguang hou bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Hanguang (hao Xuanjing 玄靖, 683-769), a Daoist priest of Maoshan; composed by Yan Zhenqing and written in his calligraphy. This rubbing includes a postscript by Shen Zhuzhou 沈竹舟 dated 1137.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xinyin ke jing ji
entries.description: Votive engraving of the Yiqie rulai xinzhenyan and an inscription concerning its dedication by the nun Xinyin of Mingjue si 明覺寺 during the Dali era (766-79).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xinyin ke jing ji
entries.description: Votive engraving of the Yiqie rulai xinzhenyan and an inscription concerning its dedication by the nun Xinyin of Mingjue si 明覺寺 during the Dali era (766-79).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huangdi ci bei
entries.description: Stele inscription from a shrine dedicated to the Yellow Emperor (Huangdi ci) on Xiandu shan, Zhejiang; in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ma Lin jia miao bei
entries.description: Stele from the clan temple of Ma Lin (d. 776) of Fufeng, military commissioner of Beiting 北庭 and Jingyuan 涇源. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan shi jia miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing commemorating construction of a clan temple on behalf of his father, Yan Weizhen 顏惟貞, and including a genealogy of the Yan clan; in Yan's calligraphy. Includes rubbings of verso and side of stele. These albums also include rubbings of colophons concerning reerection of the stele in 982; and an autograph inscription by Feng Minchang 馮敏昌 (1747-1806), dated 1787.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan shi jia miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing commemorating construction of a clan temple on behalf of his father, Yan Weizhen 顏惟貞, and including a genealogy of the Yan clan; in Yan's calligraphy. Includes rubbing of verso and sides of stele. These albums also include rubbings of colophons concerning reerection of the stele in 982 and a handwritten copy of Wang Shizhen's (1526-90) colophon to the stele (in another hand).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan shi jia miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing commemorating construction of a clan temple on behalf of his father, Yan Weizhen 顏惟貞, and including a genealogy of the Yan clan; in Yan's calligraphy. Includes rubbings of verso and sides of stele. These albums also include rubbings of colophons concerning reerection of the stele in 982; and an autograph inscription added by Feng Minchang (1747-1806) in 1787.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sangong shan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating sacrifices at Sangong shan, Hebei, by a master Feng, chancellor of Changshan, in entreaty for good fortune.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan shi jia miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Yan Zhenqing commemorating construction of a clan temple on behalf of his father, Yan Weizhen 顏惟貞, and including a genealogy of the Yan clan; in Yan's calligraphy. Sides of stele only. This album also includes a handwritten colophon, in vermilion, added by Weng Xiuguang.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Da jiao wang jing
entries.description: A portion of the Dacheng yujia jinguang xinghai Manshushili qianbei qianbo dajiaowang jing, juan 8, a sutra of the esoteric school translated by the monk Bukong 不空 (Amoghavajra) and transcribed by the monk Hyejo. This rubbing taken from the seventh stone (of juan 8) of a Jianzhong era (780-83) engraving of the sutra.

entries.title: Zhang Yanshang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhang Yanshang (727-87) of Hedong 河東, posthumously titled grand guardian.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Weiyue bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhang Weiyue (d. 775), general-in-chief of the Left Forest of Plumes Army and administrator of the army. This album also includes a handwrittem colophon added by Yuan Shizheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lingqing gong bei
entries.description: Stele inscription extolling Lingqing gong, tutelary spirit of Yanchi, Hedong; from a shrine there dedicated to the spirit.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Chuxiang bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the virtuous administration of Zheng Shu'ao 鄭叔敖 of Yingyang 滎陽, magistrate of Chengcheng; erected by imperial command. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xu Hao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Xu Hao (703-82) of Donghai, mentor to the prince of Peng. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xu Hao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Xu Hao (703-82) of Donghai, mentor to the prince of Peng. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chujin chan shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chujin of Qianfu si, master of the Chan sect; erected by the religious of the temple after imperial assent to a petition sent up by Dou Wenchang 竇文場 seeking permission to bestow on Chujin the posthumous name and title Dayuan, master of the Chan sect 大圓禪師.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chujin chan shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Chujin of Qianfu si, master of the Chan sect; erected by the religious of the temple after imperial assent to a petition sent up by Dou Wenchang 竇文場 seeking permission to bestow on Chujin the posthumous name and title Dayuan, master of the Chan sect 大圓禪師.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dou Shuxiang bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Dou Shuxiang of Xihe 西河, left auxiliary reminder. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiu chan dao chang bei
entries.description: Stele inscription recording construction of a bodhimandala on Tiantai shan by the great master Zhizhe (Zhiyi 智顗, 538-97), founder of the Tiantai sect. Includes title inscription. This rubbing taken from a Yuanhe era (806-20) engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shenxing chan shi bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of the Shenxing (Sinhaeng), Korean master of the Chan sect who traveled to Tang China in search of the dharma.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Long cheng shi ke
entries.description: Stone (fragmentary) inscribed with a poem composed by Liu Zongyuan while serving as prefect of Liuzhou. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Guangjin bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Guangjin (759-815); erected by his younger brother Li Guangyan 李光顏 (762-826), who petitioned the throne for permission to raise the stele in 820, after he had successfully subdued Huaixi 淮西. Composed by Linghu Chu and written in the calligraphy of Li Jiyuan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhu Xiaocheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhu Xiaocheng (770-820) of Sanyuan 三原, palace eunuch attendant-in-ordinary.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liang Shouqian zhu shu fo jing gong de bei
titles_all: Liang Shouqian zhu shu fo jing gong de bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the spiritual merit earned by the eunuch Liang Shouqian, on behalf of the well-being of the state, in copying out sutras at Xingtang si 興唐寺, Chang'an 長安; inscription written and erected by imperial command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Liangchen bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Liangchen (728-63), father of Li Guangyan 李光顏 (762-826). Composed by Li Zongmin and written in the calligraphy of Yang Zheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Sheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Sheng (727-93), kinsman of the imperial house of the Tang, prince of Xiping commandery, and director of the Secretariat; composed by Pei Du and written in the calligraphy of Li Gongquan, in response to imperial command, in 829.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Sheng bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Li Sheng (727-93), kinsman of the imperial house of the Tang, prince of Xiping commandery, and director of the Secretariat; composed by Pei Du and written in the calligraphy of Li Gongquan, in response to imperial command, in 829.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ayuwang si chang zhu tian bei
titles_all: Ayuwang si chang zhu tian bei
entries.description: Inscription from stele marking lands belonging to Ayuwang si. Original calligraphy by Xu Qiaozhi; redone by Fan Di at the request of Yu Jiyou, prefect of Mingzhou 明州, acting in response to the wishes of the priest Huiyin 惠印, during the Dahe era (827-35). This rubbing also includes a postscript by Yu Jiyou and a pair of poems composed by Yu and Fan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longgong si bei
entries.description: Inscription from the Longgong si stele commemorating restoration of the temple by Li Shen during the Dahe era (827-35).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longgong si bei
entries.description: Inscription from the Longgong si stele commemorating restoration of the temple by Li Shen during the Dahe era (827-35).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Longgong si bei
entries.description: Inscription (fragmentary) from the Longgong si stele commemorating restoration of the temple by Li Shen during the Dahe era (827-35).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kaicheng shi jing
entries.description: The Kaicheng stone classics, twelve texts of the Confucian canon cut in stone by command of Tang Wenzong 文宗 (r. 827-40) and erected at the School for the Sons of State in Chang'an during the Kaicheng era (836-40). This rubbing represents juan 13 (of 50) of the Yili 儀禮.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kaicheng shi jing
entries.description: The Chunqiu and Zuozhuan, juan 25 and 26; part of the Kaicheng stone classics, twelve classics of the Confucian canon copied by by Ai Jumin, Chen Jie, and Duan Jiang and engraved in stone, at the command of Tang Wenzong 文宗 (r. 827-40), between 830 and 837.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng Su bei

entries.title: Pei Yi bei e
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Pei Yi, prefect of Jinzhou; in the calligraphy of Chu Suiliang 褚遂良. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Famen si ta miao chong xiu ji
titles_all: Famen si ta miao chong xiu ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating restoration of Famen si, Fufeng, by Li Maozhen 李茂貞 (856-924), prince of Qin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guangci chan yuan ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating a change in a temple's name, from Xiangcheng chanyuan 香成禪院 to Guangci chanyuan, by imperial command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chao Gaosheng zhuan xu
entries.description: Preface to a manuscript copy of the Gaoseng zhuan of Tao Gu copied out by Zhang Zhongxun; in the calligraphy of Mengying. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Tuan shu fu shou
entries.description: Two big characters in the calligraphy of Chen Tuan: "fu" and "shou." In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xin yi Sanzang sheng jiao xu bei
titles_all: Xin yi Sanzang sheng jiao xu bei
entries.description: The "Da Song xinyi Sanzang shengjiao xu," Song Taizong's preface concerning the translations of sutras from the Tripitaka by the Kashmiri priest Tianxizai 天息災 (Devasanti, d. 1000).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhuan shu mu lu pian pang zi yuan bei
titles_all: Zhuan shu mu lu pian pang zi yuan bei
entries.description: The radicals of the Shuowen jiezi 說文解字, with a preface by Mengying and a note of appreciation by Guo Zhongshu; in the calligraphy of Mengyin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayan ta ti ming - bei men (1)

entries.description: Inscriptions of personal names dating from the Song through the Yuan dynasties, from the northern entrance to the Dayan ta at Ci'en si, Chang'an 長安.
