entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Xu ji zu zhu wen
titles_all: Kong Xu ji zu zhu wen
entries.description: Inscription of the text of the prayer read at sacrifices offered by Kong Xu, forty-fourth lineal descendant of Confucius, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Tiansheng era (1023-32).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Xu ji zu zhu wen
titles_all: Kong Xu ji zu zhu wen
entries.description: Inscription of the text of the prayer read at sacrifices offered by Kong Xu, forty-fourth lineal descendant of Confucius, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Tiansheng era (1023-32).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Daofu ji zu zhu wen
titles_all: Kong Daofu ji zu zhu wen
entries.description: Inscription of the text of the prayer read at sacrifices offered by Kong Daofu, forty-fifth lineal descendant of Confucius, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Jingyou era (1034-38).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Daofu ji zu zhu wen
titles_all: Kong Daofu ji zu zhu wen
entries.description: Inscription of the text of the prayer read at sacrifices offered by Kong Daofu, forty-fifth lineal descendant of Confucius, at the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, during the Jingyou era (1034-38).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Shihong deng ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of personal names on a stone post at the Songyang gong, Dengfeng, dating from the Huangyou era (1049-54): Zhao Shihong, Zhu Shouchen 朱壽臣, Shu Zhaoxu 舒昭叙, and Wang Shenhui 王慎徽.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wan'an qiao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating construction of the Wan'an Bridge, begun during the Huangyou era (1049-54) and completed in 1059; construction of the stone bridge was undertaken by Cai Xiang, prefect of Quanzhou 泉州, Lu Xi 盧錫, and other members of the local gentry. This rubbing represents that part of the inscription engraved on the second stone (of two) only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wan'an qiao bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating construction of the Wan'an Bridge, begun during the Huangyou era (1049-54) and completed in 1059; construction of the stone bridge was undertaken by Cai Xiang, prefect of Quanzhou, Lu Xi 盧錫, and other members of the local gentry.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wen quan zhen
entries.description: An admonition against hot springs, by Zhang Yue. The admonition, composed during the Tang dynasty, was subsequently written out in vermilion by Yang Fangping and engraved in stone by Yin Guangchen 尹光臣, magistrate of Shuo 朔, during the Zhiping era (1064-67).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Biaozhong guan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating establishment of Biaozhong guan, Qiantang, dedicated to the memory and the worship of Qian Liu 錢鏐 (852-932), king of Wu-Yue 吳越; the temple, originally a Buddhist shrine, was renamed by imperial command in response to the petition of Zhao Bian 趙抃 (1008-84), prefect of Hangzhou. Composed by and written in the calligraphy of Su Shi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu Duanqing deng ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of personal names on a stone pose at the Songyang gong, Dengfeng, dating from the Yuanfeng era (1078-85): Hao Pizhi 郝闢之 and Shu Duanqing (1038-1109).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shuang zao jia xing shi bing ba
titles_all: Shuang zao jia xing shi bing ba
entries.description: A ballad on two honey locusts, by Chen Shudu, magistrate of Lintong, Shaanxi, with a colophon by his son Chen Zhengju. The poem was composed while Chen Shudu was touring the grounds of the Huaqing gong 華清宮 during the Huangyou era (1049-54), and copied out and engraved on stone by Chen Zhengju during the Yuanfeng era (1078-85). This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on a copy by Liang Zongdao.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lü Xishu du Qingyuan bei ti ming
titles_all: Lü Xishu du Qingyuan bei ti ming
entries.description: Inscription left by Lü Xishu after viewing the Qingyuan stele during the Yuanfeng era (1078-85).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi shou zhi gui zan
entries.description: Panegyric on a juniper planted by Confucius' own hand; composed by Mi Fu and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi shou zhi gui zan
entries.description: Panegyric on a juniper planted by Confucius' own hand; composed by Mi Fu and written in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen kui ge bei
entries.description: Stele inscription by Su Shi concerning Huailian 懷璉 (also known as Dajue 大覺), master of the Chan sect, his eminence as a teacher of the dharma, and his residence at Chenggui ge; in the calligraphy of Su Shi. This rubbing taken from the Wanli (1573-1620) recarving of Cai Guiyi. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xue lang pen ming
entries.description: Inscription from the rim of Su Shi's "snow billows" basin. While serving as prefect of Dingzhou 定州 during the Shaosheng era (1094-98), Su found a rock whose venation resembled billows of snow; he had the rock worked into a basin and the lip of the basin inscribed with this short account of his find. This rubbing taken from a later recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xia Shengqiu ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of personal names on a stone post at the Songyang gong, Dengfeng, dating from 1107: Xia Shengqiu and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Ting guo Lintong shi
entries.description: Two poems by Li Ting, prefect of Suining, with a colophon by his son Li Ximin. The poems were composed while Li Ting was passing through Lintong, Shaanxi, during the Yuanyou era (1086-94), and copied out, engraved on stone, and put in place at the Wenquan xingguan 溫泉行館 by Li Ximin during the Daguan era (1107-10).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yanguang bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commending the merit and achievements of a senior official; erected by subordinate officials and local citizens during the Yanguang era (122-25).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: You Lishan shi
entries.description: A poem on Lishan, Shaanxi, by Sun Jian, fiscal vice commissioner. The poem was written by Sun while traveling through Lishan during the Zhenghe era (1111-18); the stone engraving was subsequently commissioned by Wang Shen 王慎, district magistrate of Lintong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xia Qi ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of a personal name from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: that of Xia Qi, who visited the temple during the Zhenghe era (1111-18) and left his inscription on the verso of the "Kongzi Yanzi zan" 孔子顏子讚 stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xia Qi ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of a personal name from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: that of Xia Qi, who visited the temple during the Zhenghe era (1111-18) and left his inscription on the verso of the "Kongzi Yanzi zan" 孔子顏子讚 stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xie Yan wen quan shi
entries.description: A poem on hot springs by Xie Yan, academician of the Dragon Diagram Hall, in his calligraphy; this rubbing includes an inscription by Mei Andao 梅安導, magistrate of Lintong, on erecting the stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lishan you gan shi bing ba
titles_all: Lishan you gan shi bing ba
entries.description: A poem on Lishan, Shaanxi, by Yila Lin, surveillance commissioner of Shaanxi. The poem was composed while Yila was traveling through Lishan, and subsequently engraved on stone, along with an added colophon, by former subordinate officials of Yila's.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fengliuzi ci
entries.description: A lyric to the tune "Fengliuzi," by Pusan Rubi and Pusan Liangbi; engraving commissioned by Pusan Xilu 僕散希魯, magistrate of Lintong, who added a colophon of appreciation to the stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chaoyuan guan bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating restoration of the Chaoyuan guan on Taishan, Shandong, by Yan Zhongji 嚴忠濟 (d. 1293); stele erected by his nephew Yan Du 嚴度 in 1285.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuanzheng chan shi ta ming bei
titles_all: Yuanzheng chan shi ta ming bei
entries.description: Inscription from the stupa of Yuanzheng, master of the Chan sect and successor to the dharma of the Dacaoxi school. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuanzheng chan shi bei yin
entries.description: Inscription from the stupa pf Yuanzheng, master of the Chan sect and successor to the dharma of the Dacaoxi school. Verso of stele only: inscription of personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuanzheng chan shi bei yin
entries.description: Inscription from the stupa of Yuanzheng, master of the Chan sect and successor to the dharma of the Dacaoxi school. (Fragment of rubbing of) verso of stele only: inscription of personal names.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mu'ai tang
entries.description: Horizontal inscription of the name of a hall, in the calligraphy of Zhu Xi: "Mu'ai tang." This rubbing taken from an engraving based on a copy by Yu Zijun.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi miao chong xiu bei
entries.description: Stele inscription written by Ming Xianzong on a visit to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, to view the recently completed restoration of the temple; the stele was engraved and put in place by imperial command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi miao chong xiu bei
entries.description: Stele inscription written by Ming Xianzong on a visit to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, to view the recently completed restoration of the temple; the stele was engraved and put in place by imperial command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi miao chong xiu bei
entries.description: Stele inscription written by Ming Xianzong on a visit to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, to view the recently completed restoration of the temple; the stele was engraved and put in place by imperial command.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chong jian qing zhen si ji
titles_all: Chong jian qing zhen si ji
entries.description: Stele inscription from the synagogue at Kaifeng, Henan, containing a brief historical account of Judaism, its transmission to China and propagation in Kaifeng, as well as an account of the reconstruction of the synagogue by prominent members of the congregation during the Ming dynasty. (Obverse of the "Zunchongdaojing si ji" 尊崇道經寺記 stele). Includes rubbing of top of stone, with title inscription and ornament in relief.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zun chong dao jing si ji
titles_all: Zun chong dao jing si ji
entries.description: Stele inscription from the synagogue at Kaifeng, Henan, containing a brief account of the origin and transmission of the Torah and an exhortation to hold its teachings in reverence. (Verso of the "Chongjian Qingzhen si ji" 重建清真寺記 stele.) Includes rubbing of top of stone, with title inscription and ornament in relief.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Song ri dong zheng shi Liaoan he shang gui guo xu
titles_all: Song ri dong zheng shi Liaoan he shang gui guo xu
entries.description: Valedictory, with preface, written by Wang Shouren on the occasion of the departure of the Japanese Buddhist priest Ryōan (Ryōan Keide 了庵桂梧, 1425-1514?) for Japan from China, where he had gone in search of the dharma; in Wang's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shou xuan bang shu
entries.description: Horizontal inscription of the name of a hall, written in the calligraphy of Zhu Chenglin, prince of Zhuangxi 莊僖, and inscribed in stone by his son Zhu Bingtang, prince of Yongshou 永壽: "Shouxuan."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qian gua bei
entries.description: The hexagram Qian, with accompanying text, from the Yijing 易經; in the calligraphy of Li Yangbing. This rubbing taken from an engraving based on a copy by Hu Zuanzong and erected by Sima Tai during the Jiajing era (1522-66). This album also includes rubbings of postscripts added by Hu Zuanzong and Sima Tai, both dated 1537.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fengle ting ji
entries.description: The "Fengle ting ji" of Ouyang Xiu, in the calligraphy of Su Shi. The text, composed in 1046, was copied out by Su Shi during the Yuanyou era (1086-94); this rubbing taken from a Jiajing era (1522-66) recarving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zuiweng ting ji
entries.description: The "Zuiweng ting ji" of Ouyang Xiu, in the calligraphy of Su Shi. The text, composed in 1048, was copied out by Su Shi during the Yuanyou era (1086-94); this rubbing taken from a Jiaqing era (1522-66) engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhou Chu miao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Zhou Chu (240-99); composed by Lu Ji and originally written in the calligraphy of Wang Xizhi. This rubbing taken from a Ming engraving based on a traced copy by Zhou Tianxia. This rubbing also includes postscripts added by Wan Shihe (1516-86) and Wu Yu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Chongguang shen dao bei (Zhongqin tang ji gu tie zhi yi)
titles_all: Wang Chongguang shen dao bei (Zhongqin tang ji gu tie zhi yi)
entries.description: Epitaph from the spirit way to the tomb of Wang Chongguang (1502-58); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. This set of rubbings also includes colophons added by Wang Xiangqian and Han Qushan 韓取善.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Emei shan Puxian dian bei
entries.description: Stele inscription commemorating the establishment of the Puxian dian on Emei shan by Wang ☐, prefect of Sichuan, and others, during the Wanli era (1573-1620); composed of characters originally written by Chu Suiliang, and subsequently collected and rearranged by Wu Shiduan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si bei yin ji
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan. Verso of stele only; largely illegible.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si bei e
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si bei e yin
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan. Verso of title inscription only, without text.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si can ta
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan. This rubbing is of a fragment only, without text; what part of the stele it represents is difficult to determine.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si bei
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan; largely illegible.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayuanjue si bei e
entries.description: Stele from the Dayuanjue si in Kunming, Yunnan. Title inscription only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chaoxian guo Piao □ can bei
entries.description: Epitaph (fragmentary) from the spirit way to the tomb of Pak ☐. Dry rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taigu si bei ting ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating construction of a pavilion for housing stelae at T'aegosa, by the emperor of Korea, sometime during the Chinese Ming dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huaqing chi shi bing xu
entries.description: A poem on the Huaqing chi in Lintong, Shaanxi, with a preface, by Luo Sen; in his calligraphy. The poem was composed while Luo Sen was touring in vicinity of the pool during the Kangxi era (1662-1722).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ci Wu He shi liang ze
titles_all: Ci Wu He shi liang ze
entries.description: Two poems, composed by the Kangxi emperor and written in the emperor's calligraphy, in the style of Mi Fu 米芾 (1051-1107); presented to Wu He. This rubbing preserves only the latter half of one poem.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kai tu ming ji
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: the Chinese pistache planted over Confucius' grave by Zigong 子貢, with an inscription by an unnamed Confucian scholar who visited the temple in 1667 and was moved by the sight of the tree.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jin Gui Huaqing ci shi
entries.description: A poem on the Huaqing chi, by Jin Gui, military supervisor (dushi 督師) of Tongguan 潼關, in his calligraphy; composed and engraved on stone by Jin during a visit to the pool during the Qianlong era (1736-95).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ye Konglin lei jiu si
entries.description: Verse written by the Qianlong emperor on a visit to the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong, in 1748: one poem, "Ye Konglin lei jiu."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

titles_all: Sun Zhongshan shou zhong di ji nian shi
entries.description: Dry (wax) rubbing of gold-lettered inscription in marble marking the site where Dr. Sun Yat-sen 孫逸仙 (1866-1925) died, in the home of Wellington Koo 顧維鈞 (1888-1985), in Tieshizi hutong 鐵獅子胡同, Beiping. The inscription was put in place in 1928, the year Sun Yat-sen's body was removed from Beiping to Nanjing.

entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a grotto temple and an image of the Buddha in stone by the mendicant Huicheng--the temple on behalf of the state, and the image on behalf of his father, Yuan Shiping 元始平, regional inspector of Luozhou 洛州--at Longmen during the Taihe era (477-99). Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huigan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the mendicant Huigan, on behalf of his late parents, at Longmen during the Jingming era (500-503). The last two lines of text include a votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the mendicant Faning 法寧 on behalf of his late parents.

entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of Maitreya and Sakyamuni by the mendicant Huichang, on behalf of the emperor and empress and others, at Longmen during the Zhengguang era (520-25). Includes rubbing of one image.

entries.title: Faling zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of Sakyamuni and an inscription concerning its dedication by the mendicant nun Faling, on behalf of her daughter, née Yu 于, of Anle 安樂 commandery, at Longmen during the Zhengguang era (520-25). Includes rubbing of two figures, possibly the donor and her daughter.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cao Wangxi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Cao Wangxi, on behalf of the state, himself, his family and household dependents, during the Zhengguang era (520-25). This rubbing also includes impressions of reliefs carved on the three sides of the base; in vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Da nie pan jing ji
entries.description: The Daniebanjing gatha engraved on stone during the Xiaochang era (525-27). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Rong Jiuzhou zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Rong Jiuzhou, director of the Left Storehouse, at Longmen during the Xiaochang era (525-27).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daoshi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the mendicants Daoshi, Sengshou 僧授, Sengxu 僧緒, and Huijun 惠儁, at Longmen during the Xiaochang era (525-27). Includes pictorial relief of lions and donor figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fayi deng yi bai yu ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Fayi deng yi bai yu ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a grotto temple by Fayi and fellow priests--over a hundred in all--on behalf of the emperor and their own spiritual teachers, parents, and families, during the Xiaochang era (525-27).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fayi deng sa wu ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Fayi deng sa wu ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Fayi and others--some thirty-five in all--during the Xiaochang era (525-27). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Hongcai zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Zhao Hongcai zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image by the devotee Zhao Hongcai during the Datong era (527-29). Includes rubbing of an image of two standing figures and an ox.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Du wei na Song Anjin deng gong yang tu xiang
titles_all: Du wei na Song Anjin deng gong yang tu xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: two rows of donors, including the chief Buddhist deacon Song Anjin, with attendants. The name of each donor appears in a cartouche beside the figure.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Senghuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the lay devotee Wang Senghuan of Chang'an 長安, on behalf of the imperial throne, his spiritual teachers, and all sentient life, during the Jianyi era (528). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Jun zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of Sakyamuni and two bodhisattvas and an inscription concerning its dedication by Liu Jun, regional inspector of Huizhou 徽州, on behalf of the emperor, the local magistrates, his own spiritual teachers, and his parents, during the Putai era (531-32). In vermillion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fayi deng nian yu ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Fayi deng nian yu ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Fayi and others--over twenty in all--on behalf of the emperor and all things in the dharma-realm, at Longmen during the Yongxi era (532-34).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Taoshu zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Zheng Taoshu, river-crossing general (lingjiang jiangjun 陵江將軍), on behalf of his parents and household dependents, during the Yongxi era (532-34).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Youcheng zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Wang Youcheng zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Wang Youcheng during the Datong era (527-29). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daojiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of six images of the Buddha by the mendicant Daojiang, on behalf of the continued prosperity of the empire, his spiritual teachers and fellow priests, and his own parents, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daojiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of six images of the Buddha by the mendicant Daojiang, on behalf of the continued prosperity of the empire, his spiritual teachers and fellow priests, and his own parents, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Senghui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni and the Prabhutaratna Buddha by the mendicant nun Senghui, on behalf of her late mother, Huihao 惠好, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei. Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Senghui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni and the Prabhutaratna Buddha by the mendicant nun Senghui, on behalf of her late mother, Huihao 惠好, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei. Includes rubbing of image. This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dong Sengzhi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Dong Sengzhi, on behalf of his late daughter Meizu 媚足, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wei wang fu mu zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wei wang fu mu zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin, on behalf of the donor's late parents and late younger brother, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Qiangui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the (female) lay devotee Li Qiangui at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wen Lingci zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Wen Lingci, on behalf of Zhang Siyi 張思宜, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Baosheng zoa xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the (female) lay devotee Yang Baosheng, on behalf of Kuduohanwang 庫多汗王, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Baosheng zoa xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the (female) lay devotee Yang Baosheng at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Faxiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by Zhang Faxiang, on behalf of the donor's late elder brother, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Asi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Zhao Asi at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daoyao zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the nun Daoyao, possibly at Longmen, possibly during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Huixiang deng san ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Zhang Huixiang deng san ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Zhang Huixiang, ☐ Beinan ☐備男, and the lay devotee Caihua 採花 at Longmen during the Eastern Wei. Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Luo Layue deng shi ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Luo Layue deng shi ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by ten people--Luo Layue, Li Yuan 李垣, Liu Dingdu 劉定都, Fu Lingxiu 傅令秀, Bai ☐☐ 白☐☐, Zhang Muhua 張母化, Luo Keni 羅可倪, Liu Tiansheng 劉天生, Xing Tianbao 興天保, and Bai Xxing 白?興--at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sengli deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Infinite Life by the mendicants Sengli and Senggong 僧恭, on behalf of all sentient life, during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Poluomen zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Wang Poluomen (Brahman Wang?), on behalf of his late mother, during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Zhen zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Zhao Zhen, general of strong crossbowmen (qiangnu jiangjun 強弩將軍), on behalf of seven generations of forebears, during the Eastern Wei. This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Su Wancheng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Su Wancheng, general who pacifies the east (pingdong jiangjun 平東將軍), on behalf of his father, at Longmen during the Datong era (535-56).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhangsun Sengji deng san ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Zhangsun Sengji deng san ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Zhangsun Sengji, Zhangsun Sengyuan 長孫僧願, and the mendicant Fashou 法壽, on behalf of their own well-being, at Longmen during the Eastern Wei.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Hongbao zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of the Buddha and the bodhisattvas and the construction of a temple by the priest Hongbao of Wusheng si 務聖寺, on behalf of his patron, Zhang Fashou 張法壽, and his patron's sons, Zhang Rongqian 張榮遷 and Zhang Xiuhe 張脩和, who donated the stone for the images and the land for the temple, during the Tianping era (543-37). Includes rubbing of one image, probably from one face (of four) of the ornamental stone pillar (chuang 幢) on which the reliefs were originally carved.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Shenzong deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wang Shenzong deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of niches containing images of the Buddha by Wang Shenzong and Ju Zhenluo 車枕洛 during the Datong era (535-56). Includes rubbings of shrines and images.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Seng □ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of sacred images by the monk ☐, on behalf of his late spiritual teacher Yun 雲, during the Tianping era (534-37). Includes rubbing of three sacred images and the figure of a monk.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tanhui deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the mendicant nuns Tanhui and Arong 阿容, on behalf of their own well-being, their spiritual teachers, and their families, at Longmen during the Tianping era (534-37).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tanhui deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the mendicant nuns Tanshui and Arong 阿容, on behalf of their own well-being, their spiritual teachers, and their families, at Longmen during the Tianping era (534-37). Includes rubbing of two (donor?) figures flanking text, and two lions surmounting it.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sun Sixiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by Sun Sixiang, on behalf of the donor's late son, at Longmen during the Tianping era (534-37).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Jingzun zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Yao Jingzun, wave-subduing general (fubo jiangjun 伏波將軍) and aide for Wei commandery, on behalf of seven generations of forebears, at Qianfo shan, Shandong, during the Yuanxiang era (538-39).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Su Wancheng qi Zhao Man zao shi ku bing xiang ji
titles_all: Su Wancheng qi Zhao Man zao shi ku bing xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a grotto temple and a sacred image by Zhao Man, wife of Su Wancheng, general who pacifies the east (pingdong jiangjun 平東將軍) at Longmen during the Datong era (535-51).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daoshan zao xiang
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the mendicant priest Daoshan, on behalf of the ruler and others, during the Xinghe era (539-42). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Ming zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the devotee Liu Ming, on behalf of his late parents, during the Wuding era (543-50). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tanjing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the mendicant Tanjing, on behalf of Datong si 大統寺, at Longmen during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tanjing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the mendicant Tanjing, on behalf of Anfa 安法, abbot of Datong si 大統寺, at Longmen during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huiying zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the priest Huiying, on behalf of his late parents and other priests, during the Zhongdatong era (546). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Youren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of the Buddha and an inscription concerning its dedication by Li Youren during the Taiqing era (547-49). Includes rubbing of image of the Buddha astride an animal. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
titles_all: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa and an image of the Buddha by Yao Baoyuan during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
titles_all: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa and an image of the Buddha by Yao Baoyuan during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
titles_all: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa and an image of the Buddha by Yao Baoyuan during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
titles_all: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa and an image of the Buddha by Yao Baoyuan during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
titles_all: Yao Baoyuan zao xiang ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stupa and an image of the Buddha by Yao Baoyuan during the Wuding era (543-50).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daobin zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Daobin, abbot of Longshan si 龍山寺, Wu'an 武安, during the Wuding era (543-50). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Luozu deng shi wu ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wu Luozu deng shi wu ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of Sakyamuni and Maitreya by Wu Luozu and fourteen others during the Northern Qi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huijian zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the mendicant Huijian at Longmen during the Northern Qi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Wenxiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the devotee Li Wenxiang during the Tianbao era (550-59). Includes rubbings of image, front and back.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yang wei Xing Duo deng zao fo xiang ji
titles_all: Gao Yang wei Xing Duo deng zao fo xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Gao Yang, Wenxuandi 文宣帝 of the Northern Qi (r. 550-59), on behalf of Xing Duo and forty-nine others, during the Tianbao era (550-59). Includes rubbing of lions and squatting figure.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhihui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee Wang Zhihui, on behalf of his late parents, during the Tianbao era (550-59). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Zhiyun zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by the devotee Li Zhiyun, on behalf of the emperor and all sentient life, during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Zhenming deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Gao Zhenming deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of Sakyamuni and an inscription concerning its dedication by the lay devotees Gao Zhenming, Wang Dianrong 王殿榮, and Shi Chenglin 史成林, on behalf of the emperor, their own parents, and others, during the Tianbao era (550-59); a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Qing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Li Qing, on behalf of Li Xian 李憲 of Zhao 趙 commandery and his son Li Xizong 李希宗 and in gratitude to them for their kindness to him, during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cui Chang deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Cui Chang deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Cui Chang and his wife, purportedly at Luoyang during the Tianbao era (550-59); above the inscription, a pictorial relief, the nature of which is difficult to determine. A forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tianbao ba nian zao xiang ji
titles_all: Tianbao ba nian zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by an unidentified donor in 557.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jingming zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha and the dedication of a stupa by Jingming, disciple of Chan master Zhi 智, during the Tianbao era (550-59). This rubbing includes inscriptions on the sides as well as the front of the stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Bei zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Liu Bei of Hejian 河間 during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Bei zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Liu Bei of Hejian 河間 during the Tianbao era (550-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhou Wenyou zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Infinite Life by Zhou Wenyou, on behalf of all Buddhist devotees, at Dazhuangyan si 大莊嚴寺, during the Yongding era (557-59); a forgery. Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Mengmu zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee Lu Mengmu, on behalf of seven generations of forebears, during the Wucheng era (559-60).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Xiang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee Wang Xiang, on behalf of seven generations of forebears, during the Wucheng era (559-60). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Seng fa shi ji Wang shi dao su yi bai ren zao xiang ji
titles_all: Seng fa shi ji Wang shi dao su yi bai ren zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni, Manjusri, and Vimalakirti in white jade by members of the Wang clan, monks, and laymen--a hundred in all, led by Seng, the great mendicant and master of the Chen sect--during the Heqing era (562-65).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Yanchang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Wang Yanchang and others during the Northern Qi. This rubbing includes a blank spot where the images were presumably located.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Tanjing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Yang Tanjing, on behalf of his parents and grandparents, brothers and sisters, during the Baoding era (561-65). Includes rubbing of the lower portion of the image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Zuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Laojun 老君 by Jiang Zuan, on behalf of his late son, Yuanlüe 元略, during the Tiantong era (565-69).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Zuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Laojun 老君 by Jiang Zuan, on behalf of his late son, Yuanlüe 元略, during the Tiantong era (565-69).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Zuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Laojun 老君 by Jiang Zuan, on behalf of his late son, Yuanlüe 元略, during the Tiantong era (565-69).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Zuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Laojun 老君 by Jiang Zuan, on behalf of his late son, Yuanlüe 元略, during the Tiantong era (565-69). Includes rubbing of top of stone, with entwined dragons and cartouche for title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang Zuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Laojun 老君 by Jiang Zuan, on behalf of his late son, Yuanlüe 元略, during the Tiantong era (565-69).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji Sengzi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by Ji Sengzi, on behalf of himself and his family, during the Tiantong era (565-69). This rubbing includes a postscript added by Weng Fanggang (1733-1818). This leaf also includes an autograph inscription added by Zhao Zhiqian (1829-84).

entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the wife of Wang Lun 王倫, Chen Nüpo, together with their son, at Longmen during the Tang dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu shi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee née Liu at Longmen during the Tang dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng Huangyi deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Feng Huangyi deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Inscription of personal names of donors of votive images at Huata si, Xianning, dating from the Tang dynasty: Feng Huangyi, palace eunuch attendant-in-ordinary, and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: □ Hongdao zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya and two bodhisattvas by the devotee ☐ Hongdao (Qiangdao 強道?), on behalf of his late mother, during the Tang dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Meng Erniang deng zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Meng Erniang deng zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of the Buddha by Meng Erniang and three others at Longmen during the Tang dynasty. Includes rubbing of three images.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yan Jingming deng zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Yan Jingming deng zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of the Buddha by Yan Jingming and one other donor at Longmen during the Tang dynasty. Includes rubbing of three images.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Xing□ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the devotee Yang Xing☐, on behalf of his mother, née Liu 劉, at Longmen during the Yonghui era (650-55).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhang shi zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wang Zhang shi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the woman née Zhang, wife of Wang Baoying 王寶英, on behalf of her late daughter, Youxiang 有相, during the Yonghui era (650-55).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Ning zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by Zhao Ning, on behalf of himself and the dharma-realm, at Longmen during the Yonghui era (650-55).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang can zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni at Longmen during the Yonghui era (650-55).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lei Dacen zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee Lei Dacen, on behalf of the emperor, his own parents and family members, and all sentient life, during the Longshuo era (661-63); possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: An Taiqing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by An Taiqing, palace woman of the prince of Jiang 蔣, on behalf of the emperor and empress, the princes, and her own parents and spiritual teachers, at Xiangtang shan (North) during the Longshuo era (661-63).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Su Baocai zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin in stone by Su Baocai and his wife, née Liu 劉, on behalf of the emperor and empress, during the Linde era (664-65).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sun Wencai zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stone stele engraved with sacred images by Sun Wencai, commandant tending the western frontier, on behalf of the emperor and all sentient life, during the Linde era (664-65).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang shi nü deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Zhang shi nü deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by the ladies née Zhang and Zhao 趙, on behalf of one Yandao 彥道, during the Zongzhang era (668-70). Includes rubbings of images.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gu □ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by the devotee Gu ☐ at Longmen during the Zongzhang era (668-70).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Niu Zhang shi zao xiang ji
titles_all: Niu Zhang shi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha and two bodhisattvas by the woman née Zhang, mother of Niu Mi 牛密, assistant magistrate of Boxiang 柏鄉, during the Xianheng era (670-74).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Yuanyi zao shi ta ji
titles_all: Li Yuanyi zao shi ta ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a reliquary by Li Yuanyi, member of the imperial clan through the branch of the later prince of Zheng 鄭, at Faxing si, Luzhou, during the Xianheng era (670-74); composed by the priest Hongman.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Renke zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha and two bodhisattvas by Wang Renke, on behalf of his eldest daughter, at Longmen during the Shangyuan era (674-76 or 760-61).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhou Yuanzhi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Zhou Yuanzhi, court gentleman for manifesting rightness, on behalf of the emperor and empress, the heir apparent and the princes, and his own parents and spiritual teachers, at Longmen during the Shangyuan era (674-76).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Wantong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by Li Wantong and his wife, née Xu 徐, on behalf of their late parents and the emperor and empress, during the Yifeng era (676-79).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ma Junqi zao fu tu ji
titles_all: Ma Junqi zao fu tu ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a stone stupa by Ma Junqi of Wuyi 武邑, on behalf of his late parents, during the Yifeng era (676-79).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daman zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by the priest Daman of Chanfa si 闡法寺, on behalf of the emperor and empress, at Longmen during the Yonglong era (680-81).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Siyuan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Wang Siyuan, on behalf of his late younger brother Sishen 思慎, during the Yonglong era (680-81).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiang Sanniang juan xin jing bing zao xiang ji
titles_all: Qiang Sanniang juan xin jing bing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an inscription of the Heart Sutra and images of Maitreya, Guanyin, and the bodhisattva Dizang 地藏 by the devotee Qiang Sanniang of Haozhi 好畤, Yongzhou 雍州, on behalf of her late husband, father, mother-in-law, and family members, during the Yonglong era (680-81).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cheng Ren'gan zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by Cheng Ren'gan of Shanzhou 陝州, court gentleman for comprehensive duty and secretary of the Bureau of Evaluations in the Pavilion of Culture and Prosperity (i.e., Department of State Affairs), at Longmen during the Tianshou era (690-92).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Du Wenqiang deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Du Wenqiang deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Medicine 藥師 by Du Wenqiang and others, on behalf of the empress Wu Zetian 武則天 (r. 684-705), their wives, and seven generations of their forebears, during the Tianshou era (690-92). Includes rubbing of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Du Wenqiang deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Du Wenqiang deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Medicine 藥師 by Du Wenqiang and others, on behalf of the empress Wu Zetian 武則天 (r. 684-705), their wives, and seven generations of their forebears, during the Tianshou era (690-92). Sides and back of the base holding the image (Tsa 125 (7)-13) only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Erniang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha and two bodhisattvas by the mendicant nun Erniang, on behalf of her parents, at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Zhengsheng era (695).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Erniang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha and two bodhisattvas by the mendicant nun Erniang, on behalf of her parents, at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Zhengsheng era (695).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tian Cui shi zao xiang ji
titles_all: Tian Cui shi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the woman née Cui, wife of the devotee Tian Shenjian 田神鑒 of Pingyang 平陽, at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Zhengsheng era (695).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang □ fu tu ming
entries.description: Inscription from the stupa of Zhang ☐; stupa erected by his son Zhang Weishou 張魏收 and others during the Zhengsheng era (695).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wan Dao'an zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by the devotee Wan Dao'an, on behalf of the ruler and his own parents and spiritual teachers, at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Wansui tongtian era (969-697).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng Shanguo zao fu tu ming
titles_all: Feng Shanguo zao fu tu ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha and a stupa by the devotee Feng Shanguo and his brothers, on behalf of their father, Feng Jing 馮敬, and their mother, née Shen 沈, during the Wansui tongtian era (696-97). Includes a niche-shaped blank where an image was presumably located.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Linghu Sheng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Medicine 藥師 by Linghu Sheng, on behalf of his late elder brother, at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Shengli era (698-700).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Chongzi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the bodhisattva Guanyin by Gao Chongzi at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Shengli era (698-700).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pang Laoqi □ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Pang Laoqi, on behalf of his late daughter Daniang 大娘, during the Dazu era (701).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xuangong mu zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by the mother of ☐ Xuangong at Xiangtang shan (South), Hebei, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yangui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Gao Yangui of Bohai 渤海, at Huata si, Xianning, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wei Jun zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Wei Jun, assistant magistrate of Fuping 富平, in fulfillment of a vow made for the restoration of his mother's good health, at Huata si, Xianning, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Chengsi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha by Li Chengsi of Longxi 隴西, on behalf of his venerable parents, at Huata si, Xianning, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiao Yuanshen zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of images of Maitreya and two bodhisattvas by Xiao Yuanshen, magistrate of Yangzi 揚子, on behalf of his ancestors and all sentient life, at Huata si, Xianning, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Yuanzhi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Yao Yuanzhi, acting administrator of the establishment of the prince of Xiang 相, on behalf of the ruler and his own parents, at Huata si, Xianning, during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Hui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Chen Hui, on behalf of seven generation of forebears, at Longmen during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Jianchang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Sakyamuni by the devotee Gao Jianchang at Longmen during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han Jisheng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Han Jisheng at Longmen during the Chang'an era (701-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Yuanjing zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Yao Yuanjing, aide to the chief minister of the Court of the National Granaries, on behalf of all sentient life, at Guangzhai si, Xianning; inscribed on a column in the temple's dharma hall.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Nandi deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Zhang Nandi deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Amitabha and two bodhisattvas by Zhang Nandi and his brothers, on behalf of their late mother, during the Jingyun era (710-11).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi gong shi xiang ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image in stone by the district mistress of Xiangyi 襄邑, on behalf of her husband, surnamed Shi, pillar of state, during the Yanhe era (712).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of a bodhisattva (or bodhisattvas) by the eunuch Yang Sixu (d. 740), on behalf of his late mother, at Longmen during the Kaiyuan era (713-41); in the calligraphy of Xu Hao 徐浩.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji ming
titles_all: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Yang Sixu (d. 740), palace attendant and duke of Guoguo 虢國, for the Yanghua tai 楊花臺 at Huata si, Xi'an, during the Kaiyuan era (713-41).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji xu
titles_all: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji xu
entries.description: Preface to a votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Yang Sixu (d. 740), palace attendant and duke of Guoguo, for the Yanghua tai at Huata si, Xi'an, during the Kaiyuan era (713-41).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji ming
titles_all: Yang Sixu zao xiang ji ming
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by Yang Sixu (d. 740), palace attendant and duke of Guoguo, for the Yanghua tai at Huata si, Xi'an, during the Kaiyuan era (713-41).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ruwei zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the bodhisattva Guanyin by the mendicant Ruwei, on behalf of his own well-being, during the Tang dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yichu deng ren ti ming
entries.description: Inscription of personal names from Foyu 佛峪, Shandong, left by the ascetic Yichu and other while traveling through the area during the Dazhong era 847-60).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu Qi zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin by Yu Qi, palace servitor in the Clerk's Office of the Palace Domestic Service, at Longmen during the Song dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wei □ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the lay devotee Wei ☐, on behalf of his parents and all sentient life.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: □ Hui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscriptions concerning dedication of sacred images by the clan of ☐ Hui.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xin jing
entries.description: The Heart Sutra, engraved on stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: □ Hui zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by ☐ Hui, on behalf of his late father, at Longmen.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription with a figure of a seated person; date, provenance, and identification of donor unknown. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng Waibin zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha of Medicine by Feng Waibin, administrative supervisor of Zhennan. Includes rubbing of image. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Honglüe zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Zhang Honglüe. Includes rubbing of image. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Yongshou deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Guo Yongshou deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by the Buddhist deacon Guo Yongshou and six other donors, on behalf of their parents. Includes rubbing of seven donor figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Kun deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wang Kun deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of Guanyin, savior from suffering. (For inscription concerning dedication of this image, see Tsa 125 (7)-16.2.)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Kun deng zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wang Kun deng zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Guanyin, savior from suffering (Tsa 125 (7)-16.1), by Wang Kun and members of his family.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng Liang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by the devotee Feng Liang during the Yongping era (508-12).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi qi tie
entries.description: The "Shiqi tie" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a Zhenguan era (627-49) engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi qi tie
entries.description: The "Shiqi tie" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from Xing Ziyuan's engraving of the Hongwen guan 弘文館 edition dating to the Zhenguan era (627-49).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi qi tie
entries.description: The "Shiqi tie" of Wang Xizhi, with transcription; this rubbing taken from a later engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from an engraving of Chu Suiliang's copy of the preface. This album also includes postscripts dated 1026 and 1088, and colophons added by Mi Youren (1086-1165) and Mi Fu (1051-1107).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu (Dongyang ben)
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a Zhenguan era (627-49) engraving of Ouyang Xun's copy of the preface. Engraving unearthed during the Zhengtong era (1436-49). This album also includes Liu Mingyu's (fl. 1747) handwritten transcriptions of colophons by Zhang Yuanbian (1538-88) and He Shiying (fl. 1436).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a Zhenguan era (627-49) engraving of Ouyang Xun's copy of the preface. Engraving unearthed during the Zhengtong era (1436-49). This rubbing includes the inscription "Yibai tang," said to have been added to the engraving sometime between the Qianlong and Jiaqing eras (1736-1820).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Zixhi; this rubbing taken from an engraving of a Tang dynasty copy of the preface. This album also includes a handwritten colophon purportedly by Dong Qichang (1555-1636) and dating to 1630.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a Song engraving of a Tang copy of the Dingwu version of the preface. This rubbing also includes a handwritten postscript by Yang Shoujing (1839-1915), in vermilion ink, concerning the edition of the engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu (Kaihuang ben)
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from Wang Yangdu's 王養度 engraving of a Kaihuang era (581-600) inscription of the preface. This album also includes rubbings of colophons by Dong Qichang 董其昌 (1555-1636) and Wang Yangdu (dated 1836). This engraving is considered by some to be a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from Feng Fang's (1523 進士 ) engraving of a Shenlong era (705-7) inscription of the preface. This rubbing also includes the 1076 autograph of Xu Jiang 許將 (1016-90), possibly cut from the Dingwu engraving of the preface and added here.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from Feng Fang's (1523 進士 ) engraving of the Shenlong era (705-7) inscription of the preface. This rubbing also includes the 1076 autograph of Xu Jiang (1016-90).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from Feng Fang's (1523 進士 ) engraving of a Tang copy of the Shenlong (705-7) version of the preface. This rubbing also includes the 1076 autograph of Xu Jiang (1016-90).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lanting xu
entries.description: The "Lanting xu" of Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from (a later copy of?) an engraving of a Tang dynasty copy of the preface unearthed during the Jiajing era (1522-66).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yue Yi lun
entries.description: The "Yueh Yi lun" of Xiahou Sitai, in Chu Suiliang's copy of Wang Xizhi's original calligraphy; this rubbing taken from a later recarving of a copy of the (Song dynasty) Baojin zhai fatie version of the inscription. This rubbing also includes an autograph (dated 349) and two colophons, one possibly added by Mi Fu (1051-1107).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yue Yi lun
entries.description: The "Yueh Yi lun" of Xiahou Sitai, in Chu Suiliang's copy of Wang Xizhi's original calligraphy; this rubbing taken from a later recarving of a copy of the (Song dynasty) Baojin zhai fatie version of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang ting jing
entries.description: The Huangting jing, in calligraphy traditionally ascribed to Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a later copy of the (Song dynasty) Shuihen engraving of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang ting jing
entries.description: The Huangting jing, in calligraphy traditionally ascribed to Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from the (Song dynasty) Sigu zhai engraving of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang ting jing
