entries.description: The Huangting jing, in calligraphy traditionally ascribed to Wang Xizhi; this rubbing taken from a later copy of the (Song dynasty) Shuihen engraving of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Cao E bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Cao E (d. 143) of Shangyu 上虞, who drowned while searching for her father's corpse; in calligraphy traditionally ascribed to Wang Xizhi. This rubbing possibly taken from the (Song dynasty) Baojin zhai engraving of the inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo yi jiao jing
entries.description: The Foyijiao jing; in calligraphy traditionally attributed to Wang Xizhi, but now generally considered to be the work of Tang dynasty hand. This album also includes three handwritten colophons, including one purportedly by Xing Tong (1551-1612). A forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Luoshen fu shi san hang
entries.description: The "Shisan hang," thirteen lines of Cao Zhi's "Luoshen fu" in the calligraphy of Wang Xianzhi. This album includes two rubbings of the inscription, both taken from a copy of the (Song dynasty) engraving by Jia Sidao (d. 1275).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Luoshen fu shi san hang
entries.description: The "Shisan hang," thirteen lines of Cao Zhi's "Luoshen fu" in the calligraphy of Wang Xianzhi; this rubbing taken from the engraving of the inscription uncovered at West Lake, Hangzhou, during the Wanli era (1573-1620). This album also includes colophons by Yu Qian 于謙 (1398-1457) and others. A printed reproduction.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Luoshen fu shi san hang
entries.description: The "Shisan hang," thirteen lines of Cao Zhi's "Luoshen fu" in the calligraphy of Wang Xianzhi; this album includes two rubbings of the inscription, both taken from the recarving of the engraving uncovered at West Lake, Hangzhou, during the Wanli era (1573-1620).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhiyong qian zi wen
entries.description: The Qianzi wen of Zhou Xingsi, originally in the calligraphy of the dharma master Zhiyong; with a colophon by Xue Sichang 薛嗣昌. This rubbing taken from a Song engraving based on a copy of the classic in the collection of the Cui 崔 clan of Chang'an 長安.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhiyong qian zi wen
entries.description: The Qianzi wen of Zhou Xingsi, originally in the calligraphy of the dharma master Zhiyong; with a colophon by Xue Sichang 薛嗣昌. This rubbing taken from a Song engraving based on a copy of the classic in the collection of the Cui 崔 clan of Chang'an 長安.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ku shu fu
entries.description: "Kushu fu" by Yu Xin, in the calligraphy of Chu Suiliang; this rubbing taken from a later engraving based on a copy of the fu originally in the collection of Shi Ming 史明 of Wujiang. This album also includes rubbings of two colophons dating to the Song dynasty and a handwritten note by Yang Shoujing (1839-1915) concerning the edition of the engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Taizong ai ce
entries.description: Funeral eulogy for Tang Taizong (r. 627-49), in the calligraphy of Chu Suiliang; this rubbing taken from a later engraving, based on a copy of the eulogy originally in the collection of Shi Ming of Wujiang.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Yong tie
entries.description: A letter by Li Yong, along with the epitaph of Li Sixun (651-716), both in Li's calligraphy; fragmentary. These rubbings taken from a later engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ling fei jing
entries.description: The Lingfei jing, in the calligraphy of Zhong Shaojing; this rubbing taken from a Ming dynasty engraving. This album also includes rubbings of two colophons by Dong Qichang (1555-1636).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji bo fu wen
entries.description: The "Ji bofu wen," Yan Zhenqing's elegy for his uncle, Yan Yuansun 顔元孫, former prefect of Haozhou; this rubbing taken from a later engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng zuo wei tie
entries.description: The "Zheng zuowei" of Yan Zhenqing; this rubbing taken from the Song dynasty engraving made by An Shiwen 安師文.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng zuo wei tie
entries.description: The "Zheng zuowei" of Yan Zhenqing; this rubbing taken from the Song dynasty engraving made by An Shiwen 安師文.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng zuo wei tie
entries.description: The "Zheng zuowei" of Yan Zhenqing; this rubbing taken from the Song dynasty engraving made by An Shiwen 安師文.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zi xu tie
entries.description: The "Zixu tie" by Huaisu, in his calligraphy; incomplete.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu pu
entries.description: The Shupu of Sun Guoting; this rubbing taken from the Yuanyou era (1086-94) engraving made by Xue Shaopeng (fl. 1087). This album also includes a rubbing of a postscript identifying this carving as the Xue Shaopeng engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu pu
entries.description: The Shupu of Sun Guoting; this rubbing possibly taken from the Tingyun guan engraving made by Wen Zhengming 文徵明 (1470-1559).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu pu
entries.description: The Shupu of Sun Guoting; this rubbing taken from the Yōkōkan engraving, based on the Tingyun guan engraving made by Wen Zhengming 文徵明 (1470-1559). This album also includes rubbings of three colophons, two of them added by Japanese collectors, and a postscript identifying this carving as the Yōkōkan engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huaisu san tie
entries.description: Three models of calligraphy by Huaisu: "Cang zhen" 藏真, "Lügong" 律公, and Shengmu jing 聖母經. The first two rubbings are taken from a Yuanyou era (1086-94) engraving; the third, from the (Song dynasty) engraving by You Shixiong (1038-97).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huaisu san tie
entries.description: Two models of calligraphy by Huaisu: "Cang zhen" 藏真 and "Lügong" 律公. These rubbings taken from the (Song dynasty) engraving by You Shixiong (1038-97). This album also includes rubbings of a number of Song colophons, the postface to the You Shixiong engraving, Li Bai's "Caoshu ge" 草書歌, and Sun Zhenkan's 孫軫龕 inscription of 1100.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huaisu san tie
entries.description: Three models of calligraphy by Huaisu: "Cang zhen" 藏真, "Lügong" 律公, and Shengmu jing 聖母經. The first two rubbings are taken from a Yuanyou era (1086-94) engraving; the third, from the (Song dynasty) engraving by You Shixong (1038-97).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheng mu tie
entries.description: Shengmu jing 聖母經, in the calligraphy of Huaisu; this rubbing taken from a Yuanyou era (1086-94) engraving. This album also includes rubbings of a postscript of the 1088 engraving and the autographs of Pei Xiu 裴休 (787?-860?) and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huaisu qian zi wen
entries.description: The Qianzi wen of Zhou Xingsi, in the calligraphy of Huaisu; with transcription of individual characters. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie (Xi'an ben)
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. These rubbings taken from a recarving executed by Fei Jiashou in 1646, after a copy of the 992 anthology in the collection of Prince Su 肅王 of the Ming dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie (Xi'an ben)
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. These rubbings taken from a Qing engraving of the 992 anthology; this set includes juan 9 and 10 (both incomplete) only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. Provenance of this engraving uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie (bu quan ben)
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. These rubbings taken from a Ming-Qing engraving of the 992 anthology; this set lacks juan 1 through 5.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. These rubbings taken from a Ming engraving of the 992 anthology; this set incomplete.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chunhua ge tie
entries.description: The Chunhua ge tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Han through the Tang dynasties; originally compiled and trace-copied by Wang Zhu 王著, by imperial command. These rubbings taken from a later recarving of the 992 anthology; this set includes juan 6 only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mulai ci bing ba
entries.description: The "Mulan ci," attributed to and in the calligraphy of Qian Jingchu. Rubbing taken from an engraving of the ballad commissioned by Guo Yin 郭寅 during the Xining era (1068-77).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mi Fu shu su tie
entries.description: "Ni gu" and seven other poems by Mi Fu, in his own calligraphy; text of this rubbing incomplete.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Daguan tie
entries.description: The Daguan tie (in ten juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled during the Daguan era (1107-10); these rubbings taken from a Ming dynasty engraving made by Chen Juchang of Wuxian, Jiangsu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mi ge tie
entries.description: The Bige tie (in ten juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xiyu tang tie
entries.description: The Xiyu tang tie (in ten juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled and copied freehand by Liu Cizhuang 劉次莊 (fl. 1092-1102) in 1092; this rubbing taken from a forgery borrowing only the name of the well-known anthology.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiang tie
entries.description: The Jiang tie, an anthology of early models of calligraphy by Cang Jie 倉頡 and others, compiled by Pan Shidan (11th cent.) sometime during the Huangyou (1049-54) and Jiayou (1056-63) reign eras; this rubbing taken from an early Ming facsimile engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ru tie
entries.description: The Ru tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from early times through the Tang, compiled by Wang Cai 王寀 (1078-1118) in 1109; this rubbing taken from a later engraving modeled on the well-known anthology.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Er Wang shu ba wei shi
titles_all: Er Wang shu ba wei shi
entries.description: A poem, "Er Wang shu bawei," composed by Mi Fu in 1098, in his calligraphy; this rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zuiweng ting ji
entries.description: The "Zuiweng ting ji" of Ouyang Xiu, purportedly in the calligraphy of Su Shi. The text, composed in 1048, was copied out by Su Shi during the Yuanyou era (1086-94); this rubbing taken from a Ming forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chengqing tang tie
entries.description: The Changqing tang tie, an anthology of models of Wang Xizhi's calligraphy, compiled and trace-copied during the Southern Song dynasty; this album comprises juan 1 and 4 of the anthology only. These rubbings taken from a Qing dynasty engraving. This album also includes a handwritten title inscription in the calligraphy of Sun Xingyan (1753-1818) and handwritten colophons by Sun Chengze (1592-1676) and Qiying (d. 1858).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Mengfu shi shu tie
entries.description: The Bohai cangzhen tie (in eight juan), a Chongzhen era (1628-44) anthology of models of calligraphy compiled by Chen Fushen 陳甫伸 of Haining 海寧 and trace-copied by Zhang Yong; this album comprises juan 8 only, prose and poetry of Zhao Mengfu, in Zhao's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji An zhuan tie
entries.description: The biography of Ji An (d. 112 B.C.) from Sima Qian's 司馬遷 Shiji 史記, in the calligraphy of Zhao Mengfu. This rubbing includes a colophon, by Da Chongguang 笪重光 (1623-92), which identifies the engraving as one made for an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled by Li Zongkong 李宗孔, the Lishu lou zhengzi tie.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Songxue zhai tie
entries.description: The Songxue zhai tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy including the biography of Ji An 汲黯 (d. 112 B.C.) from Sima Qian's 司馬遷 Shiji 史記 and Wang Xizhi's 王羲之 (303-79) "Lanting xu" 蘭亭序, in the calligraphy of Zhao Mengfu, trace-copied by Wen Zhengming; a postscript added by Wen Zhengming; and two letters by Wen, both in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhenshang zhai tie
entries.description: The Zhenshang zhai tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled by Hua Xia 華夏 (fl. 1522-57) of Wuxi 無錫, Jiangsu, during the Jiajing era (1522-66); trace-copied by Wen Zhengming. The anthology includes Zhong You's 鍾繇 "Jian Ji Zhi biao" 薦季直表, Wang Xizhi's 王羲之 "Yuansheng tie" 袁生帖, and Wang Fangqing's 王方慶 (d. 702) "Wansui tongtian." These rubbings taken from the set of engravings that were completed and subsequently destroyed by fire in 1522. This album also includes two handwritten colophons, one of them added by Zhang Tingji (1768-1848).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhenshang zhai tie
entries.description: The Zhenshang zhai tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled by Hua Xia 華夏 (fl. 1522-57) of Wuxi 無錫, Jiangsu, during the Jiajing era (1522-66); trace-copied by Wen Zhengming. The anthology includes Zhong You's 鍾繇 "Jian Ji Zhi biao" 薦季直表, Wang Xizhi's 王羲之 "Yuansheng tie" 袁生帖, and Wang Fangqing's 王方慶 (d. 702) "Wansui tongtian" 萬歲通天. These rubbings taken from the set of engravings that were completed in 1522 to replace those destroyed by fire earlier that year.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Huailin shu Ji Kang jue jiao shu
titles_all: Li Huailin shu Ji Kang jue jiao shu
entries.description: The "Juejiao shu" of Ji Kang, in the calligraphy of Li Huailin, trace-copied by Wen Zhengming; this rubbing taken from a later recarving of Wen's Tingyun guan engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuyan tang tie
entries.description: The Yuyan tang tie (in twenty-four juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled by Chen Xian 陳瓛 of Haining 海寧 during the Wanli era (1573-1620); this album comprises juan 20, calligraphy of the Song and Yuan dynasties (incomplete).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yugang zhai tie
entries.description: The Yugang zhai tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy by Zhong You 鍾繇, Wang Xizhi 王羲之 (303-79), and others, compiled by Wang Kentang of Jintan 金壇, Jiangsu, and engraved on stone (and wood) during the Wanli era (1573-1620); a complete copy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kuaixue tang tie
entries.description: The Kuaixue tang tie, an anthology of models of calligraphy from the Jin through the Yuan dynasties compiled by Feng Quan 馮銓 (1595-1673) of Zhuozhou 涿州. Feng's engraving is dated 1641.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mi tuo xiang tu die
entries.description: The Mituo xiangtu die; in Chinese, Korean, and Siddham (Kashmiri version of Brahmi derivative, used by Tibetans in Sanskrit transcription).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tingyun guan tie
entries.description: The "Xiyuan shi," ten poems by Wen Zhengming, in his calligraphy; these rubbings taken from an engraving made by Zhang Jianfu and included in the Tingyun guan tie compiled by Wen Zhengming and his son.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tingyun guan tie
entries.description: The "Xiyuan shi," ten poems by Wen Zhengming, in his calligraphy; these rubbings taken from an engraving made by Zhang Jianfu and included in the Tingyun guan tie compiled by Wen Zhengming and his son.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xihong tang fa tie
entries.description: The Xihong tang fatie (in sixteen juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy compiled, trace-copied, and engraved on stone by Dong Qichang during the Wanli era (1573-1620). These rubbings comprise juan 1: calligraphy of Pei Yaoqing 裴耀卿, Zhong Shaojing 鍾紹京 (fl. 727), Yan Zhenqing 顏真卿 (709-85), and Liu Gongquan 柳公權 (778-865).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dong Qichang cao shu
entries.description: An example of Dong Qichang's calligraphy; this rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baoxian tang tie
entries.description: The Baoxian tang tie (in twelve juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from early times through the Ming dynasty. The anthology was originally compiled by Wang Jin (1355-1426) and others, and copied out and engraved on stone by Song Hao and Liu Yu in 1496, at the request of Zhu Qiyuan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wenshan ting ji gu tie
entries.description: The Wenshan ting jigu tie, an anthology of early models of calligraphy compiled by Wang Xiangchun 王象春 (1578-1633); the anthology includes calligraphy by Wen Yanbo 文彥博, Zhao Mengfu 趙孟頫 (1254-1322), Zhu Zhishan 祝枝山 (1460-1526), Tang Yin 唐寅 (1470-1523), Wen Zhengming 文徵明 (1470-1559), and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhongqin tang ji gu tie ba
titles_all: Zhongqin tang ji gu tie ba
entries.description: Colophons to the Zhongqin tang jigu tie, an anthology of early models of calligraphy compiled by Wang Xiangchun 王象春 (d. 1630). The anthology includes over fifty models of calligraphy from the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng; represented here are twenty-eight colophons to the collection composed by Li Sancai and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bai zi ling bing qi lü san tie
titles_all: Bai zi ling bing qi lü san tie
entries.description: Two lyrics to the tune "Baizi ling," composed by Dong Qichang and written in his calligraphy; and two poems in seven-character regulated verse, one of them, "Chi jie Qi wang Jiucheng gong bishu shi" 敇借岐王九成宮避暑詩, composed by Wang Wei 王維 (701-61) and written in the calligraphy of Langhuanshizhang 瑯嬛世丈. Housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xincheng Wang shi da huai ji
titles_all: Xincheng Wang shi da huai ji
entries.description: "Dahuai ji," an account of the good works carried out by the Wang clan of Xincheng; composed by Qian Ruliang and written in the calligraphy of Xing Tong during the Wanli era (1573-1620).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhong qin lu xu
entries.description: Feng Qi's preface to the Zhongqin lu, an anthology of elegiac poems commemorating Wang Chongguang 王重光 (1502-58); the poems and preface were first inscribed on the walls of the Zhongqin ci in Guizhou 貴州, where Wang died in service, and later inscribed as well on the walls of the Shandong Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liang chao yu ji wen ba
titles_all: Liang chao yu ji wen ba
entries.description: Inscription by Jiao Hong commemorating Wang Chongguang 王重光 (1502-58) and his wife, written by command of Ming Muzong 穆宗 (r. 1567-72) and Ming Shenzong 神宗 (r. 1573-1620); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Taishu ren zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Liu Taishu, wife of Wang Chongguang 王重光 (1502-58); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xincheng Zhongqin ci ji
entries.description: Brief account of the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng; housed in the temple. These rubbings include only seven of the eight sheets that should comprise the set.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: He Wang Zhiyuan qi shi shou shi liang shou
titles_all: He Wang Zhiyuan qi shi shou shi liang shou
entries.description: Two poems by Zhu Zhifan and Yu Jideng congratulating Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604) of the Wang clan of Xincheng on his seventieth birthday.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Wu zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Wu, primogenitor of the Wang clan of Xincheng, by Ye Xianggao; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Chongguang ping man du mu zan
titles_all: Wang Chongguang ping man du mu zan
entries.description: Laudatory poem by Feng Qi praising Wang Chongguang (1502-58) for pacifying the tribal peoples of Guizhou 貴州 and making possible once again the collection of tribute timber; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xihong tang tie
entries.description: The Xihong tang tie (in sixteen juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Jin through the Yuan dynasties compiled by Dong Qichang (1555-1636) in 1603.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zeng Wang Zhiyuan Changchun yuan shi san shou
titles_all: Zeng Wang Zhiyuan Changchun yuan shi san shou
entries.description: Three poems by Cheng Kezhong on the Changchun yuan, a garden designed by Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Changchun yuan shi
entries.description: A poem by Wang Zhiyuan on the Changchun yuan, a garden designed by Wang; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan zan bing xu
entries.description: Panegyric, with preface, by Ye Xianggao, concerning the official career and achievements of Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Xiangchun xing zhuang
entries.description: Necrology of Wang Xiangchun (zi Jimu, 1578-1632); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Shen Shixing; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan lei bing xu
entries.description: Sacrificial dirge for Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Gong Nai; with preface. Housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan lei bing xu
entries.description: Sacrificial dirge for Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Xing Tong; with preface. Housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan ai ci
entries.description: A lament for Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Guo Zhengyu; with a sketch of Wang's life and achievements. Housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan mu biao
entries.description: Epitaph of Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Li Weizhen; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyuan zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Zhiyuan (1527-1604), by Jiao Hong; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang chongguang mu biao
entries.description: Epitaph of Wang Chongguang (1502-58), left assistant administration commissioner of Guizhou; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. This set of rubbings lacks eight lines of the original inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang chongguang mu biao
entries.description: Epitaph of Wang Chongguang (1502-58), left assistant administration commissioner of Guizhou; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Gui zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Gui (fl. 1435), great-grandson of Wang Chongguang 王重光 (1502-58); housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. These rubbings include only the first six of the seven sheets that should comprise the set.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhongqin tang ji gu tie ti ba
titles_all: Zhongqin tang ji gu tie ti ba
entries.description: Colophon to the Zhongqin tang jigu tie, an anthology of models of early calligraphy compiled by Wang Xiangqian 王象乾 (d. 1630). The anthology includes over fifty models of calligraphy from the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng; represented here is one colophon to the collection composed by Ge Xin.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyou zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Zhiyou (fl. 1577), surveillance commissioner of Zhejiang and younger brother of Wang Zhiyuan 王之垣 (1527-1604) of Xincheng; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Chongguang zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Chongguang (1502-58), posthumously titled right vice minister of revenue; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. These rubbings include only the first six of the seven sheets that should comprise the set.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Chongguang ping man du mu zan
titles_all: Wang Chongguang ping man du mu zan
entries.description: Laudatory inscription by Li Weizhen praising Wang Chongguang (1502-58) for pacifying the tribal peoples of Guizhou 貴州 and making possible once again the collection of tribute timber; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Zhiyou qi Li shi zhi
titles_all: Wang Zhiyou qi Li shi zhi
entries.description: Tomb inscription of Wang Zhiyou (fl. 1577), surveillance commissioner of Zhejiang and younger brother of Wang Zhiyuan 王之垣 (1527-1604), and Zhiyou's wife née Li; composed by and written in the calligraphy of Feng Qi and housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan of Xincheng. This set of rubbings lacks several portions of the original inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Lin zhuan
entries.description: Biographical sketch of Wang Lin (d. 1505) of Xincheng, mentor to the prince of Yingchuan; housed in the Zhongqin ci, ancestral temple of the Wang clan Xincheng.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yanxi tang tie
entries.description: The Yanxi tang tie (in eight juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from various dynasties compiled and trace-copied by Chen Jiru; this album comprises only a portion of the anthology: two examples of Yan Zhenqing's calligraphy, including his eulogy for his nephew Yan Jiming 顏季明, "Ji zhi wen" 祭姪文. This rubbing taken from the Ming dynasty engraving made by Chen Jiru.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tianguan shan shi
entries.description: The twenty-four "Tianguan shan" poems of Zhao Mengfu, in his calligraphy. This rubbing is taken from a Kangxi era (1662-1722) replica stone based on a Ming engraving of the Yuan original, and includes colophons of appreciation by Wen Zhengming 文徵明 (1470-1559) and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiubi tang tie
entries.description: The Qiubi tang tie (in eight juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Jin through the Yuan dynasties compiled by Liang Qingbiao (1620-91) of Zhending 真定 during the Kangxi era (1662-1722). This album comprises juan 6 only: three poems by Yin Changsheng 陰長生, in the calligraphy of Huang Tingjian 黃庭堅, trace-copied by You Yongfu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiubi tang tie
entries.description: The Qiubi tang tie (in eight juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Jin through the Yuan dynasties compiled by Liang Qingbiao (1620-91) of Zhending 真定 during the Kangxi era (1662-1722).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qiubi tang tie
entries.description: The Qiubi tang tie (in eight juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Jin through the Yuan dynasties compiled by Liang Qingbiao (1620-91) of Zhending 真定 during the Kangxi era (1662-1722).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhao Mengfu shou zha tie
entries.description: Ten letters written by Zhao Mengfu and addressed to Shi Yan 石巖 (zi Minzhan 民瞻, 1260-?); in Zhao's calligraphy. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original. This album also includes rubbings of three colophons by Zhang Fu (1472 進士), Zhang ☐, and Wang Hongxu (1645-1723).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tang Song ba da jia fa shu
titles_all: Tang Song ba da jia fa shu
entries.description: Models of calligraphy selected by Yao Xiaojing, representing eight masters of the Tang and Song: Yu Shinan 虞世南 (558-638), Chu Suiliang 褚遂良 (596-658), Yan Zhenqing 顏真卿 (709-85), Liu Gongquan 柳公權 (778-865); and Cai Xiang 蔡襄 (1012-67), Su Shi 蘇軾 (1036-1101), Huang Tingjan 黃庭堅 (1045-1105), and Mi Fu 米芾 (1051-1107). A forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wang Shizhen ba jue ju shi
titles_all: Wang Shizhen ba jue ju shi
entries.description: Eight quatrains (jueju) by Wang Shizhen, in his calligraphy; subsequently copied and engraved on stone by Liu Guangzhao.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sanxi tang fa tie
entries.description: The Sanxi tang fatie (in thirty-two juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy dating from the Wei through the Ming dynasties compiled by Liang Shizheng 梁詩正 (1697-1763) in 1750 from calligraphy in the collection of the Qianlong emperor (r. 1736-95).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yijin zhai tie
entries.description: The Yijin zhai tie, an anthology of early models of calligraphy compiled and engraved on stone by Yongxing 永瑆 (1758-1823), Imperial Prince Cheng 成親王.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shu pu ji lian
entries.description: A collection of couplets by Yan Xinhou, in the calligraphy of Sun Guoting 孫過庭; the text of the inscription is composed of individual characters extracted from Sun's Shupu. This rubbing taken from the engraving by Yan Xinhou, who selected and rearranged the characters comprising the text.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shiyuan cang zhen ji ke
entries.description: The Shiyuan cangzhen jike, an anthology of models of brush painting, with accompanying inscriptions, by Zhao Yisan, Deng Wenyuan 鄧文原 (1258-1328), Yun Shouping 惲壽平 (1633-90), and others, compiled by Chen Shijin, and engraved on stone by Zhang Qiao during the Xianfeng era (1851-61).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Banmu yuan ji
entries.description: The "Banmu yuan ji," an account of the restoration of the Banmu yuan, Beijing, during the Daoguang era (1821-50). This rubbing taken from an engraving based on the original text in the calligraphy of Shen Shuji, trace-copied by Wu Yue.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qian chu shi biao
entries.description: The "Qian chushu biao" of Zhuge Liang, in the calligraphy of Yue Fei; this rubbing taken from a Guangxu era (1875-1908) engraving based on a copy by Hu Shengyou 胡昇猷 (which is based on Yuan Baoheng's 袁保恆 copy of Yue Fei's original) housed in the Yue ci at Wuzhangyuan, Sichuan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qian chu shi biao ti ba
titles_all: Qian chu shi biao ti ba
entries.description: Colophons to the engraving of the "Qian chushi biao" of Zhuge Liang (184-234), in the calligraphy of Yue Fei (1103-41), housed in the Yue ci at Wuzhangyuan, Sichuan: the earlier, by Zuo Zongtang, is dated 1871; the later, by Yuan Baoheng, is dated 1875, the year Yuan Baoheng's own copy of the "Qian chushi biao" was engraved in stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Qian chu shi biao ti ji
titles_all: Qian chu shi biao ti ji
entries.description: Colophons to the engraving of the "Qian chushi biao" of Zhuge Liang (184-234), in the calligraphy of Yue Fei (1103-41), housed in the Yue ci at Wuzhangyuan, Sichuan: the colophons, by Fang Yurun and Hu Shengyou, are dated 1878, the year Hu Shengyou's own copy of the "Qian chushi biao" was engraved in stone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zigong zhi jie shi
entries.description: A poem by Shi Runzhang, inspired by the tree planted by Zigong near the tomb and Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Shijing dui lian
entries.description: A couplet by Guo Sijing; "You he song duo zhi/Wu hua di yi xiang."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Qingrong shu zha yi ze
titles_all: Kong Qingrong shu zha yi ze
entries.description: A letter by Kong Qingrong, seventy-third lineal descendant of Confucius; in Kong's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Guangzhao lun shu fa
entries.description: Three brief comments on the calligraphy of Zhong You 鍾繇 (151-230), Xie Zhuang 謝莊 (421-66), and Wang Xizhi 王羲之 (303-79) and Wang Xianzhi 王獻之 (344-388), by Chen Guangzhao; in his calligraphy. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Qiguang lun shu fa
entries.description: A short discourse on calligraphy, by Lu Qiguang; in his calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yue Fei shu chu shi biao
titles_all: Yue Fei shu chu shi biao
entries.description: The "Chushi biao" (first and second) of Zhuge Liang, in the calligraphy of Yue Fei; these rubbings taken from Ren Kai's 1876 recarving of the 1138 inscription. These albums also include a rubbing of Ren Kai's colophon of 1876.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu liang shou ti e
entries.description: Three-character inscription in the calligraphy of Kang Youwei: "Wuliang shou".

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Feng qiao ye bo shi ke
titles_all: Feng qiao ye bo shi ke
entries.description: The poem "Fengqiao ye bo" by Zhang Ji, in the calligraphy of Yu Yue; with a colophon by Yue. The poem, composed in the mid-eighth century, was engraved on stone in 1906 during restoration of Hanshan si, Suzhou.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Zan deng shi er shou
titles_all: Liu Zan deng shi er shou
entries.description: Two poems, one written in response to the other, by Liu Zan, retainer in the Academy of Scholarly Worthies, and the administrator of Yidu 益陼.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yao Hua deng mo ta shu hua
titles_all: Yao Hua deng mo ta shu hua
entries.description: Miscellaneous inscriptions and pictorial reliefs, including copies of inscriptions, pictorial reliefs, and paintings, from ancient to more recent times, by Yao Hua and others.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ji tie
entries.description: The Jitie (in ten juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy by Li Si and others; this rubbing taken from a later engraving based on traced copies by Kara Tenju, Nagamoto Shun, and the priest Daijaku of Shōmitsuji during the An-ei era (1772-79).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zi ji tie
entries.description: The Zi jitie (in ten juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy, including the inscription from the "Huayue jing xiang" stele and other pieces; selected and trace-copied by the priest Daijaku of Shōmitsuji during the Kansei era (1789-1800).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sun ji tie
entries.description: The Sun jitie (in ten juan), an anthology of calligraphy originally collected in the Song Daguan tie, juan 1 through 7, and subsequently trace-copied from it; a continuation of the anthology Zi jitie 子集帖 compiled and trace-copied by priest Daijaku 大寂 of Shōmitsuji during the Kansei era (1789-1800).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zeng ji tie
entries.description: The Zeng jitie (in six juan), an anthology of models of calligraphy, including the "Shengjiao xu" 聖教序 and other pieces originally collected in the Song Daguan tie 大觀帖, juan 8 and beyond, and subsequently trace-copied from it; a continuation of the anthology Sun jitie 孫集帖 trace-copied at Shōmitsuji during the Kansei era (1789-1900).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ba jiao pai ju yi shou
titles_all: Ba jiao pai ju yi shou
entries.description: A haiku by Bashō, written in his calligraphy, from the Komponji, Kagoshima; below the text, a portrait of Bashō seated.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong Lingyi shu zha yi ze
titles_all: Kong Lingyi shu zha yi ze
entries.description: A letter by Kong Lingyi, seventy-sixth lineal descendant of Confucius; in Kong's calligraphy.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bing Xin zi
entries.description: Inscription from an unidentified object: "Bingxin."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Yuan Fu zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the Lu Yuan zhong (clapperless bell), cast by Lu Yuan Fu, grand steward of the state of Lu 魯.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X zhong
entries.description: Inscription and some decor from the central panel (zheng 鉦) and the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the X zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) dating to the reign of Zhou Yiwang.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lu Kang zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the Lu Kang zhong (clapperless bell), concerning entreaties for good fortune, emoluments, good health, and a peaceful life.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xi Zhong zhong (1)
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the Xi Zhong zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) cast by Xi Zhong and dedicated to Fu Ji Bo 父己伯.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xi Zhong zhong (2)
entries.description: Inscription and some decor from the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the Xi Zhong zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) cast by Xi Zhong and dedicated to Fu Ji Bo 父己伯.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhu Gong Ba zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the far right side of the Zhu Gong Ba zhong (clapperless bell), cast by Zhu Gong Ba.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhu Gong Ba zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the Zhu Gong Ba zhong (clapperless bell), cast by Zhu Gong Ba.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhu Gong Ba zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the far left side of the Zhu Gong Ba zhong (clapperless bell), cast by Zhu Gong Ba.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jing Ren zhong (1)
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the Jing Ren zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) cast by Jing Ren and dedicated to his ancestors.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jing Ren zhong (2)
entries.description: Inscription and decor from the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the Jing Ren zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) cast by Jing Ren and dedicated to his ancestors.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jing Ren zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the Jing Ren zhong (clapperless bell), cast by Jing Ren and dedicated to his father.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Fu zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the Shi Fu linzhong (bell tuned to the pitch linzhong 林鐘), cast by Shi Fu and dedicated to his father Shu Shi 叔氏; this rubbing represents the inscription on the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the bell.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Fu zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the Shi Fu linzhong (bell tuned to the pitch linzhong 林鐘), cast by Shi Fu and dedicated to his father Shu Shi 叔氏; this rubbing represents the inscription on the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the bell.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang Wang Mei Shou zhong
entries.description: Inscription and some decor from the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the Huang Wang Mei Shou zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) dating to the Zhou dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Huang Wang Mei Shou zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the Huang Wang Mei Shou zhong, one bell of a chime (bianzhong 編鐘) purportedly dating to the Zhou dynasty; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zha zhong
entries.description: Inscriptions and some decor from the central panel (zheng 鉦) and the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the Zha zhong (clapperless bell), purportedly of Zhou date; possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X Er zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the central panel (zheng 鉦) of the X Er zhong (clapperless bell), cast by X Er of the state of Xu and dedicated to his forefathers and brothers; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X Er zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the "drum" or strike-point (gu 鼓) of the X Er zhong (clapperless bell), cast by X Er of the state of Xu 徐 and dedicated to his forefathers and brothers; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X Er zhong
entries.description: Inscription and some decor from the far right side of the X Er zhong (clapperless bell), cast by X Er of the state of Xu 徐 and dedicated to his forefathers and brothers; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X Er zhong
entries.description: Inscription from the far left side of the X Er zhong (clapperless bell), cast by X Er of the state of Xu 徐 and dedicated to his forefathers and brothers; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiao zhuan zhong
entries.description: Rubbings of a zhong (clapperless bell) with inscriptions in "flood dragon" script; forgeries.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ya Xu nao
entries.description: Inscription from the handle (recto) of the Ya Xu Chuo nao (mallet-struck signal bell), cast by Chuo.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ya Xu nao
entries.description: Inscription from the handle (verso) of the Ya Xu nao (mallet-struck signal bell).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ya Xu nao
entries.description: Zoomorphic decor from the cavity (qiang 腔) of the Ya Xu nao (mallet-struck signal bell).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhong nao
entries.description: Rubbing of the Zhong nao (mallet-struck signal bell) from Yinxu 殷墟 (late period); this rubbing contains no textual inscription and probably represents the back of the bell.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shou nao
entries.description: Inscription from the handle of the Shou nao (mallet-struck signal bell); possibly of Shang date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X X nao
entries.description: Inscription from the X X nao (mallet-struck signal bell), cast by X.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xu X Yin zheng
entries.description: Inscription from the Xu X Yin zheng (mallet-struck bell), cast by Xu X Yin of the state of Xu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: X zun
entries.description: Inscription from the X zun (wine cup).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fu Yi zun
entries.description: Inscription from the Fu Yi Zun (wine cup), dedicated to Fu Yi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ya Er zun

The king made the bestowal in recognition of a libation the marquis offered him in Pi 坏, where the king had paused on his return from a southern campaign against Jiao Yong 角鄘.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lüsi chi
entries.description: Inscription from a bronze ruler purportedly cast in Lüsi, Shanxi, during the Jianchu era (76-84) in accordance with specifications promulgated by the law of Han Zhangdi 章帝 (r. 76-88); this rubbing taken from a later replica modeled after the original. This page of the album also includes rubbings of the sides of the ruler, with calibrations and textual inscriptions identifying it as a replica manufactured for the Kong clan of Qufu, Shandong.

entries.title: Wang Xianghui tong zao xiang ji
titles_all: Wang Xianghui tong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of Guanyin and an inscription concerning its dedication by the lay devotee Wang Xianghui, on behalf of her son Wang Quan 王泉, that he might complete his military service safely and without incident, during the Kaihuang era. Includes rubbings of recto and verso of image and base (and sides of base).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Rong tong zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Gao Rong tong zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive image of Guanyin and an inscription concerning its dedication by the devotee Gao Rong, on behalf of his parents, during the Renshou era (601-4). Includes rubbings of recto and verso of image.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Siyong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image by the devotee Zhang Siyong, on behalf of his late child, probably during the Renshou era (601-4); possibly a forgery. Includes rubbings of image, front and back, and base, front, back, and sides.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi Haibin tong zao xiang ji

entries.description: Votive image of Guanyin and an inscription concerning its dedication by the devotee Shi Haibin during the Renshou era (601-4). Includes rubbings of recto and verso of image and base (and sides of base).

entries.title: Zhang Baoliang tong zao xiang bing ji
titles_all: Zhang Baoliang tong zao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Votive image of the Buddha and an inscription concerning its dedication by the devotee Zhang Baoliang, on behalf of his late son and his late wife, during the Renshou era (601-4). Includes rubbings of recto and verso of image and base (and side of base).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: □ fu Mian tong zao xiang ji
titles_all: □ fu Mian tong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image of the Buddha (base only remaining) and inscription concerning its dedication by an unidentified donor, on behalf of his late father, Mian, during the Daye era (605-17). Includes rubbings of all four sides of base.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo □ tong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of three Buddhas by Guo ☐ during the Chuigong era (685-88).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo □ tong zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive image (fragmentary) of three Buddhas, dedicated by Guo ☐ during the Chuigong era (685-88). (Recto of votive inscription Tsa 125 (5)-7.)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo □ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of three Buddhas by Guo ☐ during the Chuigong era (685-88). Includes rubbing of image (fragmentary).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jiao long tong zao xiang bei
titles_all: Jiao long tong zao xiang bei
entries.description: Votive stele of bronze gilt, engraved with intertwining dragons and an inscription concerning its dedication during the Tang dynasty. Includes rubbings of recto and verso of votive stele.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Siniang zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha by Wang Siniang during the Shangyuan era (674-76 or 760-62).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fei tian xiang
entries.description: Pitctoral reliefs from the surface of a bronze bell: flying devas. Casting of the bell for Pongdoksa was originally undertaken by Kyŏngdŏgwang of Silla as an act of devotion on behalf of his father; when he died before its completion, his son and successor, Hyegongwang, took over the task.

entries.title: Tong fo yi zuo

titles_all: Zhao Da tong zao xiang ji

entries.title: Da'an si sha men tong zao xiang ji

entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of the Buddha (Tsa (6)-31.1) by the ascetic Kong of Da'an si; possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taotie wen hu
entries.description: Rubbing of a hu (wine jar) with taotie decor; date and provenance unknown.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que bei mian (shi que ming)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que bei mian (shi que ming)
entries.description: Inscription and pictorial relief from the northern face of the west memorial pillar (que) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the inscription topped by two (of three) registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them smaller than the others; and a deity, tigers, and fish.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que nan mian
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que nan mian
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the southern face of the west memorial pillar (que) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three (uppermost of five) registers of relief depicting a group of six figures; a chariot and rider; and another group of six figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (1)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (1)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting eminent wives; filial sons and virtuous men; wise ministers; and a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with horses, chariots, and a fusang 扶桑 tree outside and to the right and left.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the east wall, four registers of relief (without gable) depicting eminent wives; filial sons and virtuous men; loyal assassin-retainers, Zhongli Chun 鍾離春; a scene of homage involving a county official, and a kitchen scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (3)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (3)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the west wall, four registers of relief (without gable) depicting ancient sovereigns; filial sons; loyal assassin-retainers; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (1)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (1)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 1) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (mostly damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the surging well, divine tripod, yellow dragon, and the mingjia 蓂莢 plant; and the six-legged beast.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 2) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (mostly damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the jade flower (identified by its cartouche), intertwining trees, glass bi 璧, dark gui 珪, birds joined at the wing, fish joined at the eye, and the silver jar; the birth of Hou Ji 后稷, and the arrival of the Qusou 渠搜; and standing figures, a chariot, and horses.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone slab uncovered near the Front Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the white pheasant and fish joined at the eye.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone slab uncovered near the Front Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the white pheasant and fish joined at the eye.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the uppermost portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper half of the wall, two registers of relief topped by a gable (central portion only) depicting the King Father of the East; the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the central portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion, with a horse and chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di si shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di si shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di wu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a procession of chariots, riders, and armed footsoldiers; and an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infanty engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it in the water.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, four registers of relief depicting virtuous men and women, including the Righteous Wife of Qi 齊義婦, Xing Qu 刑渠; Bo You 伯游, Lao Lai Zi 老萊子, and the younger brothers of Zhou Wuwang 周武王; a procession of chariots and a banquet scene with entertainers; and a kitchen scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the west face of the gable (largely damaged), three registers of relief depicting a dragon and a tiger; the words "Ci Qin" 此秦 inside a cartouche; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the east face of the gable (largely damaged), three registers of relief depicting a deity, supernatural beings, a dragon; horses, human figures in court dress; and a procession including a horse and chariot and human figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eave purlin at the east wall, one register of relief depicting a procession of chariots.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, three registers of relief depicting Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 221-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; two carts and a rider; and a cart and chariot.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting zoomorphic cloud patterns; seated figures passing objects to one another; a chariot and riders; and another chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting what seems to be a cross section of a multistoried building: on the roof, fantastic creatures; inside, servants in the kitchen preparing a meal; attendants on the upper floors serving the meal to a man and woman in separate apartments; and on the floor above them seated figures play and listen to music--all framed and compartmented by eaves, ceilings, floors, and columns.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting birds, winged beings, and zoomorphic cloud patterns; two mingjia 蓂莢 plants and supernatural beings; a woman being served food by several attendants; and a chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eave purlin at the west wall, one register of relief depicting a procession of chariots and a rider.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief possibly from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone whose actual position in the shrines is uncertain, two registers of relief depicting the virtuous men Hou Ying 侯贏, Yan Shu 顏淑, and Fan Shu 范贖, among others. This rubbing also includes an inscription recording discovery of the stone by Li Kezheng 李可正 and others in Jiaxiang in 1789.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, two registers of relief topped by a gable (lower half of central portion only) depicting the King Father of the East; the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall. The right half is divided into two registers of relief depicting recovery of the sacred ding 鼎 (cauldron) from the River Si 泗水. The left half is divided into three registers of relief depicting a banquet scene with entertainers; a procession of chariots and riders; and a kitchen scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the right portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them prone on the ground and evidently being attacked by another of the group; Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 246-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; Fu Xi 伏羲 and Nü Wa 女媧.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the left portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting six figures grouped on either side of a snake coiled around a vessel; a horse and chariot with a figure standing before it and another figure, behind it, giving something to a smaller, kneeling figure; two figures brandishing hammer and axe, apparently rushing to aid a third, who is prone on the ground with a snake wrapped around his body.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper left portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, three registers of relief depicting two figures in conversation and another, a woman, holding a bow, and to the left, a building with a figure seated inside, another kneeling outside, and a third ascending to the roof; Yanzi 晏子 using two peaches to kill off three generals; and a chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting, among others, the filial sons Ding Lan 丁蘭 and Xing Qu 刑渠; two women, one holding an infant and one a mirror, both of them with figures kneeling before them; the Duke of Zhou 周公 assisting Chengwang 成王 in the administration of state affairs; and a chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone from a niche in the wall, two (in the leftmost portion, three) registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with a horse and chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief depicting a water deity in a fish-drawn chariot surrounded by a retinue of human figures and fantastic marine warriors; figures in groups of one, two, and three, apparently engaged in agricultural work; and figures, supernatural and human, in groups of one, two, and three, making gestures of salutation and congratulation.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, two registers of relief depicting supernatural beings, dragons, and clouds; a male and a female deity, supernatural beings, chariots, and clouds, and below them, a pavilion, officials and supernatural beings, a chariot and horses.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers or relief depicting supernatural beings, dragons, and clouds; rain and thunder deities and supernatural beings; men, beasts, and monsters; and a hunting scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di si shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di si shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling of the Front Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting, respectively, the deities of the rain, thunder, wind, and Northern Dipper, along with their retinues.
