entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the top portion of the wall (damaged) depicting a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower half of the wall depicting an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it in the water.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable in the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the western face of the gable, one register of relief (damaged) depicting an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable in the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the western face of the gable, one register of relief (damaged) depicting an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper half of the wall topped by a gable (lacking upper half of either side, upper third of central portion), two registers of relief (damaged) depicting the Queen Mother of the West; the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi duan zhu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi duan zhu
entries.description: Inscription from a broken column uncovered near the site of the Rear Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong: "Wu jia lin."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi ci shi chong li ji
titles_all: Wu shi ci shi chong li ji
entries.description: Inscription by Weng Fanggang recording the particulars of Li Dongqi's 李東琪 reerection of the stone slabs of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong, including the names of all those involved in the project.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi ti ji
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi ti ji
entries.description: Inscription by Qian Yong written on the occasion of a visit to the Han dynasty site of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. The inscription provides a rough inventory of the stone slabs recovered at the site and records the names of those, including Li Dongqi, who directed the excavation and restoration of the shrines.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi chong li mu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi chong li mu
entries.description: Personal names and official titles (transcribed from Han inscriptions) of the four members of the Wu clan represented at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong; written out by Li Dongqi after reerecting the shrines' slabs.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que dong mian
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que dong mian
entries.description: Inscription from the eastern face of the west memorial pillar (que) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong: "Wu shi ci."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que nan mian
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que nan mian
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the southern face of the west memorial pillar (que) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three (uppermost of five) registers of relief depicting a group of six figures; a chariot and rider; and another group of six figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (1)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (1)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting eminent wives; filial sons and virtuous men; wise ministers; and a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with horses, chariots, and a fusang 扶桑 tree outside and to the right and left.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the east wall, four registers of relief topped by a gable depicting the King Father of the East; eminent wives; filial sons and virtuous men; loyal assassin-retainers, Zhongli Chun 鍾離春; a scene of homage involving a county official, and a kitchen scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (3)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang (3)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the west wall, four registers of relief topped by a gable depicting the Queen Mother of the West; ancient sovereigns; filial sons; loyal assassin-retainers; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (1)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (1)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 1) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (mostly damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the surging well, divine tripod, yellow dragon, and the mingjia 蓂莢 plant; and the six-legged beast.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 2) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the right third of the slab (mostly damaged), two registers of relief depicting various auspicious omens, including the jade flower (identified by its cartouche).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 2) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the center third of the slab (damaged), three registers of relief depicting various auspicious omens, including the intertwining tree, glass bi 璧, dark gui 珪, birds joined at the wing; the birth of Hou Ji 后稷, the arrival of the Qusou 渠搜; and standing figures, a chariot, and horses.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang Wu Liang ci xiang rui tu (2)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling (good-omen slab 2) of the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the left third of the slab (damaged), three registers of relief depicting various auspicious omens, including the silver jar and the fish joined at the eye.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi xiang rui tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone slab uncovered near the Front Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief (damaged) depicting various auspicious omens, including the white pheasant and fish joined at the eye.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di yi shi

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the uppermost portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper half of the wall, two registers of relief (without gable) depicting the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the central portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion, with a horse and chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di wu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a procession of chariots, riders, and armed footsoldiers; and an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond at, and underneath it in the water.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, four registers of relief depicting virtuous men and women, including the Righteous Wife of Qi 齊義婦, Xing Qu 刑渠; Bo You 伯游, Lao Lai Zi 老萊子, and the younger brothers of Zhou Wuwang 周武王; a procession of chariots and a banquet scene with entertainers; and a kitchen scene.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the west face of the gable (largely damaged), three registers of relief depicting a dragon and a tiger; the words "Ci Qin" 此秦 inside a cartouche; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the east face of the gable (largely damaged), three registers of relief depicting a deity, supernatural beings, a dragon; horses, human figures in court dress; and a procession including a horse and chariot and human figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eave purlin at the east wall, one register of relief depicting a procession of chariots.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Ring (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, three registers of relief depicting Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 221-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; two carts and a rider; and a cart and chariot.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting zoomorphic cloud patterns; seated figures passing objects to one another; a chariot and riders; and another chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting what seems to be a cross section of a multistoried building: on the roof, fantastic creatures; inside, servants in the kitchen preparing a meal; attendants on the upper floors serving the meal to a man and woman in separate apartments; and on the floor above them seated figures play and listen to music--all framed and compartmented by eaves, ceilings, floors, and columns.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting birds, winged beings, and zoomorphic cloud patters; two mingjia 蓂莢 plants and supernatural beings; a woman being served food by several attendants; and a chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eave purlin at the west wall, one register of relief depicting a procession of chariots and a rider.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief possibly from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone whose actual position in the shrines is uncertain, two registers of relief depicting the virtuous men Hou Ying 侯贏, Yan Shu 顏淑, and Fan Shu 范贖, among others. This rubbing also includes an inscription recording discovery of the stone by Li Kezheng and others in Jiaxiang in 1789.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di er shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di er shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, two registers of relief topped by a gable depicting the King Father of the East; the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall. The right half is divided into two registers of relief depicting recovery of the sacred ding 鼎 (cauldron) from the River Si 泗水. The left half is divided into three registers of relief depicting a banquet scene with entertainers; a procession of chariots and riders; and a kitchen scene.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the right portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them prone on the ground and evidently being attacked by another of the group; Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 246-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; below that, Fu Xi 伏羲 and Nü Wa 女媧.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the left portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting six figures grouped on either side of a snake coiled around a vessel; a horse and chariot with a figure standing before it and another figure, behind it, giving something to a smaller, kneeling figure; two figures brandishing hammer and axe, apparently rushing to aid a third, who is prone on the ground with a snake wrapped around his body.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper left portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, three registers of relief depicting two figures in conversation and another, a woman, holding a bow, and, to the left, a building with a figure seated inside, another kneeling outside, and a third ascending to the roof; Yanzi 晏子 using two peaches to kill off three generals; and a chariot and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting, among others, the filial sons Ding Lan 丁蘭 and Xing Qu 刑渠; two women, one holding an infant and one a mirror, both of them with figures kneeling before them; the Duke of Zhou 周公 assisting Chengwang 成王 in the administration of state affairs; and a chariot and riders.

titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone from a niche in the wall, two (in the leftmost portion, three) registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with a horse and a chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di yi shi

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the ceiling of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, three registers of relief depicting a water deity in a fish-drawn chariot surrounded by a retinue of human figures and fantastic marine warriors; figures in groups of one, two, and three, apparently engaged in agricultural work; and figures, supernatural and human, in groups of one, two, and three, making gestures of salutation and congratulation.

titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the celling of the Front Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting divinities of antiquity, including a chariot drawn by horses and hybrid beasts; Fu Xi 伏羲 and Nü Wa 女媧 with supernatural retinue; a chariot drawn by dragons; and an ox, supernatural beings and beasts, and human figures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the top portion of the wall (damaged) depicting a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di qi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di qi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower half of the wall depicting an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it in the water.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable in the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eastern face of the gable, two registers of relief (damaged) depicting a dragon, deities, and a horse and chariot; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a gable in the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the western face of the gable, one register of relief (damaged) depicting an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang hou shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper half of the wall (without gable), two registers of relief (damaged) depicting the disciples of Confucius; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi duan zhu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi duan zhu
entries.description: Inscription from a broken column uncovered near the site of the Rear Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong, "Wu jia lin."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi ci shi chong li ji
titles_all: Wu shi ci shi chong li ji
entries.description: Inscription by Weng Fanggang recording the particulars of Li Dongqi's 李東琪 reerection of the stone slabs of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong, including the names of all those involved in the project.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi ti ji
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi ti ji
entries.description: Inscription by Qian Yong written on the occasion of a visit to the Han dynasty site of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. The inscription provides a rough inventory of the stone slabs recovered at the site and records the names of those, including Li Dongqi, who directed the excavation and restoration of the shrines.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi chong li mu
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi chong li mu
entries.description: Personal names and official titles (transcribed from Han inscriptions) of the four members of the Wu clan represented at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong; written out by Li Dongqi after reerecting the shrines' slabs.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the central portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion, with a horse and chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a procession of chariots, riders, and armed footsoldiers; and an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it in the water.

titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di liu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting a procession of chariots, riders, and armed footsoldiers; and an arched bridge with chariots, cavalry, and infantry engaged in battle on the bridge, above and beyond it, and underneath it in the water.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, three registers of relief depicting two mingjia 蓂莢 plants and supernatural beings; a woman being served food by several attendants; and a chariot and riders.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di san shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower right portion of the wall, two registers of relief depicting recovery of the sacred ding 鼎 (cauldron) from the River Si 泗水.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di si shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the right portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them prone on the ground and evidently being attacked by another of the group; Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 246-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; below that, Fu Xi 伏羲 and Nü Wa 女媧.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di wu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the left portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting six figures grouped on either side of a snake coiled around a vessel; a horse and chariot with a figure standing before it and another figure, behind it, giving something to a smaller, kneeling figure; two figures brandishing hammer and axe, apparently rushing to aid a third, who is prone on the ground with a snake wrapped around his body.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di ba shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting, among others, the filial sons Ding Lan 丁蘭 and Xing Qu 刑渠; two women, one holding an infant and one a mirror, both of them with figures kneeling before them; the Duke of Zhou 周公 assisting Chengwang 成王 in the administration of state affairs; and a chariot and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone from a niche in the wall, two (in the leftmost portion, three) registers of relief depicting a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with a horse and chariot and a tree of intertwining branches outside and to the left; and a procession of chariots and riders.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di jiu shi

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang shi qian shi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang shi qian shi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) a the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the upper portion of the wall, one register of relief depicting the disciples of Confucius. This relief was uncovered by Huang Yi (1744-1802) at Wuzhai shan, Jiaxiang, in 1786.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang zuo shi shi di yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief possibly from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone whose actual position in the shrines is uncertain, two registers of relief depicting the mother of Wang Ling 王陵 (d. 181 B.C.), standing before an official and his chariot and holding a knife to her throat, as if about to take her own life; and Jin Linggong 晉靈公, in his audience hall, setting a mastiff on Zhao Dun 趙盾.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que hua xiang shi ji que ming
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi shi que xi que hua xiang shi ji que ming
entries.description: Inscription and pictorial relief from the northern face of the west memorial pillar (que) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the inscription topped by three registers of relief depicting a chariot and rider; a group of four figures, one of them smaller than the others; and a deity, tigers, and fish.

Here, the inscription topped by two (of three) registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them smaller than the others; and a deity, tigers, and fish.

titles_all: Wu shi shi shi Wu Liang ci hua xiang shi (1)
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Liang (78-151) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting eminent wives; filial sons and virtuous men; wise ministers; and a scene of homage in a two-storied pavilion with horses, chariots, and a fusang 扶桑 tree outside and to the right and left.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, two sides of the cornerstone of a niche in the wall. On the right, four registers of relief depicting birds, winged beings, and zoomorphic cloud patterns; two mingjia 蓂莢 plants and supernatural beings; a woman being served food by several attendants; and a chariot and riders. On the left, four registers of relief depicting what seems to be a cross section of a multistoried building: on the roof, fantastic creatures; inside, servants in the kitchen preparing a meal; attendants on the upper floors serving the meal to a man and woman in separate apartments; and on the floor above them seated

titles_all: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting what seems to be a cross section of a multistoried building: on the roof, fantastic creatures; inside, servants in the kitchen preparing a meal; attendants on the upper floors serving the meal to a man and woman in separate apartments; and on the floor above them seated figures play and listen to music--all framed and compartmented by eaves, ceilings, floors, and columns. This rubbing is cut short at the top and lacks the heads of the seated figures on the uppermost floor.

entries.title: Wu shi shi shi hua xiang qian shi shi di shi yi shi

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the cornerstone of a niche in the wall, four registers of relief depicting birds, winged beings, and zoomorphic cloud patterns; two mingjia 蓂莢 plants and supernatural beings; a woman being served food by several attendants; and a chariot and riders.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the west wall of the shrine of Wu Rong (d. 168) at the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the eave purlin at the west wall, one register of relief depicting a procession of chariots and a rider. This rubbing is cut short and lacks the horse and chariot bringing up the rear of the procession.

entries.description: Pictorial relief possibly from the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, a stone whose actual position in the shrines is uncertain, two registers of relief depicting the mother of Wang Ling 王陵 (d. 181 B.C.), standing before an official and his chariot and holding a knife to her throat, as if to take her own life; and Jin Linggong 晉靈公, in his audience hall, setting a mastiff on Zhao Dun 趙盾.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the east wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the lower portion of the wall. The right half is divided into two registers of relief depicting recovery of the sacred ding 鼎 (cauldron) from the River Si 泗水. The left half is divided into three registers of relief depicting a banquet scene with entertainers; a procession of chariots and riders; and a kitchen scene.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the right portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting a group of four figures, one of them prone on the ground and evidently being attacked by another of the group; Jing Ke's 荊軻 attempted assassination of Qin Shihuangdi 秦始皇帝 (r. 246-210 B.C.), then king of Qin; below that, Fu Xi 伏羲 and Nü Wa 女媧.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the rear wall of the Left Chamber of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, the left portion of the wall, three registers of relief depicting six figures grouped on either side of a snake coiled around a vessel; a horse and chariot with a figure standing before it and another figure, behind it, giving something to a smaller, kneeling figure; two figures brandishing hammer and axe, apparently rushing to aid a third, who is prone on the ground with a snake wrapped around his body.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone slab uncovered during the Tongzhi era (1862-74) at the site of the Wu Family Shrines, Jiaxiang, Shandong. Here, two registers of relief depicting Confucius playing stone chimes while others listen; Liuxia Hui 柳下惠 covering a woman against the cold; and Gongsun Chujiu 公孫杵臼 kneeling outside a house in which the Orphan of Zhao 趙氏孤兒 and his mother are seated.

Here, six registers of relief: acrobats; a groom leading a horse; two registers of scholar-officials grasping tallies in their hands; and two registers of grouped figures engaged in various activities. This relief, uncovered on the grounds of the Shengshou si on Qiri shan, is numbered er.

entries.title: Han hua xiang shi - qi ma tu

entries.title: Han hua xiang shi - wei che ru guan tu
titles_all: Han hua xiang shi - wei che ru guan tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Henan: a curtained carriage bearing a woman, and attendants, on foot, approaching the gate of a pass. Possibly a forgery. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu wang Qi Huan Guan Zhong hua xiang shi
titles_all: Wu wang Qi Huan Guan Zhong hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief purportedly from Shandong. Here, four registers of relief depicting the king of Wu and attendants; musicians and entertainers; chariots carrying Duke Huan of Qi and Guan Zhong (d. 645 B.C.); and a hunting scene. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shandong hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Shandong: two dragons, twined and intertwined around two reptilian creatures.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shandong Han hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Shandong: chariot, horses, and a rider.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi
titles_all: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Baoying, Jiangsu. Here, the portal of a Han tomb, three registers of relief depicting the Vermilion Bird (zhuque 朱雀); a taotie 饕餮 mask with a ring hanging from its mouth; and a figure waving a shield and sword. (Verso of Tsa 142 (1).)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi
titles_all: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Baoying, Jiangsu. Here, the portal of Han tomb, three registers of relief depicting the meeting of Confucius and Laozi; a group of three figures, one of them nude; and kitchen preparations.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi e
titles_all: Sheyang shi men hua xiang shi e
entries.description: Tile inscription added to the portal to a Han tomb in Baoying, Jiangsu, during the Daoguang era.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sheyang shi men hua xiang ti ji
titles_all: Sheyang shi men hua xiang ti ji
entries.description: Inscription written by Bao Shichen on the occasion of a visit to the Han tomb at Baoying, Jiangsu, to view the portal reliefs.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

titles_all: Li fu ren mu men hua xiang ti zi
entries.description: Inscription and pictorial relief of a deer from the portal of the tomb of Mistress Li, wife of the marquis of Qinting 琴亭; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pu sa xiang
entries.description: Image of a bodhisattva, from Datong, Shanxi; possibly of Han date. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu rui tu bing ji
entries.description: Inscription and pictorial relief from the rockface at Mianchi 澠池, Gansu: five auspicious omens, with a panegyric on the virtuous administration of Li Xi 李翕. The engraving was dedicated by local citizens and subordinate officials.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Sun Ding mai di bie
entries.description: Deed of purchase of land by Sun Ding, neighborhood marquis of Kuaiji 會稽; possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shi zi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Datong, Shanxi: a lion. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bei Wei hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone couch or seat: two attendants in the train of third, who is leading a horse.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bei Wei hua xiang shi tu lu
titles_all: Bei Wei hua xiang shi tu lu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone couch or seat: two robed figures, male and female, seated and facing one another.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bei Wei hua xiang shi
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a stone couch or seat: female musicians, carrying instruments, in procession.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Pu sa tou xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Datong, Shanxi: head of a bodhisattva. In vermilion.

entries.title: Liu Jingmao zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of Maitreya by the devotee Liu Jingmao, on behalf of the emperor, his own spiritual teachers, and others, at Qianfo shan, Shandong, during the Kaihuang era (581-600).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhaoling liu jun
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the imperial mausoleum Zhaoling: the six battle chargers of Tang Taizong. This rubbing taken from a later recarving; in vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Tianshi xiang bing zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Caoxian, Shandong: portrait of Zhang Daoling 張道陵 (fl. 142), the Celestial Master, with a panegyric by Tang Suzong. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fei xian xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from a Tang dynasty tomb: flying immortals.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi yan ju xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: full-length portrait of Confucius, attributed to the painter Wu Daozi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Dayan ta dong men mei xian ke hua
titles_all: Dayan ta dong men mei xian ke hua
entries.description: Image of the Buddha and attendants dating from the Tang dynasty and, over the surface of the incised relief, inscriptions of a poem and personal names dating from the Ming dynasty; from the lintel of the eastern entrance to the Dayan ta at Ci'en si, Chang'an 長安.

entries.description: Image of the Buddha and attendants dating from the Tang dynasty and, over the surface of the incised relief, inscriptions of personal names dating from the Ming dynasty; from the lintel of the southern entrance to the Dayan ta at Ci'en si, Chang'an.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.description: Inscription by Wang Yuanzhi of Juye, recording his acquisition of a talisman granting him the protection of Zhang Daoling 張道陵 (fl. 142), the Celestial Master, on his journey home. In vermilion and black.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan sheng di jun shi ji
titles_all: Guan sheng di jun shi ji
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Jiangdu, Jiangsu: bamboo, pine, and rock. Carved to commemorate recovery of a seal that had once belonged to Guan Yu 關羽 (160-220) and that bears the words "Hanshouting hou zhi yin" 漢壽亭侯之印.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Nanjixianweng xiang bing zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Nanjixianweng (Laozi), with a short panegyric in the calligraphy of Sansong and two colophons of appreciation. In vermilion.

titles_all: Bai yi da bei wu yin xin tuo luo ni jing bing xiang
entries.description: Image of Guanyin; above, the text of a sutra in the calligraphy of Li Guanghui, writing in the style of Qin Guan 秦觀 (1049-1100). In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han bo tu zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a variety of cypress (hanbo), with a panegyric.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Damo mian bi tu bing zan
titles_all: Damo mian bi tu bing zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a portrait of Bodhidharma (6th cent.) facing the wall in contemplation, with a panegyric.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fei tian xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a demon, attributed to Wu Daozi. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on a copy of the original painting by Wu Daozi and commissioned by Zhao Dai, magistrate of Quyang, during the Ming dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanzhong ba jing tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief. Eight views of the Guanzhong region (modern Shaanxi), with accompanying verse, by Zhu Jiyi; with a colophon by Zhou Wangbao 周王褒. The engraving of the verse and landscapes was commissioned and put in place by Zhao Shi 趙鉽 and Jin Wenyu 晉文煜. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanzhong ba jing tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief. Eight views of the Guanzhong region (modern Shaanxi), with accompanying verse, by Zhu Jiyi; with a colophon by Zhou Wangbao 周王褒. The engraving of the verse and landscapes was commissioned and put in place by Zhao Shi 趙鉽 and Jin Wenyu 晉文煜. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanzhong ba jing tu
entries.description: Pictorial reliefs. Eight views of the Guanzhong region (modern Shaanxi), with accompanying inscriptions by Lin Yi. These rubbings include only five of the eight landscapes.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wan shi shi biao tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: Confucius and sixteen of his disciples. The title inscription, "Wanshi shibiao," and accompanying verse were composed by and written in the calligraphy of the Kangxi emperor.

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: Confucius and sixteen of his disciples. The title inscription, "Wanshi shibiao," and accompanying verse were composed by and written in the calligraphy of the Kangxi emperor.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan Yu chang zheng tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: Guan Yu (160-220), armed and mounted on a charger. This rubbing taken from a Qing engraving of Guan Yu by Li Jintai, trace-copied by Dong Mu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi lin tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: sketch map of the Kongzi lin (or Konglin), the Kong family graveyard.

entries.title: Kongzi lin tu

entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: sketch map of the Temple of Confucius.

titles_all: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: Confucius and his seventy-two disciples. The title inscription, "Wanshi shibiao," is in the calligraphy of the Qianlong emperor.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taishan han bo tu ji
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a variety of cypress (hanbo), with a panegyric, by the Qianlong emperor. The emperor sketched the tree and wrote the inscription after viewing the cypress at Yuemiao during a tour of Taishan, Shandong, in 1762.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan Yu ci Chao tu bing shu
titles_all: Guan Yu ci Chao tu bing shu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: Guan Yu leaving the camp of Cao Cao 曹操 (155-220). The text of Guan Yu's parting letter to Cao appears in the upper right of the engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
titles_all: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: Confucius and his seventy-two disciples. The title inscription, "Wanshi shibiao," is in the calligraphy of the Qianlong emperor.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
titles_all: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: Confucius and his seventy-two disciples. The title inscription, "Wanshi shibiao," is in the calligraphy of the Qianlong emperor.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu bai luo han xiang
entries.description: Full-length portraits of five hundred lohans, dedicated by the prefect of Changzhou 常州, surnamed Hu 胡, the magistrate of Wujin, surnamed Zhou 周, and the magistrate of Yanghu 陽湖, at Tianning si, Wujun, in 1799.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Nanhai Guanyin xiang
entries.description: Image of the bodhisattva Guanyin: copy of the Nanhai Guanyin, attributed to Wu Daozi, at Zhenhai si 鎮海寺 of Putuo shan 普陀山, Zhejiang; this rubbing taken from a copy made for the Zuiganji of Matsushima in 1800.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kuixing tu bing zan
entries.description: A diagrom (formed from the character kui) of the constellation Kui, bowl of the Dipper of Ursa Major and tutelary god of literary art. Above the diagram is a panegyric; below is a brief account of the stone's provenance by Wang Yunquan 汪云佺. This rubbing taken from a Daoguang (1821-50) copy of the Wanli era (1573-1620) engraving originally housed in the Wenchang Academy 文昌書院 in Qiongzhou 瓊州, Guangdong.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lao Yuanen ti shi zhu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: bamboo, with accompanying verse, by Lao Yuanen.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bao Zheng xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Bao Zheng (999-1062); with a laudatory poem. Includes title inscription. This rubbing taken from a Qing engraving commissioned by Luo Yuan 羅瑗 and executed by Li Tai, based on a Zhengtong era (1436-49) portrait by Nan Yun 南雲 (who used a Yuan portrait for his model). in vermilion and black.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
titles_all: Kong miao sheng xian wei ci tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: Confucius and his seventy-two disciples. The title inscription, "De qi chou zai," is in the calligraphy of the Xianfeng emperor.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mochou xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of the girl Mochou of the Liang dynasty. This rubbing taken from a Qing engraving based on Zhu Borong's copy of the original, which is carved into the banks of Mochou hu in Jiangning, Jiangsu.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Bai Juyi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: portrait of Bai Juyi (772-886). The engraving is a Qing copy executed by Yang Jun of Houguan 侯官, after an original portrait in the Nanxun dian of the Palace Treasury.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taishan tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a sketch map of Taishan, based on the "Daoli ji" 道里記 of the Tai'an xianzhi 泰安縣志. Engraving by Jiuhe tang. This item appears to be a woodblock print rather than a rubbing.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xue Tao xiang bing ji
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Xue Tao (768-831), with accompanying inscription. The engraving was originally located by the side of a well at the site of the courtesan's former residence in Huayang, Sichuan.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Xihu shi er bei
entries.description: Pictorial reliefs carved by the Xileng Yinshe of Hangzhou: scenes from the life and deeds of Qian Liu 錢鏐 (852-932), king of Wu-Yue 吳越.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kuixing dian dou tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: the figure of Kuixing, northernmost star of the Dipper and tutelary god of literary art; composed of various admonitory and hortatory phrases, such as "Ke ji fu li" 克己復禮, "Zheng xin xiu shen" 正心修身, "Xiao di zhong xin" 孝弟忠信.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fumozhenren tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: the figure of Fumozhenren; composed of various admonitory and hortatory phrases, such as "Fu bu yin" 富不淫, "Pin bu yi" 貧不移, "Weiwu bu qu" 威武不屈.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tuo bo he shang tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: the figure of a bonze with almsbowl; composed of various admonitory and hortatory phrases, such as "Wuyu bu xing" 五欲不興, "Liuchen bu ran" 六塵不染.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Liu Hai xi jin chan tu
titles_all: Liu Hai xi jin chan tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: the figure of the immortal Liu Hai playing with a toad; composed of various admonitory and hortatory phrases, such as "Gui gen fu ming" 歸根復命, "Liao dao huan yuan" 了道還元.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zheng Xie lan zhu tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: orchids and bamboo by Zheng Xie. This rubbing taken from a later engraving of Zheng's painting.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhuxinjushi hua zhu lan tu
entries.description: Pictorial reliefs: orchids and bamboo, both by Su Shi 蘇軾 (1036-1101); in freehand copies made by Zhuxinjushi on a summer day in a gengshen 庚申 year.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanyin da shi xiang
entries.description: Image of the bodhisattva Guanyin, attributed to Wu Daozi (fl. 725); this rubbing taken from a Qing dynasty engraving based on the original painting by Wu Daozi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lü Dongbin xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Dingjue si 定覺寺 in Nangong, Hebei: full-length portrait of the immortal Lü Dongbin. The stone was put in place by Mao Xizeng 毛希曾 in 1925.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jianzhen xiang ji ming
entries.description: Pictorial relief: portrait of the Tang dynasty monk Jianzhen, seated; with an inscription by Guo Moruo. Commissioned by the Shaanxi Provincial Museum to commemorate the 1200th anniversary of the monk's death. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: portrait of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the Temple of Confucius at Qufu, Shandong: portrait of Confucius.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo xiang bing ti dui
entries.description: Image of Sakyamuni flanked by two bodhisattvas, and a couplet (each verse centered on a simulated shrine cut in relief): "Falun chang zhuan/Fori zeng hui" 法輪常轉, 佛日增輝.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guan Yu xiang bing zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief from Guandi miao in Jiexian, Shanxi: a portrait of Guan Yu (160-220), seated, at age fifty-three. Short panegyrics on the deified general appear in the upper right of the engraving, and a colophon of appreciation in the upper left.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: He he er xian xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of the two immortals He (Hanshan 寒山, fl. 627-49) and He (Shide 拾得, fl. 627-49).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Su Dongpo li ji tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Su Shi (1036-1101) in wooden clogs and a broad-brimmed bamboo hat.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yue Fei xiang bing zan
entries.description: Pictorial relief: portrait of Yue Fei (1103-41) seated; with a panegyric.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanyin xiang bing zan
entries.description: Image of Guanyin traveling across the sea on a lotus petal; with a panegyric.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guanyin xiang
entries.description: Image of Guanyin holding a willow twig, attended by Sudhana 善才童子 holding a water bottle. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo xiang bing dui lian
entries.description: A couplet (each verse center on a simulated shrine cut in relief): "Huangdi wansui/Guowang qianqiu". The couplet originally would have been positioned on either side of an image (or images) of the Buddha.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Fo xiang bing dui lian
entries.description: Image of Sakyamuni seated on the lotus throne, flanked by two bodhisattvas.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Kongzhi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Confucius, attributed to Wu Daozi (fl. 725); with a panegyric by Wu Ercheng. Includes title inscription. This rubbing taken from a later engraving based on the original painting by Wu Daozi.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Tianshi xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief: full-length portrait of Zhang Daoling 張道陵 (fl. 142), the Celestial Master, by Jingfanyanyidaoren; with a panegyric. This rubbing taken from an engraving based on Ling Zhu's copy of Jingfanyanyidaoren's original. In black and vermilion, with some green.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zhang Tianshi xiang
entries.description: Talisman for the protection of the home, bearing a portrait of Zhang Daoling 張道陵 (fl. 142), the Celestial Master. Possibly block-printed; in vermilion, green, and black.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shan zhong guan yi tu
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a chess party in a mountain setting, possibly a depiction of Wang Zhi watching the immortals play chess. Date and provenance unknown.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.title: Fei tian xiang
entries.description: Image of a deva, flying, with one arm upraised, purportedly dating to the Northern Wei. A modern forgery, taken from a wood engraving.

entries.description: Pressed tile with inscription (one character only, in mirror script); made, according to its inscription, during the Ganlu era (53-50 B.C.).

entries.title: Shou ruo da shan zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with the auspicious inscription "Shou ruo dashan." Possibly of Former Han date.

entries.title: Du si kong zhuan
entries.description: Tile identified by its inscription as made for the Capital Construction Office; purportedly dates to the second year of the regency of Wang Mang (45 B.C.-A.D. 23). Possibly a forgery.

entries.description: Tile identified by its inscription as made for the Capital Construction Office; possibly a forgery.

entries.description: Pressed tile with decorative patterns and inscription: "Niuma fan, nubi sheng." Unearthed at Yidu, Hubei. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi hou wang zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with decorative patterns and inscription: "Yi hou wang, fu zi sun." Unearthed at Yidu, Hubei. In vermilion.

entries.title: Mie huo dong jing zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on pressed tile: in the center, a man striding and a long-tailed bird; to the right and left, the words "Mie huo" and "Dongjing." Possibly of Han date; possibly a forgery.

entries.title: Han ting zhang can zhu zhuan
titles_all: Han ting zhang can zhu zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on pressed tile (fragmentary), originally from a pillar near Zhengzhou, Henan: lines of running dragons, the tails of some caught in the mouths of those behind.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han ting zhang can zhu zhuan
titles_all: Han ting zhang can zhu zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on pressed tile (fragmentary), originally from a pillar near Zhengzhou, Henan: the standing figure of a neighborhood head (i.e., custodian or guardian figure), with a border of running dragons on either side.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi shou yan nian zhuan

entries.description: Pictorial relief from a Han tomb: Queen Mother of the West, with attendants. Unearthed in Chengdu, Sichuan.

entries.title: Zhou Bo Huo zhuan mu zhi
titles_all: Zhou Bo Huo zhuan mu zhi
entries.description: Tomb inscription (fragmentary) of Bo Huo, wife of Zhou Chan 周闡, erudite at the National University; on tile. A forgery. (This inscription is not identical to Tsa 125 (5)-13.2.)

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Zang shi zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile made, according to its inscription, for the Zang clan of Wucheng, Wuxing. Date uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Tai zi zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with inscription: "Tai." Date uncertain; possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi hou wang zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with inscription: "Yi hou wang." Date uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yi shou kao wan nian zhuan
titles_all: Yi shou kao wan nian zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with decorative patterns and, on its side, an inscription: "Jin yi shoukao wannian." Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Jin zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile made, according to its inscription, during the Jin dynasty; possibly a forgery. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu jia le tu zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on pressed tile: rural scene, including a fisherman in a boat. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chen Qingzhi zao hua xiang zhuan
titles_all: Chen Qingzhi zao hua xiang zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on tile: a group of figures, possibly the ox-headed and horse-faced lictors of hell. Tile made by Chen Qingzhi during the Datong era (527-29).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Nong ju tu zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief: a depiction of rural life. Made for Zhou Wenyu (509-59).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Xian zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of a sacred image in stone by Guo Xian, on behalf of his parents, during the Baoding era (561-65); a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu zhu can zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a brick (fragmentary) bearing the impression of a coin of wuzhu denomination. Possibly of Six Dynasties date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taicang yao zhuan
entries.description: Brick inscribed with the names of officials involved in the receipt and transshipment of grain from the granaries at Aocang 敖倉 to the imperial granaries at Chang'an 長安.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Su Jian☐ zao xiang ji
entries.description: Votive inscription concerning dedication of an image of a bodhisattva in brick by the devotee Su Jian☐ during the Tianfu era (901-4).

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wan sui hua zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a tile (fragmentary) pressed with ornamentation and the characters "Wansui."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Nanhai Yuewang tai zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with decorative patterns; made, according to its inscription, for the tower of the king of Yue, Zhao Tuo 趙佗 (d. 137 B.C.). Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ren ma hua xiang zhuan
entries.description: Pictorial relief on pressed tile: two figures inside a house, a tree, and a man leading a horse. Date and provenance uncertain; possibly a forgery. In vermilion.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yun wen zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with could patterns. Date uncertain; possibly a forgery. In vermilion.

entries.description: Pressed tile with decorative patterns and inscription: "Ri li dou jin." Purported date uncertain; a forgery.

entries.title: Chi qiong zhuan
entries.description: Pressed tile with inscription in mirror script; date uncertain.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han bing tian xia can wa
titles_all: Han bing tian xia can wa
entries.description: Roof-tile (end-tile, fragmentary), identified by its inscription as made for the palace Han Gaozu 高祖 (r. 206-195 B.C.) built at Yueyang 櫟陽, Shanxi, after unification of the empire; a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chang le wa tong
entries.description: Colophon by the Qing dynasty collector Zhang Tingji inscribed on the back of one of the "Changle weiyang" roof-tiles; possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Chang le wei yang wa dang yan
titles_all: Chang le wei yang wa dang yan
entries.description: Rubbing of a "Changle weiyang" roof-tile that the collector Zhao Qianshi (Zhao Bingchong 趙秉沖) used as an inkstone, including the inscription he added to its outer edge.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Shanyang can zhuan fan ji di
titles_all: Shanyang can zhuan fan ji di
entries.description: Rubbings of a mold for bricks manufactured by or for Shanyang commandery. Possibly of Han date. Includes rubbings of recto and verso of mold.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yu pei di hua xiang
entries.description: Pictorial relief from the bottom of an earthenware fish basin: two figures, one light-skinned, one dark, standing face-to-face, grasping unidentified objects in their hands.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Er bai can ta
entries.description: Rubbing of a clay fragment inscribed with the words "Erbai"; of Han date.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Ri ba qian wan can ta
titles_all: Ri ba qian wan can ta
entries.description: Inscription from a bronze fragment or pottery sherd (type of object uncertain): "Ri baqianwan."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han hua zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a Han tile: seven registers of horses and chariots, door knockers, trees, and gatekeepers. Seven copies; modern forgeries, taken from a wood engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han hua zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a Han tile: taotie 饕餮 mask flanked by attendants and representations of bi 璧 discs, bordered by registers of dragons and fish. Eight copies; modern forgeries, taken from a wood engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han hua zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a Han tile: one register depicting riders and chariots; three registers of dragons. Seven copies; modern forgeries, taken from a wood engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Han hua zhuan
entries.description: Rubbing of a Han tile: horse and chariot with two occupants, led by a figure on foot. A modern forgery, taken from a wood engraving.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yuankang san nian zhuan fan
entries.description: Rubbing of a mold for bricks (fragmentary) pressed with a Yuankang era (291-99) date.

entries.title: Yuankang san nian zhuan fan
entries.description: Rubbing of a mold for bricks (fragmentary) pressed with a Yuankang era (291-99) date.

entries.title: Shanye ni fo xiang
entries.description: Rubbing of a shanye ni tile (verso) pressed with an inscription. Possibly manufactured at the Yan ta of Ci'en si, Chang'an 長安, during the Tang dynasty.

entries.title: Shanye ni fo xiang
entries.description: Rubbings of a shanye ni tile (fragmentary) pressed with an image of the Buddha flanked by bodhisattvas; on the verso, an inscription. From Chang'an 長安; possibly manufactured at Ci'en si during the Tang dynasty.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baozheng zhuan yan
entries.description: Inscriptions by two Qing dynasty antiquarians: the monk Liuzhoushanren (Dashou 達受) and the scholar Zhang Tingji, engraved on the back of a Baozheng era tile (dated 929) acquired by the monk at Lin'an 臨安 in 1831 and subsequently used by him as an inkstone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baozheng zhuan yan
entries.description: Inscription by a Qing dynasty antiquarian, the monk Liuzhoushanren (Dashou 達受), engraved on the front of a Baozheng era tile (dated 929) acquired by the monk at Lin'an 臨安 in 1831 and subsequently used by him as an inkstone.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Baozheng zhuan yan
entries.description: Front and sides of a Baozheng era tile (dated 929) acquired by the antiquarian Liuzhoushanren 六舟山人 (Dashou 達受, 1791-1858) at Lin'an 臨安 in 1831 and subsequently used by him as an inkstone. Face of tile pressed with geometric patterns; sides engraved with inscriptions ranging in date from the Baozheng (926-31) through the Daoguang (1821-50) eras.

entries.title: Zhuan yan
entries.description: Rubbing of a tile used as an inkstone by the collector Lu Xinyuan (1834-94); possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Mai di quan
entries.description: Deed of purchase of land, in mirror script; date and provenance uncertain. Possibly a forgery.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: He ye xi
entries.description: Rubbing of the rim of a wash basin (xi) in the form of a lotus leaf; date and provenance unknown.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: He ye xi di
entries.description: Inscription and decorative detail from the bottom of a wash basin (xi) in the form of a lotus leaf (Tsa 125 (5)-23); date and provenance unknown.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Yang zi ling
entries.description: Rubbing of a roof-tile (specifically, an elbow-shaped drain) inscribed with the word "Yang."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Wu Yue sheng shi can ta
titles_all: Wu Yue sheng shi can ta
entries.description: A fragment of a rubbing bearing only seventeen characters of the original inscription, beginning with the words "Wu-Yue shengshi."

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Guo Youdao bei
entries.description: Stele inscribed with the epitaph of Guo Youdao (zi Linzong 林宗, 128-69) of Taiyuan 太原; composed by Cai Yong and written in his calligraphy. This rubbing taken from a Qianlong era (1736-95) engraving based on a traced copy of the original.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Li Zhilian jie
entries.description: Tomb inscription of Yi Chi-ryŏm (1628-91) written by Hong Chik-p'il (1776-1852), with calligraphy by Pak Kye-su (1807-76) and Yu Hwa-gye. Includes title inscription.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Lijin Gongjia ba du kou luo cheng ji
titles_all: Lijin Gongjia ba du kou luo cheng ji
entries.description: Inscription commemorating completion of work on the Gongjia dike by Ji Eyuan, Ren Daoyuan 任道遠, and others, at Lijin, Shandong, in 1923. Includes rubbing of top of stele, with title inscription and ornament in relief.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Gao Yi shuang que ti ming
titles_all: Gao Yi shuang que ti ming
entries.description: Inscriptions from the memorial pillars (que), east and west, from the passage to the tomb of Gao Yi (d. 209), governor of Yizhou. In green. Both sets of inscriptions from the east pillar and one set of inscriptions from the west pillar only.

entries.repository: C.V. Starr East Asian Library, University of California, Berkeley
entries.title: Taiji shan ke shi (1) - zhi xiantan shi
titles_all: Taiji shan ke shi (1) - zhi xiantan shi
entries.description: Mountainside inscription by Zheng Daozhao from Taiji shan, Shandong: a poem, “Zhi xiantan.”
